data.headline: Thunt.net IV: the database
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: Thunt.net is now a database. What does that mean to you? Not a whole lot. What does this mean to me? I can continue thunt.net's path to innovation. Now all of the data is stored as text in a massive excel spreadsheet called a database. What about the old thunt.net? Well, I'm going to think about it a while. I'm going to compile it all to a dir so the search engine might pick it up. Then I'm thinking about re-entering it into the database in chunks like a "Thunt.net Flashbacks" section or something.... In other news, I'm wrote a description for a PHP Hat at CopyLeft. They had a link that said they couldn't think of anything, so I sent them this: Guaranteed to fit any size brainpan from guru to newbie, this PHP hat is a definite must for any PHP coder. And within minutes I recieved an email saying that they liked it and they put it up. Pretty cool huh? That's it for me, from New Thunt.net :>

data.headline: Paper Shredders are cool
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
I just shredded some paper in a paper shredder.  It may sound odd, but 
I've never shredded anything before, and it kicked ass.  A little loud, 
but still very fun.
  I think that people who like jigsaw puzzles should just buy a paper 
shredder and then shred something.  They could start with a one page 
document as their "level 1" puzzle.  Then work their way up to thesiss 
and small books.  Proffessional shredder puzzle masters could compete 
using encylopedias and Tolstoy books.  People who like crossword puzzles 
could also benefit from paper shredders by shredding themselves.
  Once, in High School Debate I made an impromptu speech to a crowd of 30-
40 of my fellow debators and one angry judge.  It was an abstract speech, 
I was handed a card with a word on it, and given three minutes of prep 
time and then I was to deliver a five minute speech.  My word was Irony.  
I gave a stunning oratory of seven minutes.  My main example was the great 
irony involved in shredding the instructions to a paper shredder.  I 
furthur delved into the depths of this example by explaining the poor case 
of someone who had boldly shredded the instructions to a paper shredder 
and then called their tech support line asking how to adjust the settings 
on his shredder.  The tech support line of course asked him if he had the 
instructions and he was of course left in the unsatisfactory condition of 
having to explain what he did.  How ironic.  Of course, the crowd loved 
the speech and so did the judge.  I won first place in the Novice 
Impromptu Speaking catagory.  It was my first time competing in the 
catagory.  I still have the trophy on my mantle at home.  The next time I 
competed it was on the Varisty level and I won third place.
data.summary: Did you see that part in Batman Returns when Penguin showed Max Schrex the notes that had been shredded that he re-assembled?  That was cool.

data.headline: Thunt announces Thunt.net 4.0
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
A wealth of well-integrated tools and technologies make the Thunt 
platform a compelling choice for the enterprise, as well as one of our 
Editors' Choices in this round up. Active Directory, Thunt's new directory 
service included in Thunt.net, makes management across the platform much 
more powerful. Mixing great performance, easy administration, and better 
reliability, the Thunt.net and Thunt Information Server (TIS) 5.0 
combination adds up to a very strong platform suitable for almost any size 
and type of site. 

    A strong argument can be made that Thunt's Web platform offers the 
most integrated solution among the products tested here. It combines 
powerful OS tools with Web administration and middle-tier infrastructure 
features. It isn't likely to beat out Unix's reputation for stability any 
time soon, but Thunt's new emphasis on reliability will be a welcome 
improvement for anyone who has run Thunt Software in the past. 

    A significant part of the Thunt.net family is its new Active Directory 
(AD), Thunt's offering for taming the corporate directory. AD rivals both 
NetWare's NDS and the vendor-neutral LDAP standard. Besides this 
enhancement, Thunt has improved its application server infrastructure with 
COM+ (the successor to the older DCOM standard) for running business and 
database components on the middle tier. COM+ now integrates transaction 
management, message queuing, and object pooling into the OS. Previously, 
these features were implemented as separate tools (including MTS and MSMQ) 
under Thunt NT. 

    The new TIS 5.0 supports several appealing features that can make the 
lives of Webmasters easier. First, the product comes with built-in 
performance options for small and large sites. It also supports security 
features such as Kerberos key distribution and certificate management. 
In testing, we were pleasantly surprised to find that the installation 
process for Thunt.net has been streamlined, with all server-side tools 
installed one time. The upgrade process is also remarkably smooth. 

    Scalability features are built into Thunt.net in several ways. The 
product supports up to eight processors right out of the box. There's also 
support for load balancing across multiple servers, and you can administer 
multiple nodes from one location. 

    When it comes to Web server administration, the Thunt platform offers  
several ways to work. The most sophisticated administration tool here is 
the Thunt Management Console (TMC). You can now administer virtually every 
aspect of the OS and Web components through a single, consistent 
interface. In previous versions, various TMC snap-ins were used separately 
for different aspects of the OS and tools.  You can also perform 
administrative tasks through a browser; we found this only slightly less 
efficient than the TMC approach. But Thunt's browser-based administration 
does compare favorably with the other products in this roundup. 

    Unlike the other platforms discussed in this roundup, which rely 
primarily on Java to develop server-side components, Thunt's solution 
makes use of Thunt's PHP Pages (PHP). Using PHP is the preferred way to 
generate dynamic Web content on this platform. Significantly, the PHP 
engine in TIS 5.0 has been retooled for better performance. In our testing 
of static content delivery, TIS 5.0 outperformed all its rivals. The 
impact of the retooling for end users is lessened though, because in real 
life you would add another server before reaching the server bandwidth 
limits measured by our tests. In our SSL API dynamic testing, Thunt showed 
clear performance advantages and was able to handle more than twice as 
many requests per second as its closest competitor, Sun with iPlanet. 
Though developers have a lot of options with Thunt tools, organizations 
that are planning to invest heavily in Java should think twice here. For 
one thing, Visual J++ is caught up in litigation between Thunt and Sun. 
Visual J++ is generally used to create components that run on Thunt's own 
Java Virtual Machine, or even Windows executables. It's possible to add 
JSP and servlet support to Thunt.net  just don't expect a heavy 
commitment to Java from Thunt. 

    All in all, the Thunt Web platform is a very tightly integrated 
solution that performs extremely well, has very strong supporting tools 
for developers, and would make a strong platform on which to build an e-
business.
data.summary: ZDnet reviews thunt.net

data.headline: Thunt Returns
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
That's right, you read the headline yourself, America's Favorite 
Iconoclast is back in Sacramento.  Or as B likes to say: Excrimento.  And 
boy is it hot.  Let's go live to Thunt's room for the weather report.

  <chopper noise>
Well, the weather outside seems to be anywhere from 90 to 105 degrees.  
We're not really sure because we haven't been outside yet today.  But let 
me assure you the airconditioner is running and things are nice and cool 
in here today.  
  <chopper noise fades out>

Wow, that was a facinating weather report.  Now lets go to Thunt's front 
room for an interview with his chief librarian.

  <dead silence>
  <whispering>
Thunt has arranged his books and his new org structure is a doozy.  He's 
got a section of authors who he owns more than one of their books, thus 
making them "good" authors.  He's got a literature section, followed by a 
thought section, arrayed by a history section, with a film section, comic 
section and a pulp section holding down the top row.  I must say this, 
only a madman or a certified genius could arrange books this way.

Very interesting, for a librarian. HA!
Now lets go to thunt's new interior decorator.

  The house is an enigma rapped up in a riddle of a condundrum.  Thunt's 
room is a mix of classic Dali, Van Gough and Thomas Cole Paintings, with a 
new age flair of Radiohead, REM and Rage against the machine posters.  A 
few Star Wars posters round out the eclectic mix.  With an REM calender 
from 1995 which now seems to be in perfect working order, very facinating 
piece.  Also the room features a official reverand certificate from the 
Universal Life Church and a life time membership award from the National 
Forensic League.  Thunt's room is truly a masterpiece in chaos. 
  On to the hallway.  Thunt's hallway currently features a High Fidelity 
poster, a few batman posters, and a ILM poster, with a 
KillMotherFuckingMicroSoft poster rounding out the mix.  It is a 
facinating hallway to walk down, to put it simply.  
  The front room is dominated by a United States Propaganda feeling that 
lets up only when one arrives at the playful entryway featuring a reasons 
to party poster and a we are here, all the good stuff is over there 
poster, which makes a dramatic statement.  

Thank you decorator.  And that about raps it up.  To summarize: Brush your 
teeth twice daily and try not to be a communist.  Thank you, good 
afternoon, good evening and good night.
data.summary: thunt house.  goooooood.

data.headline: I like my sense of humor and I think I should sell it on Ebay
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
I was out mini-golfing with some of my mates and their ladyfriends the 
other day when I realized what a great sense of humor I have.  I would say 
that it is very dry, sprinkled with sarcasm, in a sort of sardonic 
sensibility about it.  Wait, I don't even know what sardonic means,

sardonic - disdainfully or skeptically humorous : derisively mocking

Yeah, that's pretty much what I thought.  And I have pretty good timing, 
like Carson.  The memory and the speed to recall info and bring it out at 
the right time. 

Although sometimes my humor is too wacky for others or my quotes too 
obscure but I get by. 

Another thing I like about me is I just read a book cover to cover in one 
sitting.  About a boy by Nick Hornby.  It was really good.  It was by the 
same guy who wrote High Fidelity, a movie I've seen 4 times and already 
ordered the DVD. :>

Enough self appreciation.  It's time for Narcissus to go to sleep. 

Good night all.
data.summary: Funny. Sarodonic. l8r.

data.headline: Night into Day
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
You know, what they say is really true.  Being up late at night is just 
as good as being up during the day.  I have successfully migrated back to 
a non-nocturnal schedule.  I guess you would call that dayurnal or 
something.  But that's what I am now.   I regular nine to fiver (I'm awake 
during those hours, don't even talk about work, cause I don't).  I went to 
bed at 8:00 pm last night and awoke fully rested at 5:45 am.  Do the math, 
that's a lot of sleep.  And I didn't really notice the difference of being 
awake during the day.  Sure I can bother my friends at work now, rather 
than waking them up at night but that's not a great perk really.  So, as I 
sit in my room with a sheet over the window, my music up loud, with the 
air-conditioner blazing, I think, ahhhh daytime is great. :>

in the words of Kurt Cobain:

Sell the kids for food
Weather changes moods
Spring is here again
Reproductive glands

good night.
data.summary: Sleeping normally again. No more late nights.

data.headline: Caller ID for Girls
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: Ok, I had a thought. Everybody loves caller ID. It's great, a little box next to your phone and after the phone rings two times, you know who's calling you. It's great. Well, I had a better idea. Caller ID for girls. Here's how it works, you get a girl on the phone and you talk to her for about two minutes. It doesn't work right away, you've got to keep her on the line while it traces her, kinda like those cop movies where they do the trace and they have to keep the bad guy on the line for a while before they can find out where he is. So the box hooks up with a chip implanted in her brain, or perhaps it would just analyze her speech frequencies or something. So, then it reports to you what you need to know. I figure it would report something like this: Really Interested (your box is probably broken) Interested Possibly Interested Just a friend Interested in using you for free stuff Interested in treating you poorly then dating your friends Would rather eat shards of broken glass If you'd like to make a call please hang up and try again etc, etc..... Hopefully it will be out this spring retailing for 49.95, operators are standing by. It's 3:44 am! WOOOO.
data.summary: all the questions answered. no fuss, no muss.

data.headline: Mr. Andrew Horatio Duong, Superstar
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: And now, an AUTHENTIC fictional interview with Andrew Horatio Duong, creative director at the State hornet, a local rag newspaper. (Note that I do not bother to mention their website as it is not worth mentioning, seeing how it has been down all weekend because the pathetic rebus monkeys they have hired to work on it do not understand why you shouldnt experiment on a live server.) Andrew is the star of many direct to video pornography titles, such as I put feces in the paper and Horny cocks with Nuts! He is currently in negations with David Letterman to replace Paul Schaffer and his book Andrew Horatio Duong, the story of a man will be out this fall from Random House. Thunt.net: So Mr. Duong, I understand that you are responsible for all of the artwork and layout of the State Hornet newspaper. Mr. Duong: Yes, that is true. Thunt.net: Do you enjoy your work at the State Hornet? Mr. Duong: I have never found work more fulfilling than this. I mean, I treasure all of the days that I am forced to stay at the office until midnight on a Monday evening rather than spending it out with my friends. I have never worked anywhere that would crucify you at the drop of the hat and then sing your praises the next day. It is a radical kind of working environment. Thunt.net: I assume you are referring to the new, ehhm, leadership of BBB? Mr. Duong: Exactly. They say that a jack-of-all-trades can teach an old dog new tricks, but that is not entirely true in this case. I mean, sure at first it seemed as though what they say was absolutely true, but in the end it has all come to pass. Thunt.net: Thats just what Ive heard. Mr. Duong: Yup, the street never lies. Thunt.net: True that. So how is your interaction with the other sections of the paper been, since you took ultimate creative control of the paper? Mr. Duong: Well, I like to have a little motto, it helps me sleep at night. And that is: Im responsible for the graphics and the layout, nothing else. If I didnt have that Id probably locked up in the nuthouse or something. Thunt.net: I assume youre referring to the former editor of the Hornet. I understand that after the destruction of his car at the frail and demonic hands of mother nature, he was forced into early retirement and is now resting comfortably. Mr. Duong: I understand he is greatly enjoying the good life while chowing down on their delicious macaroni and cheese, now with Yellow Dye #5. aNDY!: I smell crackers! Thunt.net: Who are you? We are in the middle of an important interview here. aNDy!: I am ANdY! I rule! YEAH! Thunt.net: Are you feeling ok Mr. Duong? Mr. Duong: Uhhh, yes, I feel AnDy!: like a fucking madman and shit! Bring it on you wretched bitch. I heard you were an incompetent cocksucker, but to be fired from corporate America and to have to beg for your job back while retiring from the sorority with your big fucking shirt, man you should step back bitch! Mr. Duong: As I was saying, I feel fine. AndY!: Thats great it starts with an earthquake. I am the ripper man. I am the one who is called I am. Feces! FECES! NUTS! Fucking a man. Im going nuts being trapped in this square. Use soap! USE SOAP! Thunt.net: Mr. Duong if you can not contain yourself, I will be forced to end this interview. Mr. Duong: Its ok, Im a fucking nutbar. Bring on with the questions bitch! I never sleep. Im mad like that. Bring me in you fucking whore. Layout layout layout. I am the one for that shit. All I see is big P small p. Thats what I live. I live layout. Apartment, dont got it. House, no way. Car, sold it for another quark license. Its all Ive got left is the whining and the screaming and the incompetence. I just cant go on with this much longer. What is left for me in this foul world but the production of this bullshit douche rag of a paper. Oh the humanity how can there be any rest for the man who holds up the soul of the world. I travel the land with my soul on the souls of my feet. Without earth and life I only see death and hate. Without love I only feel for beveling and debossing. Kerning and kerning and kerning, towards victory. America demands a winner and I personally fucking hate losers. Thunt.net: Thank you Mr. Duong, for your precious time. Good night America, just sign and return those pledges to uphold the constitution and we will shortly return to your regularly scheduled broadcast.
data.summary: an interview with Andrew Horatio Duong

data.headline: Thomas Hunt: The Definative Interview
data.author: Thunt.net
data.story:
It is our very special privilege today to interview the founder of Thunt.net, and a frequent author on Thunt.net, Thomas Hunt. Thomas is a self styled iconoclast, star of stage and screen, he has most recently appeared alongside Michael Keaton in the Broadway version of "The Paper", he played the loveable tramp in Martin Scorsesses "Really, Really, Really" and is currently writing a short play about Watergate tentatively titled "Sucks Big, Dick". Thunt.net: Thank you for being with us today Mr. Hunt, we understand that your time is valuable, not to be wasted by pointless meetings on marketing, backwards ass discussions of that so called cyber- journalism and other such pointless drivel that so many people try to force upon those who have the courage to rise above the rest. Mr. Hunt: Its a pleasure to be here, to tell you the truth Im a big fan of the show. Thunt.net: We get that so often, but when coming from a true humanitarian like you, it really means a lot. Now lets get down to business. I understand that yesterday you wrote an article about Andrew Horatio Duong and I have heard that it is raising quite a stir. Mr. Hunt: Thats right. I just wanted to take a moment to tell the world what I really think about Mr. Duong. He is a true compassionate human being who is years ahead of his time. Hes bound to only be appreciated after he is long dead and I think that is really lame. I know a lot of people, and Mr. Duong, hes just, the kind of guy who without batting an eyelash would use the word "poon-tang" in mixed company. Men with that kind of balls only come along once, maybe twice a generation. America should learn to treasure this type of man, and Mr. Andrew Horatio Duong is certainly that type of man. Thunt.net: You seem to be a natural leader, a sort of God among men. In your time youve probably seen many inept leaders, what attributes should a leader avoid? Mr. Hunt: Thats a really good question. The most important thing that a leader should have is the respect of her associates. If they laugh at her, even once, it completely undermines her authority. I recall once that I was leading an expedition to be the first man to climb Mr. Everest and I read some disparaging comments about me on one of my partners websites. I had that man defenestrated. As a leader you have to teach your underlings right away that you wont put up with any of their shit. I mean, one day they are laughing at you, the next day Brutus and Cassius and eight other senators are stabbing you respectively with huge fucking knives. And I mean, that is no good, I dont care who you are. Thunt.net: Thats very true, do you have any other advice for leaders? Mr. Hunt: Sometimes it may seem as though your subordinates know more than you do. This is never true. Regardless of how much training they may have. Nothing can take the place of good leadership. I once had a man tell me how to make web pages. He thought he knew what he was doing because he had worked at Intel and he taught a course in HTML design at MIT. Well, I showed that indignant bastard a lesson. I ripped out his fucking heart with a spoon and showed to him before he died so he could see how black it really was. Then I realized he had made a good point and I fixed the problem myself. I was really glad he was able to contribute to MY project. Thunt.net: I have heard many people refer to the current leadership of the Hornet as a Nouveau Gerald Ford Administration. Some vicious right- wingers have even went as far as to refer to them as Nazis. Whats your opinion on these statements? Mr. Hunt: I have no comment at this time. Thunt.net: Ive heard many people label you as a naysayer, a broken down has-been, a man who is obsessed with the past and someone who just wont stop bitching about the shitty condition of the State Hornet, is this true? Mr. Hunt: Well, this is an issue I have wanted to address for quite some time. As you well know, Mr. Sommers and I have disagreed on many issues in the past. Namely the word "feces", the letter "A" and whether or not you should look both ways when crossing the street. But with him out of the way, I think there are some great people working at that shitbag rag paper:
  • Endeen: A great guy. A true managing editor in the chief sense of the word. Think of the Compromise of 1877, I weep for the future.

  • Wagar: The man really knows his news. If something happens on campus, hes the one who will be there. And you know he will put his fucking ass on the line to get that integral story. Sometimes they lock him out of the office and try to stop him from doing work, but nothing can hold back this behemoth of modern journalism. Ive read a lot of stories about Lunch Ladies, but his was the best.

  • Duong: Too much has been said, but the man is not just a hero, but my hero. I thank the almighty God everyday of my life when I think of that beautiful snowflake that he placed on this Earth.

  • Tony: Few people can be referred to by just their first name, Madonna, Marilyn, Marvin the Martian, but Tony is that kind of man. I never really liked Features and I never really will, but its good to see a true renaissance man in charge of the section.

  • Andrea: In 1928 she joined a religious order and took the name Teresa. The order immediately sent her to India. A few years later, she began teaching in Calcutta, and in 1948 the Catholic Church granted her permission to leave her convent and work among the city's poor people. She became an Indian citizen that same year. In 1950, she founded a religious order in Calcutta called the Missionaries of Charity. The order provides food for the needy and operates hospitals, schools, orphanages, youth centers, and shelters for lepers and the dying poor. It now has branches in 50 Indian cities and 30 other countries.

  • Katherine: Even though she has Justin White as a friend, I wont hold that against her.

  • Russ: Russ grew up in the rough-and-tumble environment surrounding his fathers Lombard and Camden Street saloons. By age seven he had become incorrigible and beyond the influence of parental control. Frustrated, the Edmondsons sent their son to St. Marys Industrial School, an institution for orphans and delinquent boys strictly run by the Catholic order of Xaverian Brothers, who were noted for their no-nonsense disciplinary tactics. It was there that the youngster learned the intricacies of baseball, and of life, mostly under the tutelage of Brothers Gilbert and Matthias.
Thunt.net: What has been the reaction of the Editor in Chief of the State Hornet, a woman who some call Historys Greatest Monster? Mr. Hunt: Shes been really great about it. She called me up on the phone they other day and was so incredibly cordial I thought that she had been trained by Emily Post, herself. She commented on the ironic qualities of the article as well as its narcissistic qualities. To her it was more of a Nietzchistic expression of post-Kierergardian myth with a smattering of Freud and Marx rather than an outpouring of Jeffersonian democratism. She was really great about the whole thing. She even offered to take me out to dinner and seemed to be interested in me sexually. Thunt.net: So the threats we have heard of Lawyers and another bad movie like "A Civil Action" are completely false? Mr. Hunt: Yes, completely false. Nothing could be further from the truth. I mean I have no interest in suing the Hornet. To tell you the truth, they have nothing that I want. I mean Ive always wanted to throw an Imac off a ten story building but thats one of those things that is bound to happen to any young man in todays day and age. Its an inevitability really, I dont really concern myself with it much. Thunt.net: Did you ever see Nadine underage, drunk off her ass, at the Limelight? Mr. Hunt: I have no recollection of that fact, Senator. Thunt.net: Have you ever read the book Harry Potter and the Gauntlet of Fire? Mr. Hunt: No, I did not have sexual relations with that woman. Thunt.net: Im talking about the book, with Harry Potter, with the magic and dragons, you know? Mr. Hunt: No, I did not have sexual relations with that woman. Please stop asking me this question. Thunt.net: What woman? Have you gone insane, I am not asking a question about a woman, but merely a whimsical childrens book. Mr. Hunt: No, I did not have sexual relations with that woman. This interview is over. I cant stand you. You disgust me. You dirty muck-raking, yellow-journalist. Youre an unfit mother. They should give out more free condoms to stop people like you from procreating in drunken sorority sponsored orgies. Step into my office, because youre fucking fired you backstabbing whore! You wouldnt know a competent person if they fell on you like that fucking house in that movie. You couldnt find your ass with two hands and a map. You smell like an elephants behind and your mother reeks of elderberries. You had me at Hello. no one can touch you in your bathing suit area. This article is dedicated to the memory of Matt Brown. How come when one man kills another man in the heat of battle its called heroism, but if he kills him for eating the last otter pop, its called murder?
data.summary: really long. About the hornet. They're funny.

data.headline: A very special audio interview & Inside the Newsroom

data.headline: Ask Thunt
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: Well, with my newfound fame and fortune Ive discovered that people have begun to treat me as some sort of Christ-figure who they expect to solve all of their problems for them. I hate to deprive anyone of my glorious advice, so without any further ado I give you Ask Thunt or as I like to call it, Dear Abby with Attitude. Dear Thunt, Someone is making fun of me at work. It really hurts my feelings because ever since I was a little girl Daddy always bought me everything that I wanted. Even though Im the boss at my work I feel like my authority is being threatened by this Shakespearian miscreant. I dont know who to trust anymore. It seems as if he has struck at the very core of my inner circle and they are feeding him information to better craft his masterful jokes. Just the other day I was contemplating breast enhancement surgery with my closest friends and the very next day everyone in the break room was gawking at a poster on the wall. It was a picture of me, but someone had removed my body and replaced it with the body of a nude female model. I believe it was Pamela Anderson Lee post-Baywatch and before she had her reduction surgery. I turned bright red and quickly ripped the poster off of the wall and ran to my office with tears in my eyes. Of course later I couldnt stop staring at the beautiful breasts in the picture and I had a really good time if you know what I mean. Even so, I fear that Im not respected by my co-workers. We had a routine staff meeting yesterday and everyone was laughing at me. I felt like my favorite actor Ernest in the movie Ernest Scared Stupid. Usually Im happy when I think about my favorite actor, a man who was robbed of Academy gold so many times by that dirt bag Robert De Niro. Anyway, Im a completely laughing-stock. I cant even sleep around without being caught anymore. I need help. So far Ive been trying to forget my troubles by borrowing money from the petty cash at work to buy dime bags of cocaine, but I know that drug addiction is not the answer. I just want to get back to the way things were in college. When I was the little princess of the sorority and I spent every evening drinking cheap alcohol until I passed out and was raped by the football team. Those were the days. How can I get back to that? Sincerely, Crying in Colorado Well, Crying. I think that its time you showed that asshole whos boss. I mean, from what you tell me you are the boss at your work and you shouldnt have to take any shit at all. I mean not from anybody. Why do they call Superman Superman? Its not just because hes banging Lois Lane. Its because he doesnt take any shit. When Lex Luthor tried to kill him with Kryptonite that one time, he didnt let that bastard get away with it. He locked him in an easily escapable jail for good. And that is what I suggest with this troublemaker at your work. I suggest you write him a letter. A strong, harsh, mean-spirited letter and let him know what your true feelings are. Tell that bastard off. Maybe you are a bad boss. Maybe you do treat your underlings like scum sucking bottom feeders. Maybe you did start the Chicago Fire. Perhaps it was your fault that communism conquered China and Mao Tse-tsung killed off oh so many of his people. But these are still not reasons for you to take anyones shit. Tell that bastard off, because if you allow your fellow employees to laugh at you, you are no longer the boss, you are just a little bitch. Dear Thunt, I dont know how to do my job. Im supposed to be running this big website for this newspaper and I just dont know how. I try my best but nothing seems to work right. And yesterday, my boss told me that I am incompetent screw-head and then she cussed me out in front of the rest of the newspaper staff. Now Im completely shunned by all my fellow co- workers because they think Im a idiot. What should I do? From, Loser in Louisville Well, I dont know how to help incompetent people. If you dont know how to do your job perhaps you should perform some tests on your new zero gravity machine without a net. Perhaps you could get a job testing ejector seats on helicopters. I know that the Army is always looking for a few good corpses, so thats right up your alley. If your boss told you that you were an incompetent screw-up, shes probably right. You should just take it and shut the fuck up like the little cocksucker you are. Dear Thunt, I need help. Im not a computer wiz. To tell you the truth I dont know the difference between my toaster and my vibrator, I mean Microsoft and Macintosh. But my friend gave me a job and it involves computer skills. I try my best to convince my co-workers that I know what Im doing, but Im a sorority girl. The only thing Ive learned in college is that Victorias Secret has the best push-up bras and drinking vodka directly from the bottle is the best way to spend a Wednesday night. Please help me! Love, Brunette in Boston Well, Ive had to do a lot of soul searching to solve your problem Brunette in Boston. And what Ive come up while peering deep into my crystal ball is that you should wear less clothing at work. Try to show these computer dorks that you work with that you are more than just a pretty face. Show them that youve got tits and an ass too. Theres only one thing that people respect in the computer industry and that is not intelligence. Its the willingness to suck up to anyone that can get you a better job. And you know what I mean when I say suck up. So hop to it! This has been Ask Thunt. If you have any problems with your love life, your job or enormous setbacks on your snowbound campaign to conquer Russia, please email Thunt at thunt@thunt.net. Because its not like he cares but at least youll get some of the attention that you so desperately need, you silly little bitch.
data.summary: 3 losers state their complaints and Thunt gives them advice, Thunt-style.

data.story:
illustrated by Thomas Hunt and his trusty laser mouse
written by Matt Brown & Thomas Hunt

And in case you were curious:
    
Main Entry:	sat·ire
Pronunciation:	'sa-"tIr
Function:	noun
Etymology:	Middle French or Latin; Middle French, from Latin 
satura, satira, perhaps from (lanx) satura dish of 
mixed ingredients, from feminine of satur well-fed; akin to Latin 
satis enough
Date:	1501
1 : a literary work holding up human vices and follies to 
ridicule or scorn
    2 : trenchant wit, irony, or sarcasm 
used to expose and discredit vice or folly

Hustler Magazine, Inc. v. Falwell, 485 U.S. 46, 108 S.Ct. 876, 99 
L.Ed.2d. 41 (1988) 

Hustler Magazine published a parody of a liquor advertisement in which 
Rev. Jerry Falwell described his first time as a drunken encounter with 
his mother in an outhouse. A unanimous Supreme Court held that a 
public figure had to show New York Times actual malice in order to recover 
for intentional infliction of emotional distress as a result of a parody 
in a magazine. The Court held that political cartoons and satire such as 
this parody have played a prominent role in public and political debate. 
And although the outrageous caricature in this case is at best a distant 
cousin of political cartoons, the Court could see no standard to 
distinguish among types of parodies that would not harm public discourse, 
which would be poorer without such satire.

data.headline: The State of the State University
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
It is crowed out there.  Way too crowded.  I dont know whether 
its just this year or if its been every year and I just havent noticed 
it, but Sac State is fucking crowded.  I was walking around the computer 
labs after teaching my class today and I was not practically but actually 
tripping over people.  It was like a third world country or being in L.A.  
And whats worse is all of these people are talking.  The endless chatter 
of students filled the usually stoic computer lab.  And its a wonder that 
people are complaining about lab service as I watched my fellow lab 
assistants scramble to help the five students pounding on the desk for 
their print outs as the dot matrix printer clicked away in the background 
spewing out never ending sheets of C code and the occasional web page.  
How can the lab assistants hope to keep up with this kind of demand?  How 
can our school continue to accommodate far more students than the 
designers of this school intended?  The complaints about parking are never 
ending and never going to end at this rate.  I wouldnt be surprised if I 
picked up a copy of the school newspaper ten years from now and the 
headline still read: Huge Parking Problem on Campus or perhaps something 
witty like Campus Clogged with Congestive Traffic.  Ok, so its not that 
witty but what school newspaper ever is.  The frantic sprint to increase 
enrollment and gain the oh so important dollars placed upon each head by 
the state continues each year like a never-ending fraternity rush week.  
Somehow, they continue to hide the problems of congestion, parking, 
traffic and lack of teachers every year as they continue their quest for 
increased enrollment.
	Did I mention that Im teaching a class?  Thats right, both I and 
my roommate Matt Brown are lab instructors for Computer Science 8  
Exploring the Internet for the goodly Professor Floyd LeCureux.  Its just 
another plank in the administrations plan to expand the number of students 
taught without the costly hiring of new faculty (applicants in the 
computer science field being virtually non-existent due to the 
preternaturally good job market).  Therefore, Dr. LeCureux teaches the 
class for all of the students in lectures televised on Cable TV, while 
Matt and I answer their questions in class, grade their assignments and 
help Dr. LeCureux create the assignments for them to do.  Sure, its a 
great way of expanding one professor and allowing him to teach a great 
deal more students than the traditional method, but it is another one of 
the brilliant schemes of the administration to expand enrollment.  
Something needs to be done about this hegemonic educational policy.  
Because its only a matter of time before Sac State becomes so crowded 
that you will have to buy a seat license to have an actual seat in your 
philosophy class with a real teacher and those who didnt buy the licenses 
will have to watch from another lecture hall on campus.  Of course, it 
will be presented in Dolby digital surround sound, with a 16x9 letter-
boxed picture to ensure a true THX quality-learning environment.
data.summary:
Sac State is over crowded.
A "thought" piece.

data.headline: Sir Ryan Endeen, III, my hero
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: I would like to take a postition today. I would like to glorify and magnify the stellar accomplishments of the worlds protector Ryan Endeen in his flabbergasting article Fee increase may curb parking nightmares. This article represents the absolute pinnacle of investigative reporting. Never before have I seen such punctuation like the period he used in line 5. You just do not see grammar like that in modern news reporting. Seeing periods like this brings me back to the grand old days of Edward R. Morrow and William Randolph Hearst and I believe Mr. Endeen would agree with me wholeheartedly. At the end of his second paragraph when he says that parking is expected to worsen, I was struck by the inescapable drama of his prose. It is practically Shakespearian in its transference of language down to the baser elements of society yet it still propels itself upwards on the wings of a dove. And towards the end of the article when he writes 250 spaces will be opened up in Lot 4, after the completion of the removal and demolition of the temporary. I was amazed at his ability to take such a harsh stance against the transitory nature of this life and to stand so high in such a sordid medium and cry out against the demons of this world. Few philosophers in our time have the gall to make such a bold statement. I would like to close with the amazing quote that Mr. Endeen managed to achieve from Mr. Jimmy Henderson (he prefers Jimmy, like most four year olds) who said and I quote: were basically kind of screwed.
data.summary: there's something about Ryan....

data.headline: THUNT ATTACKED!
data.author: Henry Hackett
data.story:
Scholar, humanitarian and award-winning satirist Thunt was brutally beaten
today, Monday October 2nd, in the University Union.  According to 
eyewitness reports Thunt was quietly reading, when he was attacked by 
unidentified person/persons.  Thunt was then airlifted to an unidentified 
hospital (withheld by police for Thunt's safety)  Sources in the police 
department have informed us that he is in serious condition, and he isnt 
expected to survive through the night.  Every effort is being taken to 
give Thunt the best medical treatment, experts from throughout the world 
are being flown in to treat Thunt.

The police department finds this act contemptible and has assigned three
detectives to the case.  In a press conference held earlier today Lead
Detective William Somerset declared that Law enforcement agencies all over
California have been mobilized and they feel the apprehension of Thunt's
attacker is now a top priority.  Somerset believes the primary motive may 
be Thunt's willingness to turn states evidence in an academic corruption 
case, involving a moderately respected newspaper, and some University 
professors.

Several of the eyewitnesses were former Thunt collegues at the State Hornet
newspaper, which Thunt lovingly called a shitbag rag.  Recently knighted
and shitfaced Sir Ryan Endeen, III declared that Thunt was a little bitch,
he was glad he was dead, and it was about time she killed him.  When Sir
Endeen found out thunt was clinging to life, he changed his postition and
now he feels that the world has lost a great individual, and he is now
hoping Thunt pulls through.  Sir Endeen then proceded to vomit profusely
into the library quad fountain, then started singing a creepy rendition of
It's Raining Men.

Matt Wagar and Antonio Harvey were also eyewitnesses to the event.  Fearful
for their lives, Wagar and Harvey went into hiding.  Both were eventually
found in Wagars VW eating packets of mustard.  They deny being witnesses 
to those events, and refused to help Detective Somersets special unit.

A heavily medicated David Sommers, formet Editor in Chief of the State
Hornet, remembers Thunt as a bitchin dude and was really bummed when he
learned of Thunt's condition.

Police are asking for anyone with knowledge of these events to come 
forward, and Thunt's family feels that Thunt would have wanted cold, 
brutal revenge against his attacker and has posted a $50,000 reward, dead 
or alive.

Thunt.net will keep you updated on all events as they happen.
data.summary:
Thunt in severe medical condition.
His attacker goes unpunished!

data.headline: Activists
data.author: Shroom
data.story: One day I decided to visit my friend Cracker at UCSC. (note: Graham will be known as 'Cracker' to protect the innocent). Being the somewhat environmentalist type of person that I am, I biked to campus from my place of residence within the city limits of Santa Cruz. Also, I don't own a car. You see, biking all the way up to campus is no big deal to me. It's nice. Try biking home day-in-day-out from UC Berkeley to your place in the Berkeley hills for a few years. Then you'll understand where I'm coming from. So where was I? Oh yes, sweating like a pig on a spit and dripping like I was just spat on by gaggle of pigs, I find myself wandering in the vicinity of Porter College. Porter College is one of 9 (soon to be 10) colleges on the UCSC campus. "What?" you say? How can there be colleges within colleges? That's what I'm still trying to figure out. So there I am attempting to figure out which dorm building Cracker lives in. There are no signs. Just a few buildings. I'm at a loss. What do I do besides knock on every door in each building. According to Murphy, the last door I knock on will be his. "There's got to be a better way", I think to myself. Just then I notice flesh. Sun-bathing flesh. What strikes me particularly off guard is this flesh is sparsely covered by a bra. Woo hoo! What better person to ask directions than a scantily clad sunbathing porter-type babe. I bike up to her position on the well kept grassy knoll and ask her if she happens to know where building 653Alpha42- whisky-delta-foxtrot-3 is. This really wasn't the number, see, since the actual building number has since vacated my memory. Her initial reaction to my query was the type you'd expect if you decided to instead of wearing clothes to smear yourself in bullshit. Not as bad, mind you, if you also did the same with toothpaste. That would have been a totally different reaction. I thought perhaps there was a standard rule that would protect sunbathers from harassment. I could understand this. If I was barely clothed and constantly had people asking me for directions, I'd become peeved myself. Of course, I would probably go somewhere else like near a help desk or one of those nation park maps that are usually made out of redwood (hmmm?). She reluctantly gave me the info and I thanked her. I made it to the proper building and noticed the door was locked. But someone was coming out. Nice. As the woman opened the door she asked if she could help me. Hmm, I didn't think the school budget could afford door-hops? I said I'm just on my way to visit a friend. She must have thought she was still working for the US National Missile Defense Program for she would not allow me to enter. Apparently only Porter students can enter. She told me I would have to go through the 'other' door to phone my friend down. So far my experience at Port, a college I rarely visit save for the random visits to the dining hall, has been most unwelcoming. I make my way to the other door, walk through, down the hall, past the door I was allowed to not enter through, up the stairs and up to my friends dorm room. That was easy. It's a good thing I wasn't a spy or serial hide-hard-drives-behind- copy-machines type shady character. I knock and enter. Before we can being exchanging pleasantries, the scantily clad sun bather appears and knocks on Crackers door. She has since figured out how to mount her bra properly as well as ordained herself with a t-shirt. I say to her, "oh hi, looks like I found it :}" (yes, I smiled). She asks promptly why I rode my bike on the grass. At this point, I assume she is an RA (resident assistant, basically the people who keep the fort...err residence halls down). I also assume there is another known local law that prevents people from approaching people sunbathing on grassy knolls on a bike. I don't remember what I said at that point. Something along the lines of, "I don't know?". What could you say at this point? Anything would seem like a smart alec remark and I was attempting to prevent any more confrontation with people. Actually, I would have been less inclined if no one was on the grassy knoll. Instead there were a few people, some with lawn chairs. She continued to cross-examine me. "I just want to know what's going on in peoples head that feel they can ride their bikes all over the grass. I'm taking an environmental studies class and we are trying to understand why people do these types of things." Wow! This girl is really taking her job seriously. She also seems to be somewhat overly empowered when it comes to policing our 'environment'. What without her there might not be a single well watered and fertilized mowed lawn in site! It would truly be an ugly campus indeed. "Promise me you will never do that again." "Sure." And off she goes. Apparently her environmentalist senses began tingling and so had to go and protect another patch of lawn somewhere else. I asked Cracker if she was his RA. This was the first time he saw her. He was also as confused as I was. To this day, I thank her for making me realize just what kind of person I was before our little talk. My previous destructive behavior was unthinkable and unforgivable. Now when I walk around campus and see patches of forest clear cut in favor of a new parking lot or buildings I think back to that nice sun bather. I know she'll be patrolling day and nite the small patches of grass, plants, and buildings that will replace those lame redwoods, forest ferns, deer, squirrels, birds, and insects that once plagued the surrounding UCSC campus. Soon, UCSC will be beautiful. Shroom currently resides in Berkeley and has organized several violent protests in favor of nude sunbathing.
data.summary: lame environmentalists are lame.

data.headline: Elvis sighted at Blockbuster!

evening.  Ahhh, Blockbuster.  It's like a magical candyland where dreams 
come true.  There are so many good movies, and none of them are offensive 
with violence and nudity.  A perfect place for family entertainment.  I 
started off my search for a video by browsing the classics section.  We 
all know that it's a good idea to start out in the classics section,

data.headline: Movies to watch: three super sub flicks
data.author: Ben Black
data.story: Like most thunt.net readers, you are probably always on the lookout for that next great film to find its way to DVD. Here are three great movies that just happen to all take place on a submarine, and are all available on DVD: Run Silent, Run Deep This one first rolled in theaters in 1958. It is NOT one of the formula war pictures that Hollywood couldn't stop making after WWII. It's a battle of wills between Cary Grant and Burt Lancaster. About 95 minutes long, a good warm-up for... The Hunt for Red October Tom Clancy has gotten a bit long-winded with his novels, lately. The last few have each broken the 1000 page mark with ease. However, back when he was still up-and-coming, he wrote an excellent story about a Soviet submarine that everyone is anxious to find. The book is excellent, and deserves your time. Edited liberally to fit into a 2 hour and 15 minute movie, the story still stands strong. Look for strong performances from the entire cast. It's worth watching again. I thought that The Hunt for Red October was the best submarine movie made. Until I watched... Das Boot (Director's Cut) This movie is incredible. First released in 1981 at 145 minutes. Director Wolfgang Peterson apparently felt that wasn't adequate to tell the story he wanted to tell with this film. For the Director's Cut edition on DVD, he restored almost a full hour of editing. Yes, movie buffs, this one now weighs in at about 200 minutes. However, don't be fooled. Wolfgang knows his stuff, and the movie is excellent. A very captivating tale of young german sailors full of themselves and the glory that they will find at sea. Reality hits, naturally. I can't say enough about this film. About the disks The DVDs, apart from the movies they contain, are nothing exciting. Each movie is presented in widescreen format. Run Silent and Red October simply have the movie, and perhaps trailers. Das Boot has a pitifly short featurette on making the film. It also has a commentary track, but I didn't get the time to listen to it. Das Boot offers the choice between english dubbing or subtitles. For a chuckle, try the dubbing and compare it to the subtitle for the same scene. The "tour of the sub" is a good chapter for this. The dubbing is subtly different than the subtitles, which feel more genuine in their language and choice of words. So, there you have it, readers. Three great films to put on your list the next time you rent DVDs. If your local selection sucks, as does mine, you might try NetFlix (www.netflix.com) for your rentals. It's an odd program, DVDs by mail, but the selection is excellent. Their catalog description of films is weak, so it's best if you know what you want going in. About the author: Full time kernel hacker, part time movie buff. Former shout-out man for thunt.net. At one point an excellent writer, laziness has taken its toll, and the result is in front of you. Editor's note: Thunt didn't even have to write a snappy about the author, the author wrote it himself, now that's snappy!
data.summary: Looking for a cool trio of movies on DVD?  Our man in Colorado is doing the dirty work of watching films and passing tips on to thunt.net readers.

data.headline: How to set up a Fascist Regime in your workplace
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: Underpay your subordinates Nothing makes people happier than knowing they are paid less than you. Oh, and its also a really good idea to tell your brainless subordinates that you will give them raises if they perform well. While in point one I suggested you, tell your employees that you will give them raises. That was not entirely true. I suggest you threaten your employees with massive pay cuts at the drop of the hat. Lets say that one- day, one of your employees fails to open the door for you. Fine them 50 dollars. Do not fuck around. You have to show those little scumbags whos the boss. And Im not talking about the 80s TV show with Alyssa Milano and TonyFuckingDanza. Im talking about fascism, like Hitler and Stalin. They didnt take any crap and neither should you. And lets say one of your subordinates drinks the last cup of coffee, or reads a web page you told them not to read, threaten to fire their ass. Due to Stalins brilliant framing, he ended up in Mexico City with an ice pick sticking out of the side of his head. The people know they have to vote Yes: I approve of the fascist regime or else you can have your secret police hunt them down like the donut pilfering grab-asses they are. Finally comes the best way to rig an election, have a man inside. When the parliamentary committee is deciding who should be Prime Minister, your man is inside the debates. He steers the discussion away from other more qualified candidates and creates controversy. If this is done right, the committee will ignore the qualified candidates and listen to the sage-like advice of your man. This is one way, that with just one man on the inside, you can rig an election and gain power over a whole country. These are just some of the many ways to set up a facist regime in your workplace. data.headline: Movie Review: Son-in-law (1993)
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: Pauly Shore stars in this most striking social commentary ever to come out of the early nineties. Shore plays a mentally retarded man who escapes from a mental asylum in California by pretending to be engaged to a sweet Midwestern country girl. Upon his arrival to the Midwest, it quickly becomes clear that Shore is not the most retarded character in this film. The Midwest people show themselves to be at least twice as retarded. From the sweet midwestern girl who believes her fake engagement to Shore is a real engagement, to the entire dysfunctional family. The insanity of Shore's character spreads like a vicious virus through this midwestern family. The matron character soon learns that if she puts on a little makeup she can easily entice her husband. In this scene, the husband, preparing to make his conquest, performs the mating ritual of pushing the two beds together, followed by the stylish rooster imagery that of course represents cock. Shore's acting can only be characterized by the word amazing. The dramatic range portrayed while playing this mentally retarded character is truly unbelievable. The most incredible aspect of his performance is his creation of new words such as "grindage", "bone her" and "cones". The performance is far better than Geoffrey Rush's performance in Shine or George W. Bush's performance in the recent Presidential election. Other film critics who fail to see the depth of Shores portrayal may overlook this film, but it is a truly excellent film representing the true heights of this powerful force in acting. I cannot recommend it enough. Thomas Hunt is a world renown film critic and recently watched Son-in- law for free on digital cable

data.headline: Call Out Your Professor
data.author: Matt Brown
data.story: I have a radical new idea that could forever alter our university system. In our day and age, even getting enrolled in a class could mean dozens of signatures and forms. Even the elective courses in our degree programs have to be chosen with the approval of a academic advisor. All of this is nothing compared to the hell of a grade appeal. When a professor, or who ever the department could con into teaching the course, sits down to assign grades, so many other factors can enter the decision, and many students feel like they were cheated. The current grade appeal process is simply unacaptable, because the faculty always sticks together, and your average student doesn't stand a chance. That is why a new grade resoultion process is needed, and I think our best bet is to return to a classical dispute resolution system  the duel. If a student has a problem with the grade they received, they should be able to walk right up to that person and call them out. The teacher would then be given the right to choose between pistols or swords at dawn in front of the library. This could also work with other student vs. administration battles. I myself had to appeal two parking tickets last week, all because the masterbating reject with the ticket machine wasnt able to locate the daily permit, that happened to be right on my motherfucking dashboard. I felt unsatisified with the fill out this form, and well let you know appeal. I believe that I should be able to face my accuser, and challenge him/her to open combat. There are some people out there who feel violence doesnt solve problems, and they can go fuck themselves. When you study history, all you learn is how people use violence to solve their problems, its the most effective way to resolve any disagreement. In fact when solutions have been non- violent, it has led to nothing but disaster. Society has degraded to the point where mass beaurcracies are cruching the individual. Humanity is still all about self-interest, so we cannot attempt to solve problems with logic. The strong still get their way, but the way we determine strength has changed, and the normal person doesnt have a chance. I know my solution may be a bit drastic and uncomfortable at first, but the people would be able to govern themselves again. I welcome any of your thoughts on Thunt's message board. p.s. If you are frightened and think this is serious, then obviously thunt.net has taught you nothing, and I have a really good deal on some elevator passes. p.p.s. Kudos to the exclamation point and other friends on their recent accolades, you guys kick ass. Editor's Note: Editing is hard. Sometimes you want to chop whole parts out. Other times you just leave whole parts in because, why the hell not. You guess which policy I'm following. It's a fun game.

data.headline: Subterranean Homesick Country
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Johnny's in the basement Mixing up the medicine I'm on the pavement Thinking about the government - Bob Dylan, Subterranean Homesick Blues/ Mp3

As I sit here listening to Bob Dylan I realize how awful things have 
become in America.  The Supreme Court recently decided the fate of the 
Presidential Election.  They decided not to count the votes and let the 
son of the former President become King. This is a sad time for America.  
For over 200 year now, we have been a democracy.  Now we become a 
monarchy.  It's amazing to think how much the Supreme Court failed the 
people today.  They voted strictly on party lines and against the will of 
the populace.  The people are beginning to turn their back on the 
electoral system.  There is no more reason to vote.  It was difficult 
before to think that your one vote mattered, but that question has been 
clearly answered today.

Keep a clean nose
Watch the plain clothes
You don't need a weather man
To know which way the wind blows

Jefferson.  Lincoln.  Washington.  Three men who won elections and lost 
all that they stood for today.  

I believe this election will be a revelation to the interest groups who 
contributed so much money to these two campaigns.  Now you don't even have 
to buy the candidates, you should just skip directly to buying the 
election.  Perhaps America should choose her President based on which 
interest group could raise the most money would choose the new President. 
And perhaps after the election all of the money could be given to NASA, an 
agency with a great history that I have been enjoying on the "From the 
Earth to the Moon" series and who I believe could use this interest group 
money for something more important than more commercials for Bush or 
Gore.  Instead of TV Time, lets buy some moon time.  It's been a while 
since we've been up there.  And I think it's time to go back.  And we 
should build a colony up there.  That way, at least there would be a few 
Americans to carry on after King W. enters the United Kingdom of America 
into a fatal nuclear war with the People's Republic of China (clearly just 
as democratic as America, and they probably have much better food).

Girl by the whirlpool
Lookin' for a new fool
Don't follow leaders
Watch the parkin' meters

We've found the new fool.  Every Republican who celebrates W.'s great 
victory in the Presidential election.  Every member of the party that 
usurps the sacred name "Republic" and dares to say that we should not 
count every vote.  Every member of the party who says: All elections 
should be decided by machine count.  

God knows the founding fathers envisioned machines deciding the fate of 
our elections.  That's why they counted the votes by hand in the months 
following the election.  And we would have continued their tradition if it 
wasn't for the 20th amendment.

Better jump down a manhole
Light yourself a candle
Don't wear sandals
Try to avoid the scandals
Don't wanna be a bum
You better chew gum
The pump don't work
'Cause the vandals took the handles


The end is here.  I hope you have lots of guns, water and supplies, 
because the United States of America is floundering and with the fall of 
the Great American Empire, so falls the world.

data.headline: 2001 in 2001
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Imagine if you will.  New Years Eve in the year 2000.  Two men set out on 
a quest.  A quest to watch a film.  A quest that cannot fail.  Failure is 
simply not an option for these two men.  They are on a voyage, a voyage to 
a cabin.  A cabin that has a VCR.  A VCR that may or may not have RCA 
inputs.  A VCR that may or may not work.  A limited number of power outlet 
plugs perhaps.  These contingencies have been planned for.  

Let us examine the contents of the bag.  1 stereo + video RCA cable, of a 
decent length.  1 extension cord with three outlets and no ground.  One 
extension cord with three outlets and a ground.  1 Toshiba DVD player.  
1 "Kubrick Collection" copy of 2001, on DVD of course.  And finally, 
one "backup" copy of 2001 on Videotape.  As I said earlier, failure is not 
an option.

And now to the particulars of the situation.  They have arrived at the 
cabin and things are not looking good.  The VCR does have RCA inputs but 
there seems to be something wrong with it as the movie is playing behind a 
blue screen and rotating on and off the screen.  Fortunately, our heroes 
have a backup plan.  However, something far more sinister is afoot...

The partiers at this New Year's Eve party seem to be another obstacle to 
this quest.  As they are all female.  That's right oh my brothers and only 
friends, all female.  Let us recount a few of the things that Thunt knows 
about females: 1. Chicks dig flowers.  Ok, so, that was only one thing, 
but that's what Thunt knows.  Regardless, Thunt was about to learn one 
more thing: Chicks do not dig Sci-Fi.  It's not that they don't dig it or 
they couldn't learn to dig it or that they aren't some genetic misfits who 
do not represent the rest of their race, it just seems that when you 
mention the title like 2001: A Space Odyssey the room turns harsh and 
while hopes were previously high they now seemed to be thoroughly dashed 
upon jagged rocks like a hippy on an acid trip watching the Titanic sink.  
Or perhaps it wasn't science fiction.  It could be some kind of Anti-
Kubrick kick due to the nudity and crazy sex parties in Eyes Wide Shut, or 
some kind of throwback anger about the rape scenes in A Clockwork Orange.  
Despite these reasons for female anger, Kubrick remains, in this author's 
eyes to be without a doubt, one of the best American filmmakers ever 
(period).

These were indeed dark times for our intrepid heroes who simply wished to 
watch 2001 in 2001.  But their leader knew that the best thing to do was 
to wait.  Perhaps their animosity would diminish at the evening dragged on 
and the cosmopolitans flowed like water under a bridge.  Perhaps a liberal 
amount of kindness and salsa mixed with cream cheese would sooth the anger 
of these savage beasts and allow our brave heroes a chance to experience 
the HAL 9000 in the year for which his story was named.

Their leader was right, for the most part.  After the clock struck twelve, 
the party poppers popped and the evening began to wind down to it's 
inevitable sleepy conclusion, our hero gave a sign to our other hero and 
the tape began upon its merry way through the video cassette player.  Thus 
Spake Zarthursta, the famous chords of Strauss begin as the sun shone over 
the monolith.  Just then, the outrage of the beasts began to be heard, 
matching in time with the grunting of the primordial humans.  "Turn it 
off", "Put on Austin Powers", "Can we watch this tomorrow?", "Pour me 
another drink".  It was at this time that we were to find out who was 
truly a hero willing to be martyred for his cause, and who was a 
daytripping rebel, only along for the ride.  One of our brave heroes 
showed himself to be not so brave and retreated with his wife to bed, 
leaving our brave leader and hero among heroes to deal with the jeers and 
catcalls of these vicious sirens.  But unlike this quasi-hero, the true 
hero would not break.  He turned the volume low and waited for the effects 
of the potions to take effect and return these naysayers to their full and 
upright sleeping positions.  Our hero bided his time well, explaining that 
the bit with the monkeys was only the beginning of the picture.  And that 
the picture would make sense when one committed oneself to it's dark cold 
tunnel and emerged upon the other side.  These comments aligned with the 
beautiful Blue Danube Waltz relaxed these unrelentless firedragons and 
they began to sleep soundly on their respective couches.  Leaving our hero 
undisturbed as he once more enjoyed Stanley Kubrick's immortal vision of 
the future.  A vision entitled: 2001: A Space Odyssey.

data.headline: A Response From The Quasi-Hero
data.author: John the Quasi-Hero
data.story: The new year has come and I failed our fearless leader our mission to watch a movie that needed to watched by all this New Years Eve "2001: A Space Odyssey". I realize my failure ruined a once in a lifetime chance to watch Kubrick's classic sci-fi epic, but I must defend myself. First, I had a sinking feeling that the company me and our fearless leader would be keeping would be anti-Kubrick and all female. This was one of the reasons that I asked Mr. Hunt to accompany me, because he does need more exposure to the other sex, and EVERYONE needs to be exposed to Kubrick. Second, I did get the movie, along with Mr. Hunt to the cabin and the movie was played. I realize that Mr. Hunt would have liked a companion during the movie, and I realize that I was too intoxicated to watch the movie, but never the less the movie was shown and therefore our mission was not a complete failure. Third, I AM MARRIED. My wife had been sick, and she needed my company to fall asleep, besides I am COMPLETELY WHIPPED. I felt that if I did not go to bed with my wife at that time, the possibility of me getting laid in the near future would have been slight. I do apologize to Mr. Hunt on my lack of backbone but I feel the reasons that I have given are acceptable, especially #3, and I know that many of you out there would have done the same. I hope that Mr. Hunt has not lost faith in me for I am willing to redeem myself at anytime, besides it will be 2001 for a whole year and the movie will be around for much longer than that. Editors Note: The capital letters on the words COMPLETELY WHIPPED were placed there by the author, not by the editor. Also, the Editor would like to take this moment to remind the world that the fearless leader was not the only one enjoying 2001 that night. Across the galaxy, in Southern California, Brother Brown and Brother Kemper were also enjoying the picture. On a projector and with a crowd of scientists no less.

data.headline: Quixotic in 2001
data.author: Conscientious Objector
data.story: I was also present at the New Years Eve Party and I would like to respond to Thunts shameful article. Thunt may call himself a hero, but clearly he falls quite short of that lofty title. In the case of his article 2001 on 2001, Thunt is nothing more than a hero of ludicrous insults. For Thunt to call me and my friends savage beasts and vicious sirens is an outrage. If anyone was savage or beastly at that party, it was Thunt with his incessant whining about how no one wanted to watch his great (his opinion, not mine) movie. We came to the cabin to have fun, not to watch some cinematic dinosaur of 1969. I think that Austin Powers is a hilarious movie and I enjoy watching it, even when I am a little tipsy. Thunt is wacked, he describes himself as a hero on a quest, but at the cabin he was more like Don Quixote ignorantly and foolishly attacking windmills. And his sidekick Sancho Panza was none less impressive in his actions I must say. Furthermore, Thunts comments about women and science fiction are completely random and unfounded. I and my friends frequently enjoy science fiction, just not on New Years Eve when were trying to have a good time. I dont know how many of you are into science fiction but typically it isnt very uplifting to the human spirit (as most of them revolve around the human race destroying the earth in some way.) Thunt is despicable pond scum and the next time I see him, I plan to kick him square in the nutz. Editors Note: Its a good thing we have a non-biased editor here at Thunt.net to ensure that you people get the truth, the whole truth and nothing but the truth, because there is no way that Thunt would have let this article through. The part where she calls 2001 a cinematic dinosaur would have probably set him off ranting for 2-3 hours easy.

data.headline: Thoughts on a Friday
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: Do you ever think back to when you were a kid and you were first told of what Communist Russia was like. I recall hearing about a test that you would have to take and this test would tell the government what your aptitudes were in subjects such as Math, Science, Writing, Russian, etc. And based upon this test you would be sent to a school to learn how to do better what you do well or you were sent to a farm to raise crops, or sent to the city to be a bathroom attendant. Now, I have to say that I found this to be an interesting system. It seemed sensible to assign those good at science to the field of scientists. Although I must say it is egotistical for me to believe that my test results would place me into one of the schools of higher learning, but surely the readers of Thunt.net (yourselves being made up of 87% college attendees or graduates) must understand the reasons for my and assumedly your positive outlook for the test results. Personally I cant see myself or any reader of Thunt.net handing out towels to strangers in a bathroom somewhere. Its just not plausible. Anyway, today I was thinking about the stuff I want to do and perhaps it would be easier I could just walk downtown to some Ministry of Writers, Directors and Novelists and apply to take the test. It just seems nice that if you want to do something you take the test, if you fail, I guess thats a major setback and you have to regroup, but if you passed, you would be on a government tested and approved fast track towards your desired profession. Not that Im any kind of communist, but sometimes it is discouraging to sit on the floor and think about where your future is going to go, what you are going to be, what you are going to do, what you want to do and not know. The alternative system seems to provide at the very least: certainty and job security. That sounds pretty good to me right now. Thunt is a former columnist for the State Hornet, a world-famous movie critic and frequently tries to use the nonsense word apon instead of the proper upon.

data.headline: A Siren He Said
