data.headline: Laments of a Video Game Junkie
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: With the recent release of the X-Box and the clamor of the populace to purchase the Playstation 2 I find myself thinking about video games, and I cannot help but name them as one of the foremost distractions of my life. When I was a child, I read constantly. Right up until the release of the Nintendo Entertainment System. Before the Nintendo, video games were a once in a while thing. A fun way to spend a few quarters at the local Chuck-E-Cheeses, but not a full-blown lifestyle obsession. But when I finally convinced my parents to purchase the brand new Nintendo as my Christmas present everything changed. When I think about it now, I cant fathom how much time I spent playing video games. Until my parents banned Nintendo during the week and subjugated my obsession to a weekend passion I played every waking minute. Little did I know that this glowing box was destroying my social skills one level at a time. Not to say that I stopped socializing or that I cant socialize today, but I think that I would be better equipped if I hadnt been strapped to the glowing box day and night. I did have friends. They also played video games. Of course, when I describe playing video games, I dont mean just playing occasionally for fun, Im describing total emergence in the subject matter. We bought strategy guides, read Nintendo Power, agonized over what new games to purchase and frequently combined our skills to slay the water demon in level 8. In the end, I look back to see what the years of gaming has accomplished. The unfortunate answer is not a whole lot. Certainly, I have beaten the Legend of Zelda (1,2,3), the Super Mario Bros games (1,2,3,4) and dozens of other popular titles. I have achieved near dominance at some sports games so that no non-computer opponent will dare to face my team on the field, but what does all of this amount to in the real world: nothing. But I suppose all of the hobbies of childhood pale when confronted ten to fifteen years down the line. Im sure some of you regret or lament the amount of time spent killing imaginary dragons in D&D. Others probably fear what the perfect body of Barbie had done for their self-esteem and worldview. There are many good things about childhood, but sometimes you find yourself looking back, wishing that you had spent that time more productively. But if you did, you probably never would have experienced childhood. Thomas Hunt is still a vicious killer in Half-life and proudly states for the record the only thing resembling a video game installed on his system is Chessmaster 8000.

data.headline: A Goodbye Letter (or Ryan is scared shitless about tomorrow)
data.author: Ryan Anne Polli
data.story:
At 21 years old I have successfully put off the
removal of my wisdom teeth, something my dentist has
been urging me to since 1997. I actually had a date to
do it then but backed out because I had to watch the
paint dry or something of serious urgency like that.
So now, I am T minus 15 hours away from my appointment
with my friend Anesthesia and just slightly freaked
out at the thought of having someone rip pieces of
bone out of my skull. So, in the possibility that I am
the one in 968,392 people in this world to not come
back from the happy gas I thought I would write a
little something to be forever etched in the cached
pages of the world wide web and this fantastic site
that our friend Thunt has built. Here are my thoughts_
laugh and/or learn. These are in no particular order
of importance.

- Lemon covered with salt is not a good snack.
I recently discovered (yesterday) that my better half
might possibly be crazy. At a dining excursion he had
a lemon on his plate, doused it with salt and ate it.
He didn't seem to mind it... it sounded neat. I should
have known. better_ but who am I to talk. Ask Endean
and he_ll tell you that I combine fruit and meat as an
appetizer. 

- Kharma is a bitch.
I am happy with the knowledge that the people in my
life who have fucked with me and those close to me
will get it in the end... you know who you are and you
know I am right.

- There is life without frosted flakes
Hard to believe but it is true. This is only knowledge
because being on the road in the summer when all your
money is gone you find that a box of cereal goes a
long way to replace meals before your next paycheck.
Milk not included.

- There are places not to get drunk.
Arizona, Santa Rosa (but not like there is anything
else to do) and well I dont really remember the
other place.

- Alora Blum is an awesome person.
She gets props because of all the things that I have
ever done in my life (proud and not so proud) Alora
has always been there. She is the only one who kicked
it with me in the lodge when I broke my wrist
snowboarding, she defended me when I was stone drunk
and passed out on New Years 2000, and she even helped
me hail crazy people from the freeway to tow my truck
out of the dirt. Everyone needs an Alora Blum in their
lives.

- "Brother, if you can't paint in Paris, you better
give up and marry the bosses daughter."

- Everyone has a theme song. Whether it changes from
year to year or day to day_ know it and own the CD.
Those who know me best know me as the Dixie Chicks
song Sin Wagon. Give it a listen and you will
understand why. Music (and I am not talking about the
Madonna CD) is a fantastic release for anyone. If you
don_t like music, you must not have a soul.

- Silent Horizon kicks ass.
nuff said. Well, not to mention their bass player is
freakin hot.

-Long stories and articles get boring.
So I hope to see you all at the right end of the
tunnel. Man this is going to suck.

data.headline: Happenings and detonations
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: I. Detonations I started this article by writing the Happenings (or the work that Ive done recently to add some new toys to thunt.net) but it turns out youre probably all more interested in the battle royal thats going down on the thunt.net message board. I dont know who the mysterious author is who writing angrily on the message board. But Ive got to tell you I find it incredibly entertaining and I hope he or she keeps it up. His/her recent comments about Ryan Endeeean are pretty damn funny. Theres something about the phrase "my pencil-neck is so far up my superior's ass that I can suck her dick" that I just fancy so. Its good to a phrase that so deftly attacks two people at the same time. My only regret is that Im not behind this recent rash of comments. So, regardless of your views, my hat is off to you sir or madam for spicing up the message board and raising up some controversy and mystery. II. Happenings I wanted to write a brief message out to all you Thunt.neters outlining some of the new features Ive recently implemented on Thunt.net. They arent much, but I thought Id drop you all a line and let you know whats up. Thunt.net 2.0 (simplicity) - Ive adapted the new thunt.net code to allow you to surf thunt.net the classic 2.0 way. Look for version 1.0 to come along soon. Thunt.net - State Hornet Style - Now that the business-savvy State Hornet has decided to sign with yet another corporation, Ive launched Thunt.net - State Hornet Style. Its a parody of the old statehornet.com website packed with, you guessed it, the content of Thunt.net. (expect them to attempt to kick me out of school again for this. Also theyll be quite mad to think that Im allowing you to check out the new ISPI generated www.hornetonline.com) Damn its ugly and template-like. But youve gotta respect the hornet for spending so much time and using the mighty marketing forces of CampusEngine to market statehornet.com to turn around and change their domain name. Maybe I should change this sites name to thuntnetonline.net. Quick Recap of other features - Remember you can always sign up for the Thunt.net mailing list, that will send you an automated message whenever thunt.net is updated. Its the best way to keep up on whats up at thunt.net, without doing any work. Also remember that we have a site search here at thunt.net (thanks to Matt). The site search includes a offensive picture that those who understand satire will find funny. Also try searching for fuck. You can pull up most of the archive that way. Additionally we have the submit a story button that allows you to write for thunt.net. And finally the Email story to a friend feature which allows you to easily send an article on thunt.net to your friends, or your enemies or whatever.

data.headline: School is upon us
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Now is the hour of our discontent, oh my brothers and only friends.  Well 
really, I'm not all that discontented.  I'm rather looking forward to 
school, as God willing (all of you must knock on wood to acknowledge your 
faith in the superstitions that keep the world spinnin' round); I'll be 
graduating at the end of this coming semester.  Personally, I'm not afraid 
of the classes, I'm more afraid of the post-graduation stuff.  I don't 
know how many of you are on the fast track through college to job to 
married with children to death, but that's a roller coaster that I'm not 
ready to board yet.  Thank you kindly, but no sir.  So, post graduation I 
don't know what I'm going to do but let's get back to the subject at 
hand.... The Spring 2001 semester.

I suppose that while I have all your attention perhaps I should make some 
predictions. 

1. Hannibal will be rad - The new movie by Ridley Scott from the Thomas 
Harris book will rock as much as the book did.  The replacement of 
Jodie "I understand my character, she would never do that" Foster with 
Julianne "Nudity doesn't bother me" Moore will not effect the film or the 
character.  After a few frames, you'll accept her as Agent Starling and be 
mesmerized again by another brilliant Anthony Hopkins performance 
(hopefully it will erase from your mind the fact that he was in Zorro).

2. Andy will redesign the flag 6 times - Last semester it was pretty much 
an every issue kind of thing, but this semester I think he's got it under 
control and I think he'll only redesign it 6 times.  You can start your 
own pools on this too.

3. The Hornet's "web people" will begin studying acronyms to try to look 
smart.  Words like PHP, PGP, ASP, SQL, CYA, RTFM, NATO can be confusing 
for some people, but I hope that this semester the Hornet will finally get 
that whole JPG, GIF, PNG (not really an acronym, just a sound, ping!) mess 
figured out.  Perhaps with a little help from the Macintosh computers that 
will hide all the ROTFLOL errors from their SVGA IMAC screens.

4. The man who sails the seven seas will become a pirate. ARRR!  To quote 
Steve Jobs "it's better to be a pirate than to join the navy".  Of course, 
there is something to be said about being a First Class Seamen.  Or is it 
Seamen, First Class!

5. Campus Engine will make a major comeback selling T-shirts and other 
fashionable attire.  College kids will adopt the logo of the failed 
company and wear it with pride.  Engine will become a slang phrase for 
bong hits after a former employee teaches the children how to chug-chug-
chug...

6. The pool on G's death will reach so high that the organizers of the 
pool will consider hiring an assassin but they will back out at the last 
minute, leaving one angry hit man.

7. People will learn to love reverse-board technology.  They will 
understand the value of having the newest messages at the top and learn 
when reading top to bottom, not to read threads before they read the 
original article.

8. Andy will beat up 2-3 more members of the staff, leaving him to truly 
be the master of the newsroom, the awesome artist, the triumphant tracer, 
the end of endeeean: Andy!

9. Dude, Andy fucking rules. That's all I've gotta say.




Well, I think that's enough predictions for now.  If I started predicting 
trouble for people who remain unnamed, my crystal ball and me would get in 
deep shit.  And no body wants that.  He he.

data.headline: Dotcom Detective
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: author's note: this is to be read in the style of a 1920s detective novel. Imagine the voice of Humphrey Bogart... My name is Jones. I work out of 53rd street. Im a private dick. Yesterday the bitch came to my office. Why do I call her a bitch and not a dame? Well as they say, the proof is in the pudding, enjoy your Jello. She said she had a job for me. I said, You dont look like the type who does much hiring, isnt it usually the other way around? She slapped me hard across the face and said Im business savvy. Do you want the job or not? Well, I havent had a real job since I had to do that thing to that redhead and the man shed run off with and it would be nice to get back to some normal 9-5 dicking. Perhaps I said, Whats the job? Well she said, I work for an insignificant corporation in the Union Telegraph building downtown and Ive got a problem. Oh yeah, what is it? Thats a pretty swank building. I almost added for a cheap slut like you but my jaw still stung from that last shot she got off and Id rather stick to my usual strategy of kicking my clients when they are down, not when they are sitting right across from me. Well you see Mr. Jones, Im the ruler of dozens of men and a few women. A few months ago we signed a contract with a dotcom vender to deliver our paper clips to the office, as we had no possible way of doing this ourselves I could see where her story was going. In the last few months Id handled dozens of dotcom revenge jobs. Most of them were simple firebombing. Some people just wanted some promotional merchandise that they could destroy and defecate on in the privacy of their own home, the sick fucks. But I could tell that this bitchs story was going to be different. There was something about the dull look in her eyes and the lack of chest ornamentation that I knew her story would be especially interesting. So you see Ive re-signed with the company that bought the failed dotcom and my employees are angry. Theyve been talking about revolution. There is an organizer named TtamNrowB. I hear that it is not his real name, but an acronym (try this at home, Junior Crimestoppers!), but Im not really bright enough to figure those things out. Can you help me detective? You still havent asked me what you want me to do, Miss? Flatlands, Marsha Flatlands. Well Miss Flatlands, what do you have in mind? She shifted in her chair in an obvious attempt to increase her attractiveness. One of her spaghetti straps fell and once more I realized that it anytime was a good time to invest in silicon futures. She said Well, Mr. Jones Id like you to Tune in next time, same Thunt time, same Thunt.net for the next exciting chapter.

data.headline: Dotcom Detective, Part II
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: I still cant forget the mouth on that bitch yesterday. There was something about the way she spoke to her present and former employees that just gave me the creeps. And Im a private dick, I should be used to this kind of language. But to hear it from a bitch, thats something else entirely. After I agreed to take her case yesterday, she took me over to her office to meet her staff. Then for some unknown reason, she just went berserk. She beat one employee to the ground screaming postit ion! Assume the postit ion!! Then she pulled another employee into her office and fired her on the spot. I suppose she was trying to impress me with the size of her dick. But she didnt realize that private dicks keep their dicks private for the same reason Shaft keeps his public. There was just no comparison between her dick and mine, so she paid me the five hundred and I was on the case. The first thing she wanted me to do was rough up a hooker who worked in the office under her. I assumed it was your typical catfight situation, but boy was I wrong. I knocked on the door. It opened a crack, with a chain holding it closed. Who is it? she said. Tina Slinger? I said. Yes? My name is Jones. Your boss Miss Flatlands sent me here to talk to you. I was wondering if we might have a word. Ok she said, as she closed and opened the door removing the chain. Come on in. I walked into her apartment. It was plush and well decorated in pastels. The life of a hooker suddenly didnt seem that bad. Miss Flatlands tells me that you do tricks in the office. Are you interested? she said, 20 for a blow, 50 for a fuck, and for a 100 you can stay all night. Affordable rates, I thought, and not a bad package as I surveyed her tight fitting t-shirt and thigh high skirt. But not today, I thought. Im on a case. Has Miss Flatlands spoken to you about her disapproval of your profession? Oh. Is that what this is all about? Im sorry that shes a hermaphrodite, detective, I really am. But I follow the prostitutes code: men yes, women maybe, freaks with both sex organs no. Thats quite a code. Well mainly its because we werent trained in pleasing freaks of nature. I see. So what did she want you to do, pound me? Yes, she did. Well, are you gonna do it or not? Not this time. The hermaphroditic bitch lied to me, and I dont do dishes for lies. I must get going. Thank you very much for your time, Miss Slinger. No problem detective, oh and if youre ever in the neighborhood, drop by and Ill do you for free. Im just dying to get my mouth around that Private Dick. Hookers, I thought. Theyre all the same, absolutely in love with the LD. Sure I said I might stop by, knowing full well that it wasnt true. I had other problems to deal with and this neighborhood was the pits. Frankly, I hoped Id never come back through this way again. But what to do about this hermaphrodite bitch. I suppose Ill have to Will Mr. Jones beat the bitch into a bloody pulp? Will he let bygones be bygones and take the bitch out for a beer? Will he attempt to use the LD to please the unpalatable hermaphrodite monster? Find out on the next Thunt.net.

data.headline: Dotcom Detective, Part III
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Well, Id dealt with the hermaphrodite all right.  But that was a 
bloody mess and only psychopaths like Lecter, Bateman, and Caulfield like 
to sit around and discuss that kind of shit.

	Suddenly the clouds parted and the sun shined brighter than I have 
ever seen.  I dropped to my knees as I absorbed all of the glory of Gods 
creation.  Then the Lord came down from heaven and in a voice that sounded 
like Bobcat Goldwaith the Lord exclaimed Jones?

	Yes?  I said.

	Do you enjoy serving me? said the Lord.

	Well, it beats the hell out of doing laundry.

	Excuse me?

	Uhhh, yes, I greatly enjoy serving you.

	Good, because I have a job for you.

	What a week I thought, first I get hired to murder a hooker by a 
hermaphrodite with dental problems.  And now God is on my ass.  Im never 
going to get to watch that tape of the Super Bowl I made last week.  And 
it is only a matter of time before somebody tells me the ending.

	Baltimore beat the hell out of them.  Said God.

	What?

	Oh, you didnt know I could read your thoughts?

	Well, I thought you might be able to but I needed to do an inner 
monologue to keep the readers entertained.  Otherwise its all talk talk 
talk.

	Hmmm.  Sounds like a good idea.  Ill have them put some in the 
next version of the Bible.

	When is the street date for that anyway?

	God sighs and said I wanted to release it in 2000, but the 
lawyers havent cleared the rights yet.

	You have lawyers in heaven?

	Theyre on loan from Hell.

	And you think thats a good idea.

	God shrugged and said Youve gotta have somebody to throw 
lightning bolts at.  And I think the communists and Mormons were getting 
tired of the constant anal pain.

	Touché.  So, not to rush you or anything, but whats the job?

	Well my son, there is a bit of a revolution going on.  I dont 
really like to talk about it because too many people know already and that 
gives the surprise away.  But regardless of what I can tell you, I can 
tell you this:  I want you to be in charge of propaganda.

	I thought to myself, most people would consider this a holy quest, 
a gift from the great garbanzo bean, a divine offer from monster.com, but 
to me it was just another job.  Although there was something about running 
propaganda for Gods revolution that really interested me.  And it sure 
did beat working for hermaphrodites.  So I said, Sure thing God, when do 
I start?

	Tomorrow he said.  Tomorrow you will tell the people about my 
revolution.  Tomorrow you will know the reasons that things happen.  And 
tomorrow you will all rise up for my revolution.

	God is a bit repetitive, I thought, but he sure does know how to 
do a cliffhanger.  Tune in tomorrow to learn more about the revolution.  
Right here on Thunt.net  Inform the masses.

data.headline: Dotcom Detective, part IV
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: So the last time we spoke, Id been tapped on the shoulder by God. A true holy command. At first I followed Gods will like a fucking lap dog. But no inspiration came. God had left me hanging out to dry like he did to his chosen people time and time again. That pink whore was trying to play me for a fool. Boy was I angry. So angry that I felt I needed something to relax me and help me forget about Gods vicious ploy. So I stopped in at the local tea house. The tea house was decorated in the classical British style. Lots of deep rich oak walls, dim lighting and well-dressed men and women kicking back after a hard day lounging around on their fat rich asses with a cup of tea. It seemed like the perfect day to kick back and forget the anger that had plagued me earlier. I walked up to the maitre-d and requested a table. The cocksucking whore said Im sorry sir, but all of our tables are in use by a private party. Less then a second after he spoke, he knew he had made a mistake as he felt the cold steel of my .45 magnum on his clammy white temple. I believe you have one empty table. Ahhh he cleared his throat and said we do, sir. Step right this way. Thats right bitch, I thought as I slid the magnum back into my trench coat. The table was nice but it was right next to the private party that had reserved the entire tea house. Some how I could tell by their top hats, diamond tiaras, and well dressed man servants standing behind them that we werent going to get along. Now, what can I get for you sir? Earl Grey, straight up. I said, And make it snappy. Of course sir. My tea arrived quickly and soon I had a hot cup in my hand. You there. What are you doing at our private tea party? said the well dressed man who seemed to be the leader of the party. Just sitting here, drinking my tea, trying to relax. I said. You cant relax here you commoner! Get out of our private tea party! Sir, you are making me Angry. Your anger is disinteresting to me, common man! Maitre-d, Maitre- d, remove that thing from our tea party. That was it. Sometimes these rich fucks go too far. Hed picked the wrong man to mess with. I rose from my table and in a single movement I hurled my tea cup at his face. It smashed up against his cheek and allowed the searing Earl Grey to scald his face and neck. He fell to the ground screaming like a little girl. I said to the crowd of horrified debutants, Whos next? The rich felchers dispatched their servants to attack me. I screamed out, No! Brothers, let us unite! Let us have an Angry Tea Party. They stopped and began to contemplate my offer. Then one of them grabbed the tea kettle and poured boiling hot tea on the head of his master ruining her $200 hair cut and her $1500 Armani outfit. That was all it took. The others joined in as we tore that tea room to shreds. Every one of those rich pukes got burned and bruised, including that asshole maitre-d, who I scalded myself. As we were pouring the last of the tea on the remnants of the party I realized that I was still in the employee of the Lord. This is what he wanted me to do. He wanted me to start an Angry Tea Party and spread the word that the days of the rich were numbered and that the poor were quickly becoming aware of their collective strength. With my quest now clearly defined I set forth upon a mission and I created the AngryTeaParty.com website. The website is a place where all of you will be able to spread your anger, drink their tea and have a fucking party. I ask you, the people of this earth to follow God as I have and write for the Angry Tea Party. We need Rants, Reviews and Poetry to fuel the fire of our revolution. Rants should be any expression of great power. It need not be angry but it must be strong in its views. Reviews can be of movies, music or Other. Other can review anything from restaurants, dotcom collapses, shoes, classes, gum, backpack straps, websites, pens, pencils, etc. Absolutely anything! Poetry is essentially that which is not a review or a rant. Prose poetry and short stories are more than welcome. The key to the party is you. We need your help to make this the best Angry Tea Party ever! So please visit AngryTeaParty.com today and then fire up the old word processor and show us your true power. Write for AngryTeaParty.com today and join the revolution! Eat Snacky Smores!

data.headline: Facts, Rumors and False Truths
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: Ahhh, the demand of the people for new content. Since the people are demanding it, Ive decided to pull a few rumors out of the old rumor mill and print them here on Thunt.net. Since, like the Hornet, we have no journalistic standards, I see no reason why I shouldnt print these harmless rumors. Remember the key word here is rumor. Rumor can sometimes mean false. Other times it can mean true. Its up to you, True Believer to make your decision and see the lies. (Or the truth) Fucking Incompetent Lets talk about these words. These words are a mighty insult; an insult that Im sure none of you thunt.neters would take sitting down. These words are a direct attack on your ability to do your job correctly, but let us guess who these words have been labeled upon recently. Could it be that one estranged AD told one E that she was FUCKING INCOMPENTANT? Could that be true? Well it is true because thats what Ive heard from the extensive grapevine streaming out from the hornet, the grapevine that I try to ignore, but hey, when its this good, how can I just say no? Embarrassed about the paper? Well, we all knew this was going to be a year from the record books. As the selection of the false E was closed last year, we all discussed it and we all knew how bad it would be. Even full-time members of the hornet sadly stated, Some years, you know its going to be a good year and some years you know its going to be a bad year. Some knew that it would be worse than others. But let us, right here on thunt.net (Im referring to my friends and enemies in the message board), lets get all of the goods and bads out on the table and decide how bad it really is. Ive heard all kinds of horrible stories from the Hornets recent past. I heard that a few years ago, a totally wacked Editor in Chief ran a story on the front page stating that the newly built Mendocino Hall was really a landing pad for Gs Martian Buddies. He later wrote a letter against our dearest faculty-advising friends, distributed it to all of the faculty mailboxes and fled the country in disgrace. The funny thing about that story is that Ive always rooted for him, not the faculty advisors. Its funny how sometimes the bad guy is really the good guy. So, back to modern times. There is no doubt that everyone has read Vinces letter to the editor from last week and Davids letter this week (that I had the pleasure of previewing on one of those neat printouts that look like the paper, you know what Im talking about, boy its neat to be outside and yet see so much). They were both pretty damn disappointed in the Hornets latest stripper escapades and complete lack of reporting. These are pretty strong charges from former hornet faithful. Moreover, with the way things have been going in the message board its clear that I, while once the villain of the office am now being raised to the level of savior. Alternatively, if not savior, at least now, I am in perfect position to say, I told you so. And nothing is better than that. I told you so. In addition to David and Vinces indignation Ive also had the pleasure of talking to last years designer savant, who also expressed some of the same ideas espoused by Mr. Sommers and Mr. Faola. (Shell probably kick my ass for mentioning her here, but hey, Ive got a big ass and Im sure Ill heal.) So the paper sucks, all of the hard work of the little men could not save them from their evil boss. I hope that next week they write another article without facts and suck on Gs big dick some more. Why do I say hope, lets face it, we all know what theyre going to do next week. What a sweet future lies ahead, oh my brothers and only friends. False E? Well, its been a while, and Ive stopped my mouth from spreading rumors long enough. It is a year of stolen elections and it is time to expunge old rumors from my closet. Now let me remind you Junior Crimestoppers out there that these are rumors. Im not saying that theyre true or anything. They could be as false as the Apollo Moon Landing; Im just using my right to free speech (knock, knock) to say what Ive heard, no more, no less. What I heard was during last years voting for E, there was a minor coup detat played by Americas favorite player (not shaft). 1. They were supposed to hold to vote until one voting member returned from a meeting. They didnt. 2. Other student votes were not present at the meeting. There were three candidates for E. We will call them by cute nicknames as we always do here at thunt.net. One candidate was named Clueless- Outsider. Clueless-Outsider had an excellent standing and reputation with the student body but knew nothing about newspapers. One candidate was named Decent-Man. Decent-Man was a decent man; hed done nothing wrong, but hadnt really done anything right. He was decent and would have done a decent job. One candidate was named Clueless-Insider. It was clear by his performance inside, that he was absolutely clueless, despite his cool inside status. Americas Favorite Player had a really cool plan. If Clueless-Outsider becomes E, then I can rule him and run the paper my self. (Insert evil laughter, HAHAHAHAHAHA) The voters heard the speeches and AFP called for a vote. The vote was won by Decent-Man. Hooray for Decency and good will! Clueless-Insider came in third, as no one really wanted him. And the AFP led Clueless-Outsider came in a close second. Well, according to the rules this vote is it, congratulations Decent-Man, right? No. Wrong. The AFP said that even though she wanted the vote, that the board should talk about it some more. The board, without any student representatives, said, Sure, lets break the law, that would be cool, fuck Decent-Man! The board then discussed, discussed and discussed. Finally, they were deadlocked. The two Clueless-Outsider camps and Decent-Man camps were dead even. So what did they do, they chose Clueless-Insider as a compromise. This worked out great for all parties. The board got to go home. The AFP got someone she could control. Although not as easy to control as Clueless-Outsider, but hey, when youre evil, you cant win em all. And that is the story of the rumor of the Election. (Please note that for you trying to prescribe additional meaning to my parable, please insert Al Gore for Decent-Man. I know it was a transparent label, but I wouldnt want poor Al to feel bad.)

data.headline: What would happen if my other professors met my ethnics studies professor
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: I have a class that I absolutely cannot stand. Ethnics Studies 11. My professor treats us like children because there are freshmen in the class and lets face facts, they are children. In addition, she feels that because she is a professor now she does not have to do any research before spouting out her conclusions and that everything she says is pure truth, because she said it. I would now like to examine, for my entertainment and perhaps yours, what would happen if my other professors, who actually do research and are generally knowledgeable about their subjects were to meet this Ethnic studies professor and were forced to suffer through her class with me. English 50  My English 50 professor would no doubt scoff at her belief that literary characters are real people. Her example today was that the book The Indian in the Cupboard was racist and she asked why the book was not the other way around (The White Boy in the Cupboard). He would laugh at her and remind her as he reminds us that literary characters are not real. To do a Freudian analysis of Hawthorn is foolish because Hawthorn is not following Freud. He is following his own beliefs. Not to mention that the characters in his books are no real. The do not do anything other than what is written on the page. Tom Sawyer will never die, because Twain did not write that chapter. They are fictional, NOT REAL! Also on the subject of The Indian in the Cupboard she stated that it is so hard to regulate ideas. It was amazing. She was talking pure Orwellian censorship and the whole class sat silent before her learned opinion. If I thought she was interested in discussion I would have responded but I am well aware that the only opinion that matters is her own. This part of the class was fantastic. I only wish I had a tape recorder with me so I could have caught the words of this latter-day PC Thug and passed them along, they were stunning. Anthropology 102  My Anthro Professor would have no doubt pointed out to this uniformed fool that when she spoke of why anthropology seems to cover other cultures as the other, that it is the main point of anthropology to study other cultures. Yes, they seem to be treating the Indians like their culture is different from the culture they are writing their papers on their word processors, with their faxes, phone calls and emails. Well guess what! The CULTURES ARE DIFFERENT! The anthropologists are trying to study them! It is necessary to acknowledge these differences when studying them. In addition, she would point out that the use of the word shaman does separate the Indian Doctors from our modern scientific doctors. Shamans used witchcraft, magic, and herbs to cure people, while our modern doctors use the scientific method and western scientific medicine. There is a difference. Shamans are not doctors. Sure, to your tribe they were doctors, but because they lack x-ray machines and shit, they are not modern doctors. Maybe you could call them healers because their goals are to heal, but the ENGLISH word DOCTORS implies SCIENCE. NO SCIENCE, NO DOCTOR. History 162  My History 162 Professor, who is also my hero, would probably throw shit at this misinformed professor. He would point out that everything she has taught us so far and everything she is endeavoring to teach is VICTIUMOLOGY. IE: The study of the horrible things that the people of the past did. So far we have learned that the people of the past were bad to immigrants, made stereotypes, had slaves, i.e.: THEY WERE BAD. It is amazing that she can fill a whole semester with the horrors of the people of the past but she is doing good so far. In addition, the only people who seem to be good are the people who taught her, the enlightened minds of the 60s. Did I mention she went to Berkeley? It sure is true that before the 60s at Berkley, everyone was bad. And after the 60s at Berkley, everyone was good. What an amazing time the 60s were. Gosh, Im so glad to live in the good times that the 60s created, we are so lucky. Otherwise we would be bad like those slime balls of the past. Also the esteemed published History Professor would smack her for the way she does research. She went to Roseville or some other god damned place and someone tried to give the parking spot they were pulling out of to someone else, not HER! It was obviously because they were RACIST. Do you know why she sees racism everywhere she goes, its because shes LOOKING FOR IT. It is a common rule when studying History to not have your conclusions made before you do your research. If you have your conclusions made you will ignore other evidence that doesnt support your conclusion, it happens all the time. Let us examine her story. 1. She doesnt note what direction the exits of the parking lot were. Perhaps this man was simply turning in the direction that he was going. I do not expect him to go in the direction away from his destination just to please the angry native american driving the mini-van. 2. Perhaps these two people knew each other and he was trying to be friendly to an acquaintance. There are dozens of other factors that could have been vital to telling the story, but she left all of those out so that she could present her BIASED story to the class as TRUTH. History 192  My History 192 professor would probably try to explain to her using evidence. We just read a book about the great plains (The contested plains by Elliot West) that clearly proved that the native americans underwent dozens of changes in their culture before and after the white man came. They were not a perfect society, an Eden, as she believes. Also he might point out what another great History Professor once said The people who complain about the white man should examine the work of the Mongols. When the Mongols came you wouldnt have any of your culture or your land because they would kill every last man woman and child and take your land and wipe out your culture. They were lucky that the Mongols never invaded North America or things would have been very different. Well, I think thats enough of a rant for today. This class sucks, but I have to take it to graduate (thats another rant for another day).

data.headline: Hornet Jeep towed & objective journalism
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
I recall it just like it was yesterday.  I was driving to my distant 
parking spot at a school that is all too near in my memory when I saw the 
hornet jeep hooked up to an orange facilities management tow truck.  My 
first thought was: Holy Shit! Do I have my camera?  The fortunate 
answer was yes.  I did have my camera, due to my recent idea to shoot the 
roll of film I found in my desk.  I pulled my car into the lot and quickly 
double parked it and preceded to take pictures of the jeep being towed.  I 
had a great story on my hands, right?  The hornet jeep being towed, talk 
about symbolism, talk about the destruction of a once decent paper, talk 
about incompetence.  My first thoughts were that it was towed because it 
was parked in the ECS parking lot, not in its normal spot by the Union.  

With these ideas and these photos I could have had a great story, a 
headliner, a true breakthrough for Thunt.net.  But ladies and gentlemen, 
this is where we learn a lesson about journalism.  Check the facts.  Being 
the good Samaritan I am, I parked my car and walked to the Hornet office 
to inform them that their jeep was being towed.  I rang the bell and the 
Hornets friendly secretary D (for her security we have removed her name 
from this publication.) walks out and greets me politely, for as you all 
know I once worked at the Hornet and D once hooked me up with free movie 
tickets.  I informed her that the hornet jeep was being towed and she told 
me that they were having it towed because a wheel had fallen off.  I 
nodded my head in understanding and she thanked me for the heads up.  See 
how checking the facts can really effect a story?  Unlike the journalists 
of the Hornet, the journalists of Thunt.net always check their sources.  
Now the facts of the story read: Hornet Jeep towed due to wheel falling 
off rather than Hornet Jeep towed due to parking violation.  That is 
the difference between fact and fiction.  Also note the objectivity of the 
journalist, who did not point out that the wheel falling off is a much 
greater symbol than it just being towed for the mere carelessness of a 
poor parking job.  This has been Thomas Hunt in Sacramento, for Thunt.net 
Special Reports.

data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Nostalgia is a powerful force ladies and gentlemen.  Nostalgia and 
thunt.net  The Tiffany show was an amazing event.  There were hundreds of 
people there and it didnt rain.  What does this show?  The power of 
Celebrity.  Tiffany was once famous.  The people in the audience were not 
all Tiffany fans.  Most of them probably did by her record then and they 
probably wont buy it now.  But regardless of their various levels of 
fandom they were all united by their incredible obsession with famous 
people.

Tiffany was once famous.  In those days she was a cute 14-year old Mall 
Queen Pop Star.  Now shes a twenty nine year old mother of one.  The 
years have been both hard and soft on Tiffany.  Her tight spandex pants 
hide little from this reporters sight.  Her breasts are sagging but she 
remains attractive.  Attractive enough to regain her pop stardom and 
compete with the likes of Jennifer Lopez, Christina, Aguliera and Britney 
Spears?  In the view of this jaded correspondent, not a chance.

Enough about the appearance of this aging has been.  On to the music.  
Tiffany played mostly new material on this sunny winter day.  Her songs 
are an eclectic mix of the tired style of Alanis Morrissette, Sarah 
McLachlan, and Shania Twain.  The lyrics are uninteresting and the band is 
average.  

But the crowd was overjoyed.  Say what you will about the lack of 
musicianship, but the woman sure does know how to work a crowd.  Fans 
around the the edge of the stage were treated to physical contact and the 
occasional kiss and hug.  The Mall Queen worked these poor rubes like a 
prostitute.  She knew exactly what her pathetic male fans wanted and she 
gave it out in spades.

Nostalgia, Celebrity and Thunt.net, powerful forces in todays consumer 
driven economy.  An aging Mall Queen in spandex pants on a sunny day.  She 
closed the show with I think were alone now, a song dwarfed by Weird 
Als brilliant I think Im a clone now.  She looped the chorus about 
twenty times but the crowd didnt care.  They sang along blindly like 
sheep frightened by the sun.  They followed their Mall Queen with blind 
faith, and every where the Mall Queen went, her lambs were sure to go.

data.headline: My Erotic Linus Torlvalds Dream

Tolvalds, in all of his splender and glory dancing in front of a terminal.

data.headline: In honor of the death of kings
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
In Honor of the death of kings
(the ides of march)


A lone man sits in his chair by the window watching the grass sway back 
and forth on the open praire.  The sky is a vibrant orange as the sun 
slowly sets.  As the sun fades behind the mountain the man pulled back the 
hammer on the shotgun and slowly brought it to his shoulder.  He looked 
again out the window longingly.  He sighed and pulled the trigger.

That was three weeks ago.  Three weeks ago that Lawerence Henry committed 
suicide.  What impacts did this have on the world?  The unfortnate answer: 
several.  Henry was a bit of a stock owner you see.  A bit as he owned 35% 
of IBM and 45% of Cisco Systems.  It goes without saying that the market 
took a little dump.  What do I mean by a little dump?  I mean when you 
wake up after a bender and find a dumptruck sized load of shit in your 
pants and wonder if you really had a good time or not.  Because of the 
market crash a record 352 people committed suicide last week.  Which we 
can only correctly state as a record because during the first week after 
his death there were only 125 and during the second week a mere 250.  352 
is quite a number when you make these kind of comparisions.  Sure, a mere 
727 people have left the earth because of this man's death.  Make that 728 
including the man himself, but I would like to think that this is a little 
extreme because one old man decides to kill himself.  I would like to 
think that people have a little more control than that.  I would like to 
think that we live in a world of sanity and not a world of insanity.  But 
what I like to think doesn't mean a god damned thing to anyone else.  Only 
to me and I suppose since you're reading this it means something to you.  
The world has gotten out of hand.  I know it's a lousy transition but 
you're reading my mind right now and sometimes I shift from train to train 
without telling you.  Like a Beatles song that ends abruptly because of a 
bad rip I reserve the right to go anywhere I want.  And I'll be sure to 
take you with me.  The world has gotten out of hand.  The power of the few 
has never meant so much to the many.  Buying and selling is all that 
remains of the culture.  Buying.  Selling.  Buying again.  You can't stop 
buying can you?  Well neither can I.  And how can I expect to stop?  With 
new things commign out every day?  New techologies?  New toys? New movies? 
New CDs?  I just gotta have them all and you do too.  I know you do.  In 
fact you're probably thinking about buying something right now, because 
that's the way our bankrupt culture works.  Got a girlfriend? You'd better 
buy her something if you want to keep her.  Got a wife?  Don't forget your 
anniversary or your ass is in the shitter.  I'm not kidding about this 
shit.  I can't tell you how many relationships ended because of the lack 
of roses or the size of someone's diamond.  It's not a metaphor I'm 
talking about the size of the rock here.  And no, you one testicaled 
freaks out there, I'm speaking about diamonds and nothing else.  

Lawerence Henry is dead and the economy has gone to shit.  But let's face 
it.  Things were pretty shitty before the economy crashed weren't they?  
You had just a good a reason to do it then as you did now right?  The 
working man is left with little power in this world, with the exception of 
his vote and the right to return products to Target.  And hell, they don't 
even count the votes anymore so what's the point.  The rich run it all, 
from Microsoft to the Presidency there was no aspect of this world not 
controlled by Lawerence or one of his fraternity brothers.  They all may 
not have nicknames and sweaters but in the end they all belong to the 
fraternity of inheritance and private propety.  Property being the kind 
usually ¼ the size of Wyoming, with armed guards ensuring that the sign 
reads correctly.  There will be no tresspassing.  Maybe all that matters 
is that the poor remain happy with their Beatles records and their race 
based inner class conflict.  I suppose there is some joy in that.

data.headline: Ryan Anne Dead
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
It is our sad duty to report the passing of long time supporter of 
Thunt.net, Ms. Ryan Anne Polli of Wilton, California.  

Most recently, Ryan broke the record for longest staff member at the State 
Hornet, serving six consecutive semesters as art director.  Her major 
accomplishments while at the paper included: redesign of the front page, 
winning of the prestigious pacemaker award and seduction of the editor-in-
chief.  Her successor, Andy Duong proudly stated that he "has accomplished 
two of her three major accomplishments and he hopes to be successful on 
the third before the semester comes to a close."  Ryan continued her 
passion for graphic design excellence or excellence in graphic design as 
she liked to say right up until her unfortunate fiery death.  Most 
recently she was involved in pimping her boyfriend's band Silent Horizon 
to the before mentioned State Hornet.  Ryan hoped that one day her 
boyfriend Matt Linder would become a Christian Rock Superstar (like Jesus) 
and dump her for a younger blonder taller thinner blacker bluer redder 
whiter blacker more religious (crucifix masturbating champion) exorcist-
proof woman.  Ryan hoped that her future would lead her to accomplishing 
her dream of spending her days drawing pictures of pretty pretty pretty 
pretty ponies, but alas this was not the case.

According to the Thunt.net Message Board, "Ryan was on her way home from 
church with her boyfriend, and she got in a car wreck."  She unfortunately 
passed away at 11:30 am, March 18, 2001 at the Bradshaw Veterinarian 
Clinic (which was the nearest trauma center to the scene of the 
accident).  According to Doctor Mephisto, treatment with animal 
tranquilizers on hand proved unsuccessful.  Doctor Mephisto later 
attempted to revive the patient by performing an emergency neutering, but 
alas, this too proved to be unsuccessful.

Former boss and one-time boyfriend David Sommers, when reached for comment 
said "Dammit, just when I thought we were on the road to hooking back up, 
she had to go and die on me, now I'll have to find someone else to stalk.  
But regardless, I'm incredibly pleased to see that when you hire an 
assassin from the Endeen Assassination Corporation (EAC) that it looks 
just like an accident.  I can honestly say that I have never been more 
satisfied from spending twenty bucks.  I'm so glad I finally found a use 
for the profits from my Campus Engine stock."  Ryan's one time friend 
Candice Luse was rather broken up over Ryan's passing and simply 
said "What a relief, maybe the dreams will stop now."  Sarah Sieber, long 
time friend and confidant said, "I will have exclusive photo rights for 
the funeral.  As usual, I'll be shooting for Thunt.net.  And now, I 
finally have a shot at Davey Sommers, I think he's sooooooo cute." 

After much investigation, we were able to reach Mr. Dusty Wolf (who spoke 
on the condition of anonymity) at a gay commune in the South Seas.  Mr. 
Wolf suggested that we call his commune, a sanctuary for alternative 
lifestyles, we told him to go fuck himself, which he promptly did.  Mr. 
Wolf seemed to have used a great deal of acid, but we interviewed him 
anyway.  Mr. Wolf, when asked about Ryan, had a lot to say.  "A lot of 
people know I had a thing for Ryan, but that was simply because she had a 
boy's name.  It was butch; it makes me horny just thinking about it.  I 
feel warm in my bathing suit area, like that time in the hot tub. Oh and 
David's couch, that was fun" said Mr. Wolf.

She is survived by her parents Stephen and Anne Polli and several horses.  
One horse named Blacky, when asked to comment said "More oats for me."  
Another horse however did not share his contented outlook; Mr. Clompy 
said, "It is a very sad day for horses, wulwululuwluwluwluwluw."

do it for the money.

Thunt.net: Why do women pluck their eyebrows and wear fake eyelashes?

Endeen: How the hell am I supposed to know?  Shit, I'm out of cigarettes.  
This interview is over.

Since our interview, there has been a campus-wide manhunt for Endeen.   So 
far, all that the efficient campus cops have discovered is three girls in 
yellow sundresses, a box of Krispy Kreme donuts and an empty pack of Camel 
Cigarettes.  Endeen is considered smoking and silly.  Do not attempt to 
apprehend him and if you do, keep your hands and arms away from his mouth.

data.headline: Ryan Anne not Dead?
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
In a shocking revelation today, Thunt.net reporters have learned that Ryan 
Anne Polli is not dead.

How do we know she's not dead?  Well, simply put, our lead reporter saw 
her at school.  He rushed up to her and pinched her on the arm.  She 
appeared to be real and by pinching her, he had ensured that she was not 
dreaming.  It was all true.  She was alive.  Of course, she could have 
been a robot, but that's another story.

Regardless of the robot possibilities, this leads to the moral of today's 
story: never believe anything you read on Thunt.net.  All of the articles 
of this past week have been the lies of a raving madman.  Endeen the 
assassin, Ryan drawing pictures of ponies, Sarah taking photos, Andy 
designing the paper: all lies.  What you are reading right now is a lie.  
Oh, shit.  Is this a paradox?  If I'm lying in a text that constantly 
lies, is that a truth?  

I know this has been a wild roller coaster ride for Ryan's friends, 
families and enemies.  There have been some factual mistakes here at 
Thunt.net and for this I apologize.  I would especially like to apologize 
for those (Matt's parents) who might have seen the crucifix-masturbating-
champion comment in the original lie declaring Ryan dead.  That was just 
uncalled for, really, what a horrible oversight that was.  The real 
crucifix-masturbating-champion was Linda Blair in the Exorcist, not Ryan.  
That was my mistake, and my fact checkers promptly informed me the next 
day, when it would have been pointless to repair the error.  Also Endeen's 
lawyers have informed me that he is busy getting cancer from Marlboro 
Lights not Camels, although the article didn't specifically say that he 
was smoking camels, if you read the text carefully, I would like to make 
sure that I don't botch any of Endeen's cigarette endorsement plans.  In 
addition, the Animal Rights activists have informed me that if the 
Veterinarian from the "Ryan Anne Dead" article attempted to neuter her, he 
would fail.  Ryan would have to be spayed.  Again, I apologize for all of 
the factual mistakes, cause I am really really sorry.

data.headline: 3 Brief film reviews and Frigidity
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: Good Day Thunt-keters. I'm awake, well barely anyway. We had our good ole ACM Game Night last night, or more correctly, last night deep into this morning. I felt lucky that I managed to escape at the reasonable time of 1:30 AM, seeing how last game night if I recall correctly I was there until 5 or 6 and felt like puking for the next two or three days. Not that I feel to great right now, but hell, I'm alive anyway. There weren't too many people there but we had some pretty good Counterstrike (Half-life Add-on) Games. I'm not as good as I used to be, but occasionally I come off with some good kills. It's now 12:10 pm here at the labs. The temperature is cold because the air conditioners are on full blast to protect the servers. My hands are freezing, but I type on regardless. It looked like it might rain again. The weather is eerie. Beginning of the week I'm wearing shorts and shades, end of the week back to jeans and sweatshirts. It's been a slow couple of weeks on Thunt.net since the Ryan's Death fandango. I have the strange feeling that people might actually be reading what I'm writing now and that kind of freaks me out. I mean if you look at the flashbacks of early Thunt.net, mainly I just blew off steam and typed randomly to try to combat the pressures of a scholarly college life. But now I feel that every time I type something it has to spark controversy or at the least stir up the idiots on the message board to write something more interesting than "STUPID: This board sucks ass" and "Homer: Bor-ing". Well, I'm not sure that's going to happen this time. Reasons for the slow week. Hell I could give you the standard answer that I had two midterms and a paper due last week, but that doesn't really matter now does it? I don't think so. "Sure, I did them and they're done" he said nonchalantly with senior-itus weighing heavily on his treble- free voice. Damn my hands are freezing. This typing isn't working Im going to have to cut open some of the computers and use the overheating processors for warmth. Other reasons: Boredom. Nothing much is happening I guess. The year is winding down. The semester is coming to a close. Movies watched recently (that I'd never seen before): Glory - I watched Glory for the first time after much ragging from my friends Matt and John, the latter being a true-blue civil war buff and re-enactor. It was a good war movie. I enjoyed it, despite it's morbid ending, which I understand was historically accurate. While I feel Ferris Bueler did a good job in his role, I still believe is was miscast because I just can't see him as anything other than Ferris. The movie was good, but it wasn't groundbreaking or anything. For groundbreaking, mind- blowing shit please see Pierrot le fou, 400 Blows and Soy Cuba. The English Patient - I try to avoid tradegy-prone chick flicks, but this movie was fucking rad. I feel fortunate that Matt had it in his DVD collection and I got to experience it in full widescreen with good sound. Truly amazing flick, well deserving of it's awards. Nice visuals, excellent pacing, the flashback structure works for once, and the bookends are gloriously cut together. (by bookends I mean that the story begins and ends with the same visuals, most movies and stories try to do this and very few succeed to the level that this film did.) To put this movie in line with other war movie type dramas, I'd say better than Saving Private Ryan, but not as impressive as The Thin Red Line (1998). The Doors - Why hadn't I seen this movie yet? I don't know. I suppose that having Val Klimer and Meg Ryan in it sounded like a lame bunch of answers, a cut up version of it was constantly on television (see other movies also constantly on: Ishtar, Chasers, Love Stinks, Short Circuit). Also I heard it was bad. But then I heard it was directed by Oliver Stone and I rushed to see it. Well I didn't have to rush or hear really, it just arrived in my Oliver Stone 10 pack DVD collection and I said, "hmmm, I guess he directed The Doors". I've always been a big fan of Stone's work and I can honestly say that I am now a big fan of his film The Doors. The visuals are stunning, Kilmer does an incredible job as Morrison, better than I ever believed possible for the Red Planet, Ghost in the Darkness, Top Gun Star. Meg Ryan was pretty good, very unlike the modern bland girl next door bullshit that she does in all of her recent films. You even get to see a flash of her tit during one of the many sex scenes in the film. Morrison and his life pretty much went rolling out of control and Stone's film puts you right in the action and after it all I felt pretty wasted despite the fact that I did not do any drugs during the 2 hour and 30 minute masterpiece. The sound on my Dolby Digital 5.1 DVD version was well worth the extra money invested in the disc and the surround sound system. This movie is rad, it's a total trip, just make sure you're ready for the ride. Other good rock movies: Stop Making Sense (Jonathon Demme's Talking Heads Live Concert Film), Gimme Shelter (Rolling Stones at Altamont, you can see the point where the peace and love stopped and the 70s began. They even run it in slow mo with Mick looking on and Sympathy for the Devil playing), and of course Spinal Tap (The best rocumentary of them all. Even if you didn't like Tap's early work, you'll love their film.) I think I've said enough. I don't know if anyone will read to this point of the article. If you do, please post your thoughts about the new slashdot-like weblog Thunt.net. By shifting the front page focus from articles to a mix of articles and net interest I feel I've added some interesting stuff to Thunt.net, what do you think? Post on the message board or email me at thunt@thunt.net.

data.headline: ASI: The Slates
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: Welcome to Thunt.net's ASI Election 2001 Coverage. We're going to need a catchy name for this coverage, so Ill be holding a contest on the message board. Please post any ideas you might have and maybe youll be the lucky winner. Current ideas: Who Gives a Shit? 2001 (WGS-2001), Who the fuck cares? 2001 (WFC-2001), or perhaps A Stupid Ignorant Frat Asshole Guy Succeeds in winning the presidency (ASI-FAGS). The latter being the current front-runner although I'd prefer a one-word title similar to Comedy Central's Peabody winning Indecision 2000. I'd like to begin my coverage by getting you up to date on the slates. There seem to be three slates running for election this year. To inform anyone who is unfamiliar with the basic idea of slates, they assume that the voters are uninformed idiots and will say hmmmmm, Jason Davis is a good candidate, so after I vote for him, I'll vote for all of his frat buddies on the Fuck Students First slate. It's a moronic idea, but around this school what do you expect. The slates are also a neat way to mask campaign spending, and funnel illegal (mommy & daddies) money into your campaign. Support US  A stupid name you say? We have just begun our election coverage. Expect more stupidity from the rag-tag bunch of morons running for office this year. The Support US slate can clearly be identified as the slate of weirdoes, freaks and ASI outsiders. Led by Hans something or other, they hope to capture the freak vote and turn the campus into a freak paradise. Don't expect them to win though, despite the large population of freaks and weirdoes on this campus, the honest truth is freaks dont vote. The people who vote are the fraternities and sororities because they herd their members to the voting booths like lambs to the slaughter (for similar policies see the religious right, note how many polling places are churches and how few are at chemistry labs). I expect these tattooed and pierced freaks to go back down from where they came from and never be heard from again. However to remain impartial and honest to you my viewing public, I would like to state for the record that I know two of these freaks: Ryan Murphy who is running for Director of Business Administration and Alin Wakefield who is running for the Union Board of Directors. Despite my familiarity with these candidates, I do support and hope that they win their selective offices. Vision - As my good friend Brendan mentioned in his column a few weeks ago Vision is the party of Big Brother. Their signs all feature a hideously frightening V embedded in an all knowing all seeing eye. Frankly Im terrified that some idiot thought that these signs and slogans was a good idea. I know no one reads books anymore but there was a fairly popular novel written by George Orwell named 1984, in which Big Brother controlled the common stupid people and watched their every move using viewscreens and a complex system of thought police. The novel also featured Victory brand cigarettes, among other victory brand products. The V in victory is prominently featured in the movie by the same name, an excellent adaptation, although typically the book remains superior. The idiots who selected this logo should not be allowed to run a cruise ship let alone this campus. Their slate consists of mainly non- frat infidels who have been serving in ASI due to the fluke election of Arturo Pimmentale last semester. Again, without the frat vote this slate will sink, and with this stupid vision shit I hope they never rise again. DOWN WITH BIG BROTHER. DOWN WITH BIG BROTHER. DOWN WITH BIG BROTHER. DOWN WITH BIG BROTHER. DOWN WITH BIG BROTHER. DOWN WITH BIG BROTHER. DOWN WITH BIG BROTHER. Students First - The last slate to enter the campaign, and the first in lame tired slogans is the Students First slate. This is the favorite, and as I always bet on the biggest evil, I have to say that we at Thunt.net expect a near sweep from this hackneyed campaign. Ably helmed by former ASI President Jason Bryant and BMW owner (thank you mommy and daddy) this slate is filled with ASI insiders and should be able to monopolize the all important fraternity and sorority vote. Highlights of this slate include the Gladiator-esque Rugby Player Joe Devlin running for President and the Politician's Politician David Sommers running for Executive Vice President. As with the Support US slate, expect Thunt.net to support Mr. Sommers in his quest for glory, but again don't expect the satire to be light. Just because he used to be Editor-In-Chief of the State Whore doesnt mean that Thunt.net will relax our coverage of him. Although our rumor-hounds at the Whore have informed us that the Whore will be supporting him without any disclaimer expressing their obvious bias. Expect morals and ethics here on Thunt.net. Sommers has aligned himself with the frats and sorority fiends and for this he will have his victory, but Thunt.net will have its say. This is now the end of the first part of Thunt.net's election coverage: The Slates. Please note that here at Thunt.net we are not interested in fact checking or politically correctness. The candidates are public figures by their desire for office and hence leave themselves open to satire as stated by the Supreme Court Decision: Hustler Magazine, Inc. v. Falwell, 485 U.S. 46, 108 S.Ct. 876, 99 L.Ed.2d. 41 (1988). In which a unanimous Supreme Court held that satire has played a prominent role in public and political debate and that the Court could see no standard to distinguish among types of parodies that would not harm public discourse, which would be poorer without such satire. Thanks for reading!

data.headline: The overthrow of the Whore
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: I would like to begin by saying that from what I hear there is a highly skilled group of individuals and Andy running for Editor-in-chief this year. I thought I would take some time out from my exciting ASI Election coverage to identify some of the brave fools who desire to become the Editor-in-chief of the State Whore. Tony - Tony is a great guy and I think he would do a swell job as EIC. Tony has a great deal of experience and is really a hard worker. I believe the paper would be in capable hands with Tony at the helm. Brendan - Brendan cannot write 500 words on a single subject in his column but he has a great sense of humor and actually believes that he is funny. Brendan has some good ideas for the paper and I think he would make a great EIC. Andrea - Damn Andrea is hot. And she seems frightfully intelligent too. Although I think Brendan and Tony have to be the favorites in this election, I think that Andrea has a good outside chance of becoming EIC. Although I do not feel that being Copy Editor correlates much to being EIC, I think Andrea could wow the board at the interview and achieve a surprise victory. Andy - Andy is running for EIC. Yes the Andy that spells his name ANDY! And put fart, poop and dick jokes in the newspaper. Andy is a brilliant designer but I think he lacks the restraint to safely (lawsuit and protest free) manage the paper. Although I did help him write his cover letter, so I am sure they will give him an interview but I cannot see the board deciding on Andy. It would be awesome if he won, but as a wise gambler, that's not where I'd put my money. For those who are interested in setting up a pool, or laying a wager on the race, I've set up an odds projection: Odds - Tony 1:2 - Brendan 1:2 - Andrea 1:5 - Andy 1:12

data.headline: ASI: The Dirt
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: Ok, since this is Thunt.net, not the New York Times, we allow ourselves from time to time to print rumors. This is one of those times... Sommer's flight from Support US - Thunt.net sources tell us that originally Sommers was working for the Support US? slate as their campaign manager. Well, apparently Mr. Sommers felt that they weren't allowing him to do as much as he wanted to, so he jumped ship and took two or three of their candidates with him. Sommers is now running for VP of University Affairs on the "StudentsFirst?" slate, starring Joe Devlin. Obviously bad news for the pierced freak Hans because with Jason "BMW" Bryant, Joe "Gladiator" Devlin and David "hero of the shield law" Sommers working together, they form an unholy alliance that will either bring on the apocalypse or support the flaming death and destruction of the Support US? campaign. Jason Bryant is a Choad - Readers of the ever popular Thunt.net message board know full well that someone claiming to be Jason Bryant posted to the board (Apr 18, 2001 at 12:32 am). A second party named "Jason Bryant's a fucking idiot" replied the next day at 11:23 am. I have two questions about these interesting posts. First question, is this really Jason Bryant posting? Let's examine the message. It is campy, lame, mildly complimentary to Thunt.net and ends with a campaign announcement, so from what we know about Jason Bryant, that's all up to code. But there is a minor discrepancy. What would Jason Bryant be doing up at 12:30 at night reading the Thunt.net message board? While I do admit that from time to time I myself keep a strange and depraved sleeping schedule that few normal people will ever try and few will ever comprehend (going to bed at 4am and sleeping until noon). But is Bryant a freak of similar colors or are we merely dealing with a well-disguised imposter? A difficult question for a difficult age. Second question, is Jason Bryant a Choad? If he's posting to the message board at 12:30 am, that proves that he has less of a life than we all thought and those two girls getting out of his beamer with him at Albertson's must have been hired hands, and not hired to do the kind of work you'd like those girls to do, ho ho. But having no life is different than being a choad (props go to the poster for choosing such an excellent and indeed rare word to insult our current ASI President. I myself haven't seen the word choad used since I saw Trey Parker's Orgazmo in the no defunct Landmark/Sacramento Inn theater a few years ago.) Regardless of the rarity and brilliance of the insult, we are still left with the question: Bryant? Choad? I for one say yes. Bryant has handled the presidency poorly since he was elected and they threw him in the fountain election night. I've heard nothing but horror stories about his strange and deluded administration since that fateful night. I heard he tried to bribe and coerce the State Whore into doing his bidding and giving him positive press coverage, but failed due to the impeccable integrity of the News Editor who refused to do her vicious handiwork. Additionally the whole Child Care scandal reeks of choadadom. Closing the Child Center to balance the budget, putting the little children out on the street without even a Where's Waldo book to keep them company. Atrocious! Not to say that I haven't had the same idea myself about a hundred times, seeing as how I have no children to send to that useless Child Center and I think it's a god damned waste of student tax payers money. But I especially abhor Bryant's actions because he went at the glorious project like a pussy. He cried to anyone that would listen, "Boo hooo, mismanagement of ASI's millions is going to force us to cut the child center", "think about the children" and other such political nonsense. Well, the campus (the whiney people with children, mainly) united against Bryant's plan and somehow they saved the Child Center, because every day when I walk by there I expect it will have been destroyed with a wrecking ball to add more parking to our already impacted campus, but every day there are little children running and playing totally oblivious of Jason Bryant, the great destroyer's vicious designs on their playground space. Jason Bryant is indeed a Choad and no doubt he'll go far.

data.headline: Shock and surprise in EIC Selection
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Shock and surprise fill the air today in the wake of the Hornet 
Elections.  The Hornet has chosen a "surprise" candidate (meaning someone 
who I don't know personally).  Her name is Layla Boom or something like 
that.  Apparently she assisted cult leader Matt Wagar and his News Section 
in this last year and based upon a stellar interview and her journalism 
classes with Mike "married to Silvia, pity me" Fitzgerald has led her to 
victory.  

The shock has been easy to take for those of us who don't care, but some 
people had their entire future riding on this election and for them, 
defeat is hard to take.  Wonnacott had to cancel his victory party and 
celebrate a hideous trouncing.  anDy! took to bed at the hour of eight o 
clock after having to wake up early at nine am for the interview.  Tony no 
doubt smashed some shit, complained loudly to the elder Fitz and is 
probably going to quit the paper and work at the Sacbee, not a bad 
decision if you ask me.  Andrea probably took the decision with a grain of 
salt and felt no hatred or envy for the winner.  Dina no doubt hopes that 
this new EIC will distract people from making fun of her and her Louis-
Philippe/Charles X-like reign of foolishness and idiocy but its not going 
to happen.  Like Chernobyl and Three-Mile-Island, when you make a mistake 
this big, it takes years for the radiation to dissipate.  

As to the new EIC, we wish her the best of luck and hope that someday 
students will be able to read the State Hornet without being embarrassed 
by the poor quality of articles and lack of journalistic integrity.  We at 
Thunt.net hope for that day, but again we don't really care.  Graduation 
looms on the horizon for our brave hero and thoughts turn to the world 
after our first college degree.  What level of insane debauchery will we 
be involved in this time next year, I don't know, but that miserable 
excuse of a paper will not be on our mind.

As to the losers, I give my most heartfelt condolences.  In every race, 
there has to be a winner and many losers, but what's important is that you 
tried.  What's important is that the EIC from Santa Cruz lost as well.  
What's important is that George-ogre also failed.  With losers like that 
you're in good company, company to be enjoyed and mocked.

data.headline: Hornet EIC Selection: ALL FUCKED UP!
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
The fix is in.  Or it was in, again.  I swear, this pub-board thing is one 
damned well organized group of scam artists.  First, they fuck over 
Smoking Boy last year, and now they fuck over Brendan.  Now I want you to 
keep in mind that this is only rumor, but here is the scoop:  

Mr. J. Nature, Mel Gibson, Smoking Boy, News Man and some other people are 
united in their support for Brendan Wonnacott.  That means he should be 
celebrating right now.  But wait, Michael J. Fox and Dinaaaa decide that 
he's not the man for the job and somehow lead a charge that tips the pub 
board and they decide that Brendan, who is best for the job would be best 
as Managing Editor and Layla Bomb should be Editor-in-Thief. 

I would just like to ask you all to reflect on this and think about how

data.headline: David defeats Goliath or The Goliath defeats David
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story: As I walked along the campus, this bright sunny day, I know that what I read on the message board was true. David Sommers and his Students First slate were brutally defeated by the grass roots donut-pushing Vision slate. The ASI-tocracy of Jason Bryant has been defeated by the upstart vision-ers led by Arturo Pimentale. How was I able to confirm this? It was simple, all over campus the Students First Billboards are still up while the Vision and even the SupportUS signs have been taken down. These billboards remain standing like corpses littering the field after a mighty battle. It is clear that Students First has lost because there is no one left alive to bury their dead. The victors of a war always bury their own first, leaving their enemies to rot on the field and be picked apart by carrion. Why did Students First finish last? Blame could easily drift right to the top. Perhaps Sommers should have stayed with the pierced freaks of SupportUS and they might have triumphed over the Orwellian Vision. Perhaps the lack of donuts, fancy fliers and a website brought down the golden dreams of Students First. But once should not place all of the blame on the David the Brave. Other members of the slate played a share in this colossal defeat. Perhaps it is the fault of Champagne from Omega Chi Alpha for not placing candid (yes candid) photos of herself on the Students First signs and fliers. Maybe all they would have had to do was change her name to Champagne "I'm Hot!" Turismo. Nah, it probably would have taken more than that. Sometimes it seems like they were doomed from the start. Perhaps David Sommers is just a bad luck charm. This is no doubt a sad time for the perennially unlucky would-be dictator Chairman Sommers. He's just lost a major election and from what I can tell, his slate will have little or no power in the new Government (now with Vision[tm]). Additionally his chosen one for EIC: Mr. WannaCock? was hideously robbed by the nonentity Ms. Bomb. What's next for David Sommers? What could go wrong now? We here at Thunt.net have fired up the old BS-2000 and we are about to take you on an action packed tour through: The Future of Daaaaaviiiiiddddd Sommmmmmerssssssss Drunken Disaster - In a drunken post-defeat stupor, David discovers that he has slept with a MAN! For the record, it was a man dressed as a woman, but the event contributed in no small way to the everlasting snowball effect that sent his political career spiraling out of control. The matter is made worse when the man-woman who calls himself Dino went on television in a dirty sweatshirt and told Fox40 news that her and Sommers had a night of "sexual passion" and that it should be considered "news". Back to the Hornet! - Sommers returns to the Hornet as a lowly news reporter. He is assigned to write the Police Log, a degrading job that doesn't even require writing, you simply have to copy down the information from the log at police headquarters. Seeing it as a stupid and pointless duty that a trained monkey could do, Sommers began to photocopy the police log and place it in the paper verbatim. His editor liked the photocopies for their edgy real-life quality and nominated for EIC again. Unfortunately the trained monkey the trained monkey he hired to do all the photocopying ran against him and won. The monkey was supported by two powerful idiots: Teen Wolf and Teen Wolf Too. The BS-2000 is starting to fail as class draws neigh. Our Nobel-prize winning hero Thunt must now rush to class, but fear not gentle souls, Thunt will return soon with hard-hitting satire and a sense of humor that is borderline: sick, perverse and twisted.
