data.headline: fuck school
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
I really don't give a flying fuck about school anymore. I went to my ethnics studies class today. What a grand waste of an hour and fifteen minutes of my time. Later English and anthro will repeat the horrifying experience. Why even bother. Who fucking cares. I have seven things left to do and none of them rely on class, with the exception of those fucks who take attendance, which seems to be every fucking teacher I've got, I don't see any god damned reason for me to attend anymore. Every minute, every seconds my thoughts drift back to the road, to freedom, to sweet oblivion. To a time when I'll sleep as much as I like. When I won't have to get dressed and rush to school to be on time for some bullshit class.

Enough of this madness. I'm middle class enough. I read books and I write pretty well. Isn't that enough for these mad fuckers. Won't they just leave me alone and let me cruise out these last two weeks in an animal house fashion of constant toga parties and mad fat drunken orgies. Enough of this false learning. The seeds have been planted and I'm ready to be let loose upon the world. I've got ideas, I've got goals, plans and books to read. Enough forced lectures, I'll attend the lectures I want to hear. Enough forced learning, from now on I'll learn what I want to know. If I'm not interested in your fucking recitation of the problems in your school district, I'll fucking walk out. And you won't give a fuck either because you only want to talk to people who support your backwards ass cause, and clearly I'm not one of them.

Enough of your nonsense. Enough of your ASI elections. Enough of your Vision and your management opportunities at Krispy Kreme paying a cool 30,000 a year. I'm not interested anymore and I don't think I ever was. You can have your society with your shiny cars and big houses. Just give me the road and a library card. Your bullshit will never reach me anymore.

data.headline: a dirt path
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
There's a dirt path by the biology building and the math/history 
building.  The landscape engineers (gardeners) at Sac State decided that 
dirt paths were ugly, so they roped off the area and planted grass seed.  
The various chickens that live on campus pecked the ground and ate the 
grass seed.  When the gardeners removed the ropes, the students began 
walking on the path again and the baby grass was soon pounded into the 
earth.  The gardeners wonder why the students don't just walk around the 
corner to get to where they're going and the students can't figure out why 
the gardeners are trying to stop them from walking in a straight line 
between two points, which is after all, the fastest way to get somewhere.  
Perhaps there is a greater theme at work here.  The gardeners are the type 
of people who never take shortcuts and always keep off the grass.  While 
the students constantly take shortcuts in whatever they do.  They skim 
books instead of reading them.  They cut classes.  They cheat on tests.  
They do their essays the night before they are due.  The mentality of 
college students and the mentality of gardeners are profoundly different.  
No doubt, the war over this dirt path will continue by the biology and the 
math/history building.

data.headline: Five proffessors worth taking at CSUS
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Brian Roberts  History  Roberts is a hard-nosed history professor who is willing to curse in class. He has been published and is one of the few teachers Ive ever had who Ive thought might be smarter than me. Roberts teaches history in a way that few others do and helps it to be interesting and exciting. I cant describe the way that Roberts teaches. I can only say how incredible it is. Unfortunately this recommendation comes a little late, as Roberts is leaving to teach in Iowa or some god- awful Midwestern state. Its a major loss for Sac State and the History Department must be filled with fools to let him go. Hes phenomenal.

Jerry L. Tobey  History  Tobey only returns to campus in the fall to teach one course: World War II. And with good reason. Tobey is an incredibly brilliant World War II scholar. When you ask him a detailed hypothetical question he knows the answer. Tobey is the kind of teacher when you ask for statistics or more information he rarely has to look it up and get back to you. His lectures are perfect and his lecturing style is calm, cool and calculated. His tests are organized and fair. This was one of the few classes where I actually read all of the text books and enjoyed it. The books he assigned us were brilliantly written. Books such as the Pulitzer prize winning Freedom from Fear by David Kennedy, its a tome, but its amazingly well written. Also we read How the allies won by Richard Avery and The Second World War by John Keegan. Brilliant authors, brilliant works, brilliant teacher. I cannot recommend Tobey enough.

Thomas Strasser  History/Humanities  Strasser takes you on an Odyssey through the Iliad. A book that seems boring at first but comes alive as he forces the class to read aloud. As a fairly decent reader, I enjoyed this class immensely. I learned all about classical Greek and roman mythology. Strasser is also a big fan of Woody Allen and you get to write a paper on Mighty Aphrodite. An excellent film, that fits perfectly into the subject of mythology. He also equated Medea to Pulp Fiction as in both, you root for the bad guys. He reminds me of Indiana Jones, hes actually been to Greece and Rome on digs, and he speaks Greek. An incredible teacher, I dont know what the fuck hes doing at Sac State. (Do not confuse him with Elizabeth Strasser in Anthropology. Shes not a bad teacher, but she is a little German troll.)

Edward Bradley  Math  I hate math. But his British accent and sensible teaching style helped me survive this class. I recall most what he said about division by zero, If youre dividing by zero he would say in his strong British accent, youve got real problems. It was classic, and it helped me through.

Chancey Ridley  English  The man is a bit whacked, but he picked his readings well and they proved his point: You must read the beginning, the middle, and the end of literature. The man is a devout structuralist, and by using Walden, Legend of Sleepy Hollow and even The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn he proves his point over and over again. Ridley gets on the list because he plans his classes well and when there is nothing left to discuss he lets us go early and even cancels class. The students respond to this by doing their work and gain their rewards.

data.headline: Oh well, whatever, nevermind.
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Welcome to the end, the end of April in the spring of this foul year of our lord, two thousand and one. It is a special time, a time of graduation. There's something about this time, perhaps it's the light quality of the air or the fact that my college career is coming to an end, but for some reason I don't care about school anymore. I simply attend my classes to pass the time between now and May 26th, and I'm not the only one.

I often ask myself, "Why do I even bother attending class?" The answer is brutally simple: most of my professors have that annoying and degrading habit of taking roll. Of course, they can never seem to tell the difference between physical attendance and mental attendance. Although some of my professors are beginning to feel the shift. The few who understand the dangerous mentality that is starting to form in their students are attempting to combat it with strategies that are reminiscent of lyrics from the classic Nirvana teen-anthem: "with the lights out, it's less dangerous, here we are now, entertain us." Unfortunately, my professors lack the captivating power of the now-defunct Seattle based grunge band. My Anthropology professor has been begging us to read the assigned text for the last two weeks. So far, her pleas have fallen upon deaf ears. My English professor took a different approach and asked us to drink coffee before class, in an attempt to improve class participation. Alas, there was little change the next week. Both my History and Ethnics Studies professors have begun to augment their lectures with videos and slides. However, these valiant attempts have the major downside of turning the lights out, quickly leaving the truly apathetic among us a chance to catch up on lost sleep. Of course, these days sleep isn't lost due to studying or frantic paper writing, it's lost due to video games, movies and other such distractions.

Why are we so distracted? What has stolen our passion for intellectualism? Simply put, Graduation. It looms over our heads like the caps and gowns we were forced to purchase from ASI at 30 bucks a pop. The real world has us now. Most of us will soon be shackled in jobs that we will hold for the rest of our lives. A few will again dodge the real world and run to the convents of Masters Degrees and Law Schools, but the sanctuary they seek will only be temporary, the real world will still be waiting for these cowards. A few of us are going to try to refuse the whole system entirely. For us rare breeds the future bears real promise and perhaps bitter fruit. We are the Peter Pans of this world and we still hope to accomplish the two-fold feat of not growing up and accomplishing our dreams. Dreams are strange things, when you're deep in the shit that is college life; it's hard to have any dreams except getting out. Your life becomes a day-to-day prison of papers and reading. Monotony in our learning system sets in almost immediately as you stop reading fiction and stick to the hard liquor of textbooks and monographs. Fresh ideas are quickly discarded in an attempt to match the views of our professors, in pursuit of our new gods: grades. With these new gods, your dreams are soon left dormant as you dig in for four years of daily torture. Sure, it beats working, don't get me wrong, but I didn't find the experience too enlightening either. We attend a middle class conformity factory. You come in as freaks and weirdoes and you leave as elite members of the 24% of Americans who have attended college. You're a perfect candidate for employment and employment is what you'll get. Perhaps deployment is a better term, as you are deployed into the real world like an interchangeable part into Ford's assembly line. Remember the lesson learned by factory workers in Ford's factories: that interchangeable parts are easily replaceable.

Graduation looms close on the horizon. Laziness has taken hold and it is unlikely that while on this journey to the nirvana of perpetual employment will ever break free of this fear. The fear of the real world is the fear that drives our apathy. This month could be our last chance to goof off and not do our work without fear of being fired. But this is how it has always been, and always will, until the end.

data.headline: Six pairs of Tities and a Fire
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Alright. Now dig this, baby.

You don't care for me, I don't care about that.
You got a new fool ha! I like it like that.
I have only one a burnin' desire.
Let me stand next to your fire,

What a night. I got about four hours sleep and I don't mind too much. Let me set the scene for you.

It's 2:25 in the morning. I've been sleeping for about four hours now. I hear someone outside screaming "Thomas!". I assume it's Andy who has woken Matt and I up for such exciting events as: "Andy Arrives Home", "Andy and Andrea come home from a dance" and "I'm Andy! I'm Drunk!". He bangs on the door, he rings the doorbell repeatedly, but at 2:25 in the morning and knowing his track record of idiocy, Im content to ride it out. Finally at 2:30 he stops. "Hurrah!", I said, "I won't have to kill Andy today".

You know that it would be untrue
You know that I would be a liar
If I was to say to you
Girl, we couldn't get much higher

Then he starts again and this time he yells out "There's a fire next door". This deserves real punishment I think, joking about a fire to wake us up is really low, even for Andy. So I get out of bed, put my robe on and begin thinking about how I'm going to grab Andy by the throat and punch him in the head until he passes out in a bloody heap. Then I think I'll tie him to a tree and hang him upside down until morning so he can understand the difference between friends and enemies.

The time to hesitate is through
No time to wallow in the mire
Try now we can only lose
And our love become a funeral pyre

As I rush to the door, I notice that Matt is already up ahead of me and I rush out the open front door. I look out across the street and see a huge orange fireball, "Holy Shit!" I say, Andy wasn't lying. Now that's a sentence you don't hear very often, and thus deserves repeating, Andy wasn't lying. By this time, I was still looking around for Andy, but he was nowhere to be found because it wasn't Andy yelling at us, ringing our doorbell, banging on the door and throwing pennies at our windows, it was Andy's roommate Joe. Suddenly the entire story shifted, because Joe, unlike Andy, knows when you should wake up your roommates and a huge fire across the street is an excellent reason.

That's what I'm talkin' about
Now dig this. Ha! Now listen baby!

You try to give me your money. You better save it, babe.
Come on baby, light my fire
Save it for your rainy day.
Come on baby, light my fire
I have only one a burnin' desire.
Try to set the night on fire, yeah
Let me stand next to your fire,

The entire roof of the apartment complex across the street was engulfed with the fire, there was also a great deal of sparking and noise like the fire had hit a fireworks or ammunition stash, but closer examination showed that it was an electrical fire, with blue sparks popping and flashing from the corner of the roof. The entire neighborhood was out on the streets watching this thing. The fire department was there in force, about 3 or 4 fire engines, a paramedics van, a chiefs car and much much more. After about five minutes of shock, I ran upstairs to get my video camera and Matt's digital camera and we went to work. Unfortunately when I got back outside, the Fire Department had taken care of most of the fire, but we still got some excellent photos by Matt, available here: thunt.net/photo/fire. The video of the fire will be up in a few days.

Ah, move over, Rover. And let Jimi take over.
Yeah, you know what I'm talkin' about.
Yeah. Get on with it, baby

What about the Six pairs of Tities? Well lets just say my mildly wild night of Fire wasn't the only thing happening that evening. The six pairs of tities are a whole different story all together. A story that must be told by Joe Rodriguez, in video form. So, stay tuned to Thunt.net, as we explore: Six pairs of Tities and a fire.

data.headline: Swimming in Boredom in Vacaville
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Good evening thunt.neters. I just wanted to drop a brief article in and let you guys know what Im up to. A lot of people have been asking, whats going to happen to Thunt.net, now that you wont have the hornet to make fun of? The answer is a simple one. Thunt.net has never been just about making fun of the hornet. If you browse the archives, especially the flashbacks section (pre-thunt.net database), youll learn that Thunt.net has been about making fun of lots of other things besides the hornet.

Now, on to what Ive been up to. Ive been hanging out at my parents house for a while. Living in Vacaville is pretty damn lame. Most of my friends live in Sacramento or Davis, and now Im stuck in Vacaville, so needless to say I havent been too social lately. Most of my time is spent reading, watching movies and of course swimming.

Also, Ive been watching a great deal of sports lately. My father and brother are interested in the Stanley Cup finals, which Ive been following with minor interest. But what Ive found more interesting than hockey is basketball. Ive always been a bit of a closet Laker fan. I was a fan back in the eighties when Kareem Abdul Jabar, Magic Johnson, James Worthy, etc, played. I decided to stop watching basketball the next year when they said they were going to introduce expansion teams. Ive never liked expansion teams and Ive never looked back. But these new Lakers, Shaq and Kobe are incredible. I tuned in to the finals to watch domination. I wanted to see the amazing power of Shaq and Kobe that I had seen in the Kings series, where I won a handy amount betting against the home team, and the brilliant work that I had seen against the Spurs when the Lakers were down by twenty points at the half and came back to win as if they were playing a friendly game in the park.

Basketball itself as a sport is a mild interest to me. Several aspects of the game intregiue me. The fact that it is constant warfare, point to point is extremely interesting. The quickness of the game is like true warfare, where battles do not stop for commercial breaks. I enjoy the fact that the players play both ways, the same men having to be gifted at both defense and offense. It is still rather dull to watch sometimes, but the brilliance of the Shaq/Kobe combination makes the game seem like a well rehearsed ballet.

I just saw a commercial for the French Open and it seemed to be a lot more about Jennifer Capriatis nipples than tennis. But maybe that was just me. Of course I do know who Anna Kornocova is, despite my lack of interest in that sport.

I digress. I was shocked and still shocked to see the Lakers crumble and loose to the Sixers in the first game of the series. I am even more shocked to watch this second game as the Lakers are blowing their twelve point lead with Shaq in danger of fouling out.

One thing I really dont like about basketball is the insistence of fouling as strategy. Right now, both of the teams are past the foul limit, and the game seems to hinge on Shaq fouling out as yesterday it hinged on Motombo fouling out. It just seems un-sportsmanlike.

The two series have been interested. The Colorado Avalanche should have steamrolled the New Jersey Devils, as they did last night, 4-0. But they have crumbled and cracked, some even laying blame on the goalie of all goalies, Patrick Roy. The Lakers seem to be on the way to doing the same thing. Rumored to be considering a sweep of the Finals, as they swept the rest of the playoffs this year, the Lakers are off to a incredibly slow start in this series. Certainly the Lakers dropping the early games and coming back with a vengeance will make for an interesting series, but as a minor Lakers fan Id just rather seem them beat the hell of Iverson and his Sixers.

Another neat thing about the two series is that they both have fancy cameras that they are trying out to mimic the Super Bowls matrix-esque camera. Hockey has Panasonic scan vision, allowing them to make the entire rink matrixey and rotate around the puck when needed. They also have a really neat goalie cam, which allows a clear view of where the puck entered or where the goalie was successful. Basketball also has a neat camera mounted on top of the basket that seems to be a wide angle lens, allowing the announcers to pause the camera and scan back and forth, monitoring where every player is and where the play broke down or succeeded.

These reality TV shows are really pissing me off and they arent even on yet. Fear Factor, SpyTV, etc. The rumor I heard is that they were planned to keep the TV schedule interesting during the writers strike, but unfortunately the strike was avoided and now these network slime balls are making the public suffer them regardless.

I think Ive ranted my rant for today. Ill be uploading this update using a blisteringly slow 56k dialup connection. Who am I kidding saying 56k, Im more likely to connect at 28.8 or 33.3. Well, I hope youre all enjoying your summer as much as Im enjoying my fun in Vacaville.

fyi: the Lakers did hold on and win Game 2, 98-89. But dont kid yourself, it was a hell of a lot closer than it looks. What a great series of Basketball its turned out to be.

data.headline: Moulin Rouge
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
I went in to hate this movie. I wasnt really sure what it was about from the confusing trailers. I assumed it was a period piece about burlesque houses and sex, and that it was rated PG-13, never a good sign for a film about sex. I read Matts review of it on ATP and while he gave it an incredibly favorly view, I assumed he was smoking crack. Like I said, I went to hate. Unfortunately I couldnt. Frankly speaking, they had me from the first b&w shot of Paris where Lehrman began panning through the streets maintaining his b&w crummy looking film, but performing visual tricks we know to be only of the digital age. That was all it took.

The film could have been a major failure. The idea of the man writing the story as he remembers it happening is as old as Murder she wrote, and older. But under Lehrmans brilliant directing, the film shines as every shot is hand crafted, every cut is well timed, and the music, well the music deserves an entire paragraph to itself, and I will see to that.

The music is incredible. Basically the film uses the 1900s Paris Burlesque house as a theater where they do for popular music, what Shakespeare in Love did for obscure lines from Shakespeare. Im not going to reveal any of the songs mixed into the medleys, because I believe that would ruin the pure joy and surprise of hearing them start talking what sounds like lyrics, but the obscurity is so that you dont recognize them. Then they break into the chorus and your heart leaps out as you realize the brillance at which the song has been mixed into the film.

The movie is like the Rocky Horror Picture show because you want to sing and dance along with all of the songs, but unlike Rocky, this movie is actually good.

Fuck good, this movie is great. I plan to see it a few more times in the theater. And when it gets to the cheap theater, I might go every day for a while, or just sit there all day and watch it a few more times. Also, I plan to get the soundtrack, post haste (using mp3s of course).

Problems with the film: Yes there are some. But they are minor. Nicole Kidmans nipples slip out of her dress a couple times during the film. No wait. Thats not a problem. There is a long scene that reminds me of Yoda acting, but then perhaps thats because Ewan McGregor is in the film and he was also in Episode I (btw: this film is ten times the film that Episode I was, because Lehrman knows where to put the camera and doesnt have a wookie up his butt, like some directors). Also whenever Lehrman zoomed in on a building, and would then disolve into the inside, were clear homages to Orson Welles classic Citizen Kane. The film is cut a little fast, but unlike other former-music video directors, Lehrman knows when to slow down the cutting and tell a story.

Moulin Rouge is an incredible film. Drop everything you are doing and go see it. Right now. Dont check the message board, go see the film. Go now! Go! Im going to stop typing now, because you should already be on your way to the theater.

data.headline: Portland Bound
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Tuesday June 26th our hero will depart on a most epic voyage. An airplane (Well, it's a big pretty white plane with a red stripe, curtains at the windows, wheels, and it just looks like a big Tylenol!) will carry our hero to Portland, Oregon. Land of trees, rain and Phil. Our hero will be visiting Phillip Holmstrand in the beautifully designed, well-planned metropolis.

Portland is a fantastic city, home to the world's biggest tree, the world's biggest logging company, and the world's biggest paper mill. The girls of Portland are known for their buck-teeth and strange desire for extra strength toothpicks. Most inhabitants of the City of Portland spend their afternoons playing Quake III on the internet while watching Blade Runner on DVD. They live by the river, which is so polluted it is a nationally recognized superfund site (way to go Portland, New Jersey won't know what hit 'em). Portland is also home to the Corporate GIS project, or Graphical Information Services. GIS is not just a neat way of saying JIZZ, it's also the first step towards the American Dream: The totalitarian police state. Yes, that's right, with accurate mapping tied into a satellite monitoring system you will be able to stop even Will Smith of Enemy of the State, or maybe even Warren Beatty in The Parallax View. But there's one thing it won't stop and that's wheelchair-bound superman Christopher Reeves. Only kryptonite and horseys (drawn by Polli) can stop that elderly rear-windowed Superman. But perhaps a short reading from Nietzsche's Man and Superman would stop him.

Yes, Portland is a fantastic city, and I look forward to visiting it again. Which means I'll have to cancel my plans to sit on my ass and watch MTV on my 3D screen while jacking off, I mean drawing. No, wait, those aren't my plans. Those are someone else's plans.

In other news: went to the Wagar-discovered classic Cornerstone Restaurant today. Fantastic as always. Even discovered to my joy they have a "Beat Lit" section in the groovy bookstore next door. Having recently listened to On the Road by Kerouac, read Junky and Queer by Burroughs, I'm starting to really get a feel for this beat lit and I like it. Oh Yeah. Potato platter, can't beat that, only 6.95! For a limited time only. Don't smoke crack.

Peace out.

data.headline: The truth about Capitalism
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Having just watched Dennis Miller make an ass out of himself, saying that we should drill in Alaska so he can keep his stupid fucking SUV, I thought I would try to speak some truth for a while. Cant say Ive heard any truth anywhere lately. So, here goes.

What people have to understand is that business does not care about them. If a business can make a profit selling a product that kills people, they will (tobacco). If a business can make a product selling a product that destroys peoples brain cells and causes car accidents, they will (alcohol). If a business can sell a food product with no nutritional value whatsoever that makes its customers fat, they will (Twinkies).

People seem to believe that because businesses are made up of people, they will care about people. This is wrong. For some reason the people who are in business forget the fact that they themselves are people and do whatever is necessary to gain a profit. They do not worship a religious God figure, nor do they give a damn for human life. They worship profit.

Business will wipe out species, destroy the rainforest, make Americans fat, lazy and complacent to make a profit. They will fix elections, kill millions, create monopolies, crush the competition, and destroy downtown shops to make a profit. There is nothing that has stopped business from their holy duties. Imagine a man wants to pay your company to make a weapon that will destroy the entire world. You as a person might say No thanks, I dont want to be in anyway responsible for destroying the entire world. That would be bad. Ill just stick to being a human who didnt destroy the planet. But businesses such as Lockheed Martin, Boeing, Aero jet, Bell Helicopter, etc, said Will we make a profit? The Government said Of course you will, this is America, after all. And the businesses said, Ok, well make neato bombers, submarines, missiles and other cool stuff that will destroy the world. We dont have a conscious and we dont give a fuck whether little Johnny gets to graduate elementary school or not. As long as we make a profit we will fuck every single human being on the planet in the cornhole. And while were at it, wed love to knock off a few more species. Too many god damned caribou on this planet anyway. And besides, when is the last time that caribou have helped us turn a profit? How about never. Fuck those stupid caribou bastards. Lets tear up their natural environment in Alaska to dig for oil so that Americans can have SUVs and pay less than $2 a gallon for gas. SUVs are good because they pollute the planet reducing the amount of breathable air and helping the greenhouse effect, they destroy small gas conserving cars in accidents, and they can drive OFF ROAD! Of course, you should always keep your SUV shiney and clean because youre a disgusting yuppie swine. And besides you cant even drive OFF ROAD anymore because we paved the whole fucking earth to make it easier for us to transport Twinkies in-between states so that all of America could have a grease puff to eat while they watch TV. And while we were setting up their TV signals, we put enough radio waves in the air to give everyone brain cancer so that we could make money on cancer treatments. We want all of your money and if we have to kill you to get it, thats fine with us. Because were American Business. And in America, the business of America is business. Fuck the People!

this message was brought to you by the George W. Bush drill in Alaska, and burn the stupid caribou breeding grounds, those fucking animals never did nuthin fur me, and Im the President now, just like my daddy Foundation.

data.headline: Rocket Man
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Good evening everyone. I've been a bit of a bad webmaster lately and it seems to be the beginning of a trend rather than the end of one. I'm not writing much lately, not just for you guys, but for everyone. Mainly I've been sitting around chewing cough drops, being sick, attending weddings and sitting around. A lot of sitting around. I feel andy-lazy, but I'm still keeping a relatively normal time schedule, seeing daylight most everyday.

There's an awesome "behind the music" on now about Green Day and the trouble they had once being a punk band, and then becoming popular. I enjoyed their first album greatly, Insomniac, their second was fair, Nimrod, their third was excellent. Their fourth, Warning is still growing on me. It's an interesting album, the title track "Warning" being excellent, "Minority" is also excellent, but the rest of the album has a very curious feel that seems rather polka-like. Perhaps polka isn't the right word but it just has an odd carnival type feeling that I just can't shake.

The show just focused on "Good Riddance", a very popular track from Nimrod. I'm torn as to whether or not GR was a good idea or not. They said it became incredibly popular, being played on the last episode of Seinfeld, the World Series, at proms and graduations. Which is good, but I feel popularity has thoroughly ruined excellent songs such as Imagine, We Will Rock You and of course We Are the Champions (author's note: because they were back to back on Queen's Greatest Hits (red), radio stations always play them together, despite the fact that they were originally released by Queen on different albums in different periods of the band's development. It's true, look it up: allmusic.com. Green Day tried to come off like releasing GR was a "punk" thing to do because it was the unexpected. I grant them that. GR is an amazing song that I would never had expected from Green Day. Actually I believe it too beautiful to belong to the band that once sang of "taking all you down with me, explosives duct taped to my spine" (Please note, I know there are more than four Green Day albums, from their punk-pre-popular days. I have the albums but haven't absorbed them yet and I just wish to speak of the popular albums for today's discussion.)

The show has shifted gears now, it seems to be an advertisement for their new album Warning, which is really too bad because I was enjoying the controversy of the past.

On to new topics. Do you think the Elton John song Rocket Man is about heroin? I do. Or at least drugs anyway. Rocket seems to imply Needle, you shoot it, you fill it with fuel, it's pointy,

Examples:

"Got my bags packed, pre-flight" (loaded the needle).
"It's lonely out in space" (high).
"I'm gonna be high as a kite by then" (obvious).
"till touchdown brings me down again to find" (the after-crash, and restarting).
"I'm not the man they think I am at all" (hiding the addiction). "

And this part about Mars, he must be high. What the fuck else could he be talking about. And they play this song in Supermarkets, for chrissake. Of course I was at a party recently where full grown adults played Bob Dylan's Rainy Day Women ("everybody must get stoned") as background music for a 50th anniversary. Nobody really noticed, which is ironic considering the stir the song made upon it's release.

I was at the supermarket today. Did you know there is a book version of the movie Pearl Harbor? Can you imagine that? It's like making a book version to Armageddon, Bad Boys or Coyote Ugly. Perhaps you would enjoy a book version of the Van Damme flick Hard Target. Excerpt:

"Mark (Van Damme) rose up and kicked the other guy. He screamed in agony, but then Van Damme, oops I mean Mark, spun around and kicked yet another guy. The kicking went on unabated for about an hour and a half, perhaps less if you discount the credits. But during the opening credits, Mark, who is a part eskimo-indian-police officer did kick the shit out of some other guys because they said that Casablanca was a better movie than Gone with the Wind, which in Mark's fucked up world is like saying that Coke is better than Pepsi."

It goes on and on about the kicking. One would think the author just smoked some weed and then watched the picture on cable a few times. Which it actually says on the dust jacket, John Smitthe is a unemployed carpet shampooer living in Torrance, CA. He enjoys smoking weed and watching Van Damme movies. He hopes to make a book of some other Van Damme movies in the future. The idea sits fine with Jean Claude because he cannot read.

With all the other shit that is coming out in the theaters, that Planet of the Apes movie is starting to look better and better. Of course I want to see the originals first, not that I don't know the plot or it hasn't been ruined for me a hundred times, but it's important to see Charlton Soylent- Green-Is-Best-Used-Without-a-trigger-lock-while-shooting-moses-in-the-ben- hur Heston scream at "damn, dirty apes". Ironic to see a man of the cloth talking about evolution so plainly. Of course if we went back to being apes that would be de-evolution, which I'm certain Charlton as a good Republican would be in favor of. Anything to get back to good old fashion family values where blacks had no rights and women were treated as property. And the man has a point, I think America would look great if we had a few pyramids of our own to compete with the so called "Great" pyramids of Giza. No, the Luxor doesn't count. Any place that has a non- 24hour buffet must feel my wrath.

I think I have rambled enough so I'll take my leave of you. Expect more rambling in the future, and some hot lesbian action on the message board. (gotta plug the board, right?) For the best Thunt.net experience, sign up for the mailing list so you don't have to check everyday when I write, like every week, and remember use only as directed. (do what I say.)

Art Pimmentale  Art has quit his position as President to work as at used car lot. During the day he sells Hondas and Fords, and at night he parties down at TGI Fridays, the ultimate place to party. His drink is a rum and coke, which he usually drinks with a side of coke. In the future he plans to run for office again and then quit to get stoned.

Champagne Turismo - Champagne didnt seem to learn from her defeat in the recent ASI election. She has once again teamed up with her old pal David Born Looser Sommers. Thats right. Teamed up with Sommers. You know his dating record! Hubba Hubba! But wait! Hes not dating her! Theyre living together! But wait again! Theyre not LIVING together; theyre just living together. So it does prove that like fine wine, Champagne has good taste. Shes sleeping with another ASI hanger-oner; we regret that we cannot reveal his name here, as decorum prevents it. But lets just say that his initials are JB.

Jason Bryant - This former ASI President looked to be having a string of bad luck. As you recall his candidate for EIC, Brendan Wonnacott was dropped like a bad habit (insert bad habit of your choice, nose picking, ass picking, picking up skanks, picking up hitchhikers, picking up dime sacks, picking up the pieces, etc). Additionally, his golden boy David Sommers and his Students First slate turned up Students Last at the polls, leaving Bryant shit out of luck in the worst way. But the man may be down, but he sure isnt out. Rumor has it that he dumped his old service provider (although she was loved by one artist) and has signed on with a new provider. But now Bryant knows how important it is to have your own wine cellar. Its said that the man is now having all he can drink. Which in some circles is believed to be ten times better than all you can eat, which Im told hes having as well

Chairman Sommers - Apparently the new living arrangements are keeping the Chairman in touch with his roots. Or at least in touch with the dyed blonde roots of some sorority sweethearts who like to hang out with the infamous Champagne. So in that department, things are well for Mr. Sommers. However when it comes to jobs, Mr. Sommers is in desperate need of employment! Hes been out of work for over six months now. All he does is sit around and smoke cigars, drink brandy and screw whores. His friends and family have tried to talk him out of it but the man is inflexible.

So that about raps it up for the ASI candidates of days past. Lets thank them all for having legs as long as the Radio City Rock-ettes, tongues as long as lounge lizards and mouths as big as Hoover vacuum cleaners. Never go back in time and count the number of exclamation marks used in this article. You dont want to know!

data.headline: States in Review
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
California  The boring flat farmlands of Dixon and Vacaville were soon transformed into the urban sprawl of Sacramento as I flew across the golden state. The towering trees of Auburn and Truckee quickly replaced the sprawl. I then zoomed across the state line into

Nevada  After Reno, Nevada became one long stretch of desert. The mountains flew by as the hours dragged on. Gas stations with slot machines and rest areas seem to be the only cultural attraction of this dull state. As I reached Windover I saw the last bastion of legalized gambling and sinful debauchery. The casinos seemed to scream out "This is your last chance to sin before Utah!" I ignored their lecherous cries and passed through two huge rock formations as I entered

Utah  Past the rock formations the ground was covered with salt in every direction. The salt flats are more immense than you can imagine. It looked like snow, but I knew it was too hot outside for any snow to exist. I stopped at the first available rest area. A mean sign warned me to be on the look out for Snakes and Scorpions. Snakes are bad enough, but in combination with scorpions it was clear that I wasn't going to spend much time at this rest stop. I walked out onto the flats and performed all the scientific tests I could think of on this unusual substance. When I walked on the salt it crackled and broke, it was not soft like snow but brittle. I smelled it and tasted it. It was salt, and it was everywhere. After I got out of the salt flats, Utah wasn't as exciting. Nice mountains and brilliant tactical design of nestling the cities right up against the mountains (see Salt Lake, Provo). A few days after my arrival in Provo, I drove through the canyon into

Wyoming  For years I have said that Wyoming does not exist. That it is a government conspiracy. The facts are: It's a perfect rectangle. You have never met anyone who is from Wyoming. You have never visited Wyoming; you just think you did because they injected you with mind-bending drugs that made you think you were in Wyoming while they did vicious tests on you without your knowledge. Now, having driven through Wyoming I'm not entirely sure that this is false. There are some really nice mountains with flat tops that I think are called mesas. Also I passed the continental divide a few times, not sure what that means, but I've been there now. There are no major attractions in Wyoming. With the exception of "Little America", a gas station, restaurant, auto repair rest area type place that I passed as I first entered the state. After I passed it they immediately began advertising for the next "Little America", which was over three hundred miles away in Cheyenne. It must be pretty pathetic to assume that everyone on that road is going all the way to Cheyenne. Unfortunately for the tourist bureau in Wyoming I found this to be true. I flew towards Cheyenne occasionally stopping for gas and playing cat and mouse with a hot girl from the University of Wyoming who drove a white Toyota Celica. Of course this could have been a dream, I'm not entirely sure that any of it happened, I was after all "in Wyoming", at least until I crossed the border into

Colorado  At first glance Colorado looks very similar to Wyoming. The mountains are a little taller and you don't feel woozy anymore, as the government has stopped injecting you with drugs. I haven't seen much of Colorado yet. Ft. Collins, where I'm staying with my good friend Ben is very close to the northern border of Colorado. I plan to see Denver sometime soon and that may enhance my description of this state. Although instead of a parade to celebrate my arrival, we had a brief but very strong rainstorm. The rain came down diagonally as it was blown by a great wind. We had to close all the windows and batten down the hatches of the apartment, but we weathered the storm and went out after for dinner. It left the air clean and the temperature nice and cool.

After dinner we returned to Ben's apartment, which is fucking huge and watched a movie named Sugar and Spice that could only be described as "god awful". It could have been a fun high school type movie where cheerleaders rob a bank, but unfortunately the writer and director took themselves seriously and made a unmatchable cross between gross out over the top humor that fails, and lame attempts to be poignant. It's filmed in scope (2:35:1 aspect ratio, really wide), which is all wrong for the picture (scope is usually used for epic pictures such as Lawrence of Arabia, The Godfather and 2001), and the director uses it like a retarded child trying to eat with chopsticks. Shot by shot the picture is horrifying as characters talk directly into the camera and scenes that feel 100% like dream sequences turn out to be real. It's just that bad.

Thanks for journeying with me on Thunt.net  States in Review. I'll have the pictures up soon, so you can experience the trip in full color. Thanks for reading.

data.headline: New! New! New!
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Good morning everyone. I've got lots of new stuff for you today at Thunt.net. And now with no further ado, new stuff!

NEW: thunt.net/photo  We've got new photos in the photo section. Lots of em. Highlights include Devil's Tower & Mt. Rushmore, featuring Thunt riding a gas station dinosaur and much much more. Also included is a cute tour of Downtown Ft. Collins, which has a place that sells "swell food". I kid you not.

NEW: Thomashunt.com  A whole new website is up at our sister site Thomashunt.com. I think it's nice looking and I just implemented the database last night so now it will be easy to update. Check it out.

NEW: Story  Man, what is it with cops anyway. Ben and I were driving back from South Dakota when a cop pulls us over. He starts with the typical cop question "you boys know why I pulled you over?" "Was it the five pounds of uncut black tar heroin in the trunk?" "Nope, u was going 39 in a 25." "Well no shit. Imagine people coming off a highway into your redneck town and still going highway speed. Amazing what can happen when you don't put any signs up you cocksucking sob." "Can I see your driver's license?" "Why, never seen one before?" "You boys been drinking?" "Yeah, it's nine o'clock so we decided to get drunk and do Lusk, Wyoming. That's what all the kids are doing these days." "Well, I'm going to let you go. Go a bit slower next time." "Oh, thanks officer, you're so great, Shakespeare must have been writing about you when he wrote 'with form and moving how express and admirable, with action how like an angel, with apprehension how like a God.' You really are that great. Of course why did you pull us over if you weren't going to give us a ticket, having trouble getting it up with the wife and needed to feel powerful again? I see. Totally understandable. Have a nice day officer."

We sure were lucky; if we were driving my car (california plates) or I was driving (california license) he would have thrown the book at us for sure. Stupid redneck back country cops. Can't wait till they're all replaced with robocops. Robocop may have had a Frankenstein complex, but at least he was polite. "Thank you for your cooperation."

data.headline: Weather and Grunge
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
As I sit here up in Ft. Collins I think back to Sacramento. Yesterday I talked to my good friend Justin who informed me that you guys were in the middle of a heat wave and that it was over 100 degrees there. Wow, that must be hot. It's hard for me to understand such heat as I glance out on the mountains, which I can barely see because there are clouds blocking them out. It's about 65-70 degrees here; with a slight mist from the recent rain and the clouds overhead insure that there will be more rain this afternoon. So I just thought I'd gloat a little. Hope you guys are enjoying the heat.

Now on to more important business. I'm watching this awesome documentary about the Grunge music scene in Seattle called "HYPE!" Shocking enough to me it's on the oxygen network. If you can believe this shit? Oxygen is the second women's network as I recall. The first being the Golden Girls dominated Lifetime network. I swear, they run Golden Girls for twelve hours every day. And what's worse is I kind of want to watch them all just hoping that the episode with Quentin Tarantino will come on. As all of you Tarantino fanatics know, Quentin played an Elvis impersonator in an episode of Golden Girls where they went to Vegas or something. I really want to see it. But I can't put myself through watching twelve hours a day of Golden Girls or even to watch the beginning of the episodes. Who the fuck out there finds "Blanche" attractive, fucking shit the woman is old.

Pasadena on Fox - I can't believe how low people will go to rip off the Sopranos. I just saw an ad for this Pasadena show. It has a mob family and a young daughter who learns that her parents are mobsters. Wow, how original.

Damn this is a good documentary. Lots of live performances, including the first ever performance of "Smells like teen spirit" and according to the net, the soundtrack is a great album, like the soundtrack to Singles. Also Oxygen is trying to keep my watching by asking a very difficult trivia question about documentaries at every commercial break and then giving the answer when they come back to the show. But what they weren't counting on, was that I might actually know the answers to some of the questions, and I mean know them cold. Example: two of the easy questions were: 1. What was the name of the documentary about two kids trying to get into the NBA?, obviously the answer is Hoop Dreams. Another easy one was who directed the documentary Roger and Me?, obviously the answer is Michael Moore, who also directed The Big One, wrote the book Downsize This, and created the television shows "TV Nation" and "The Awful Truth" (now on Bravo, check your local listings). Since I knew the answers to these questions, I guess I don't have to watch the show anymore. I don't have to wait for them to come back from the commercial break! I can leave! The question won't haunt me in the middle of the night! I won't have to watch commercials for parentsanonymous, inside of every woman there is a girl: let her out, and every type of horrible tampon ad you can imagine. But the documentary was so good I watched it anyway, despite my mastery of the questions.

data.headline: One evil genius trying to destroy the State Hornet
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Well, it appears that thunt.net and I have become the focal point for many rumors and speculations about the State Hornet. Ive even heard that the faculty adviser over there is warning people about me and telling people (journalists, the type of people who should learn to always have two sources for everything) that I am some kind of evil genius bent on the utter destruction of the State Hornet. I would like to say loudly and clearly that this is not my plan. The Hornet seems bent on destroying itself, and I must say, it is doing a damn good job of that (picking more and more incompetent people to be EIC is a damn good start). But here at thunt.net all we do is spread the rumors, and we seem to excel at that. So, here we go again:

Ban Thunt.net! - Word from inside the hornet is that spring chicken EIC Layla Bomb wants to ban the IP of the evil website that is going to destroy the newspaper. The IP does not belong to a porn site or a Haitian revolutionarys website, but to good old Thunt.net. I hope she does succeed in banning Thunt.net. You know it will only be about ten minutes later when I get around it, either by an email distribution system or a series of mirrors. The thing I like best about the State Hornet is that they are the press, the media, the ones who are supposed to be fighting for the first amendment, but they hate it so much when little old me publishes rumors and news on my little old web page. So, ban me you fuckers, ban me all you want.

Layla Bomb: Not Found - This is a good one. Miss Bomb went on vacation all summer and didnt tell anyone where she went. King Fitz even called the police and filed a missing persons report on our dear Miss Bomb. Miss Bomb showed up from a vacation that is heavily documented (diary entries every five minutes) on her website (Layla Land). She returned to find a paper on its knees. Layla has not hired a staff, or any writers and will not be doing a survival guide this year. Also, the advertising staff has even been canceling ads because they werent even sure that there was going to be a newspaper this semester. Additionally computer-genius Miss Bomb allowed some mac guru to wipe all of the hard drives of the hornets computers, including the servers. Leaving the hornet without any templates of what the paper used to look like, so making a new issue quickly will not be easy. In another stroke of brilliance, no one can find the original cds for the software needed to create the paper (Photoshop, Quark, etc), so theyre really fucked now. This is why I think that Layla is the best EIC ever. Even better than Dina, sorry dear, but when youre beat, youre beat.

Biggest Looser Ever - Guess whos coming back to the hornet. After attending one comic book convention, sans resume, sans samples, sans business cards, Andy has given up his dream of having anything to do with comic books to go back to the hornet and be Laylas bitch. Andy has returned to the de-maculating, underpaid position of Creative Director. A position once held by another three time looser, Ryan Anne Polli. Will Andy beat her record and go for four, only time will tell, but its a safe bet.

The best part about the story is how Andy got his job back. First, Andy called Laylas voicemail and left several panicked calls, begging for his job back. Then he met with her and begged some more. Ive even heard stories that he got down on his knees and pleaded with her to get his lame position back. Its just really sad, and Im not sure I want to talk about it anymore.

Well, that about sums it up for rumors today on Thunt.net. Id just like to wish good luck to all the poor fools who have to work at the hornet this semester. data.headline: Books in Review
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Now in an attempt to follow the advice of the self-proclaimed "best band in the wurld", U2, I will attempt to elevate the commentary of Thunt.net with "Books in Review".

Currently I am reading:

Gates of Fire by Steven Pressfield  It's an engrossing history/fiction about the Spartan's desperate last stand at Thermoplye.

I have recently read:

On the Road by Jack Kerouac  An epic tale of one man's journeys in the United States. A true classic of the beat era. Kerouac uses incredibly simple language, but the whole book seems to read like poetry. My favorite line was "We went seventy.". There is just something so simple, but so joyous in that line. I can't describe this book, but I can tell you that you should read it. It is a slow book, the kind of book to be read a chapter a day in the evenings when the sun is setting, but it is definitely a book worth reading. It contains the idea of freedom in it. And freedom is an idea worth holding in your head and thinking about for a while.

Trainspotting by Irvin Welsh  The tale of a young Scottish lad's troubles with heroin. You've probably all seen the excellent movie by the same name, but the book remains worth reading. Mainly because it is written in Scottish, not English, and when you read it you are forced to sound out every word until the voice in your head adapts and speaks Scottish to you. It's a trip. Also it includes several stories that were not in the movie. While the movie is clearly one story, the book reads more like a collection of short stories about the same group of characters. Sometimes the author really throws you a curve when he has one character narrating in one story and another character narrating in the next. It's a bit of a trip, but soon you begin recognizing the narrators because they all speak differently. Like Spud who always uses the word "likesay", even in his head. It's a fun read.

Breakfast at Tiffanys by Truman Capote  I've wanted to read Capote for a long time now and I finally got to him last week. I had this book and several others on my shelf and it was only a matter of time. Breakfast at Tiffany's is absolutely stunning. It's only 115 pages long, so it's rather short, but it tells a complete and wonderful story. Capote also uses a simple writing style similar to Kerouac, and manages to graft his characters instantly into your mind. The name of the main character, Holly Golightly is so perfect that it has become a part of the English language, similar to Joseph Heller's Catch-22, very few people know that the book came before the term. Breakfast at Tiffany's is definitely worth reading and you should be able to finish it rather quickly. If you're lucky you'll find the same version of the story I found, because it had two or three other short stories included, one of which "The Christmas Story" (or something like that) was incredibly moving and even made me cry. Capote is a stunner, I plan to read In Cold Blood and Music for Chameleons in the next few weeks.

Thank you for reading Books in Review, on Thunt.net. Please post your own reviews of what you've been reading to the message board. We're all curious to know what you've been reading. Maybe someone will read that book because of your recommendation or perhaps if a few people have read it, we can discuss the pros and cons of the book.

data.headline: Josh Leon's Party
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
This is probably the most difficult article I've ever had to write. I mean, there's a lot of pressure on me. I just met a whole bunch of people at this party who know me only as "thunt.net" and now they're probably all pointing their web browsers this way to see what I have to say about the party. I thought about many different ways of doing this article. I thought of a straight person by person route, analyzing and picking on each person I met, but then I remembered what good old Hunter S. Thompson said, "When the going gets tough, the tough go Gonzo." And so I did.

Editor's note: First of all, the photos from this spectacular event are up in the photo section. Second of all, I spoke to Mr. Thunt briefly after he finished this article. He informed me that he had created a masterwork of fiction. I asked him what that meant and he informed me that he had taken the party and twisted it around and made it into a story, so that the people who attended the party wouldn't be sure of what actually happened and what didn't happen. He also informed me that everything had been re-arranged for dramatic purposes. I just thought it was best to inform you, the audience, of Mr. Thunt's twisted themes, and to remind you, as always, never to believe what you read.

It was around 10:30 when I called Andy to see if we were gonna go to this "Josh Leon party". The party started and 9:30 and typically Andy and Matt hadn't even left yet. I told Andy I'd give him a ride and he said that I had to be there in five minutes. I said I would. He repeated five minutes, I mean it, five minutes. I grabbed my trusty Radio Shack timer and set it for five minutes and told him to fuck off. I arrived four minutes and 32 seconds later, flaunting the timer. Andy, packing a bottle and a video camera told me I was driving. He and Matt got in the car and we were underway.

As we pulled into the apartment complex, Andy started talking about how he wanted us to make our entrance. He spoke of him going in first so that he could get the reaction shot on Layla's face, he also spoke of him announcing us, and other grandiose schemes of lunacy. I'm a shy person myself, so I told him we would just walk in like normal, leave the great entrances to Batman and his heterosexual life mate Robin. After we parked, two slightly toasted gentleman rushed to car, shocked and excited to see us. The gentlemen were two "formers". Former EIC David "Chairman" Sommers and Former Opinion Editor Brendan Wonnacott. They clapped us on the back and led us to the party. They seemed to be expecting some serious fireworks, and I, ever worried for my own security asked them for assurances that if this was an ambush, or if anything went down, that they would have my back. They assured me that this was true and we went inside.

The party was clearly in low gear. A collection of hat-wearing smokers were out on the balcony and a random assortment of people gathered in the living room boozing. The first person I recognized was Layla Bomb, the current EIC of the Hornet. I thought she looked familiar because I had seen her picture a few weeks ago when I wrote that horse-fucking article. I walked up to her and introduced myself (see photo). I don't think she recognized me as I used my super-sneaky alias "Thomas". But then the beautiful Jen Tillett screamed out "Oh my God, Thomas Hunt in the flesh!", so I suppose the whole secrecy thing was pretty much blown at that point.

I continued to circulate the party meeting other Hornet people new and old. I met Marin and Kim from the dorms. They informed me that they came with Janet, someone else from the dorms. I informed them that this was fine with me. I continued on my way. I found Josh Leon barricaded in his room reading an "academic" version of the bible with a harem full of girls on his bed. I was impressed. I thought to myself, I never knew Josh Leon was such a player, but I guess now I know the truth.

Eventually I ended up on the balcony with the smokers where I met Tara McDonald (or something like that, it's so hard to learn people's names at a party). Tara informed me that she was very angry because her name was mentioned on my website. I dredged the depths of my memory and realized I had no clue who this girl was. I certainly hadn't written about her, but I did recall that there was some message on the message board about her having a "nice ass". Nonplussed, she continued to tell me how my message board was full of gay-bashing, racist, sexist people. I told her that I dont censor my message board, whatever people want to post is what they post. She still seemed angry and told me that she read a short story of mine that I submitted to the News & Review. She asked me how the story turned out, confusing reality with literature. I explained to her that the story was made up. She seemed confused. She told me that my website and my story were smut. I don't think she is a very nice person.

Rude Tara was interrupted by Brendan pushing a beer into my hand. As I tried to explain to him that I was the designated driver and thus couldn't drink, I noticed that Brendan had bigger problems on his hands. Or more aptly put, in his hands. Somebody had gotten the already drunk Josh Leon stoned and it seemed his knees were giving way. Brendan, flanked by Sommers carried the inebriated Leon to his bed where again chicks flocked to him. To be more specific, one chick named Sharra (not Charmin) and a guy named Derrick (not Darrin) lay on either side of our intoxicated host. Apparently Josh Leon's magnetism was not limited to merely the opposite sex.

Suddenly a phone began ringing. It was a small "american-style" cell phone. I answered it. It was some guy named Ed looking for Bridget. We screamed for Bridget, then we went to work on Ed. Ed told us that he was one of Bridget's ex-boyfriends and he desperately wanted her back. He started to expose all kinds of other highly personal issues to us, but then Bridget came and took the phone away. She freaked out and told us not to answer it if it rang again. She left the room. Suddenly the phone rang again, so we answered it. It was our old pal Ed again. Ed told us about the problems that he and Bridget experienced in the sac. Apparently Ed was very much into experimentation, but Bridget was not. I felt bad for Ed but there was nothing I could do for him, so I handed the phone to Matt Brown. Then Matt hung up on Ed to take call waiting. It was Tony Harvey. He was calling Bridget back. The room made the instant conclusion, that somebody should go pick up Tony. Matt began surveying the room to see if anyone was sober and could pick Tony up. Typically, I was the only one sober and thus it was my duty to go get Tony. Bridget noticed that we were using her phone and she came with me. We raced to Tony's, brought him back, and then the party really got started.

I walked out on the balcony to get some fresh air and I found myself alone with Layla. As I always do with people that hate me, I decided to engage her in conversation. I asked her opinion of the horse-fucker story. She remained silent. I realized she was probably from Generation X or some MTV-addled generation, so I decided to talk to her in "who wants to be a millionaire" style. I gave her two choices: "were you offended" or "did you think it was funny". Survey says: Offended. I laughed and told her that everyone else thought it was funny, hoping to make it clear that no one really believes that she is fucking horses, despite the ample photographic evidence. She seemed perturbed and did little to carry on the conversation. I jokingly asked her if she was going to sue. She snapped back, have you been worrying about it? I smiled and told her that I knew copyright law and it was clearly on my side. She made a quip about how Thunt.net was not the National Enquirer. I was glad she knew the difference. I think of Thunt.net to be more like The New York Times, myself. Suddenly there was a loud crash in the next room as Brendan was wrestling Andy. I watched the carnage unfold and Layla drifted off without saying goodbye. I wasn't surprised, these GenX/GenY type people really have no manners anyway.

After the wrestling match, which shockingly was won by Andy, I went to check on Josh and his harem. I found Josh to be in dire straights. Jen Tillett had put down her Bud Light (Jen Tillett always drinks Bud Light, I think it's her beer of choice, if I was ever in a bar and I was going to buy Jen Tillett a drink, I would make it a Bud (grand pause) Light.) and she was attacking Josh Leon. I asked Russ what all the commotion was about. Russ told me that Josh had told Jen that she liked fucking Brendan up the ass or some other such nonsense and that Jen had taken offence at it. Jen beat Josh bloody and banged his head up against the filing cabinet next to his bed. Then Jen, joined by her tag-team partner Sharra threw Leon off his bed and continued to pound him into submission.

It was about then that the cops showed up. The music stopped and everyone started freaking out. Shouts of "5-O" circulated the sedated crowd. As the only sober person there I figured I should go out and speak to them. I descended the staircase from Leon's apartment knowing full well that no one was following me and it was to be me and the cops, mano-e-mano. It turned out that they weren't cops, but simply security guards. They informed me that we had to break up the party because there had been complaints about the noise and that if we didn't break it up, they would call the police and we would be fined. I apologized for the noise and asked them if it would be okay if we kept the party going but kept the noise to a minimum. They informed me that there would be no further warnings and that the next time they got a noise complaint they would call the police. I told them that was fine, we wouldn't be noisy anymore. Throughout our conversation they were constantly fingering and talking on their fancy walkie-talkies (the only weapon a security guard can carry), I was impressed. One of them got out a pad to write down my name, since he thought I lived in the apartment. I informed them that I did not live there, I was just sent out to speak. They didn't like this. They wanted to speak to the man who lived there, I told them that I would inform him of his rights. They were displeased. I told them that the owner was sleeping. They questioned how he could be sleeping in the middle of a party and I kindof, looked at them. They got the idea. I again apologized for the noise and returned to the party. The cops took off.

I went back inside and told everyone that we had a second chance, but we'd just have to keep the volume down from now on. I also told them that from a worst-case perspective, when the cops arrived to cite us for disturbing the peace, I could try to talk them out of it as well. Josh Leon seemed pleased and told me I was his lawyer. It was a job I wasn't crazy about taking, it was clear, even this early in the night that Josh Leon was going to need a lot of legal advice before this thing was over.

I needed a break from all the action so I went and sat in Josh's bedroom. Unfortunately, I didn't find much of a break. I found Russ from Canada talking to his cousin from Canada and I'm not sure, but I'm pretty sure that he was hitting on her. I didn't want to judge him, him being Canadian and all, but it sure looked like that was what was happening. Also Keith Reed was sitting in the corner talking about how he wanted to talk to Trader Joe and that he had a great trade for him. He spoke of many different trades, from four lobsters for two pigs and ten salamis for one ham, but Trader Joe seemed uninterested. Although it is still unclear to me whether or not Trader Joe is a real person and whether or not Keith was actually speaking to him, or just speaking to himself. The party was definitely in second gear, it had gone from strange to weird.

I walked back out into the front room and I was greeted with all kinds of horrors. Someone had gotten Andy high and he was dancing around with a lampshade on his head. Bridget had passed out in the corner and Josh Leon was collapsed in an easy chair. Only Russ and Brendan seemed to have any energy left as they rapped along to a Jimmy Hendricks song. People began filing out the door, unable to deal with the weirdness of the scene. I stayed, drawn to the weirdness as I always am, unable to turn away. But somehow without the normals, the weirdness started to loose its thrill. Andy took off the lampshade. Bridget woke up and had a conversation with Sommers. Everyone was sitting on the carpet staring at each other. Jen Tillett was sucking on her fingers. The party was beginning to end. Someone turned up the volume on the stereo and it began to blare the closing notes of Hendricks's fantastic rendition of the star-spangled- banner (live at woodstock). Everyone seemed to bob their heads together as Jimmi went higher and higher, until finally he could do no more, and he brought the song back down and with it went the party. Even the weird had too much now, they had jobs and lives to return to. Several left without saying goodbye, you just glanced once at where they were sitting and noticed they were gone. Josh Leon retired to his bedroom and began setting his alarm clock. Andy was butchering a guitar and I told him it was time to leave. Andy gathered up Matt and his camcorder and we were on our way. Josh Leon's party had ended. It was something like three a.m. and we were hungry.

Prologue:

We flew down Howe Ave in the mood for Tacos. Andy began singing the praises of Del Taco, but Matt and I are both more into Taco Bell. We asked Andy if he had any money. He didn't. So we went to Taco Bell. I ordered a ten pack to feed the huddled masses in my car and Andy kept screaming from the back seat that they were going to spit in my food. His fears were soon forgotten as he munched silently on a taco I paid for. We got back to the old apartment building only to be greeted by the sound of another party. Andy and Matt's downstairs roommates Joe and Juan Rodriguez (no relation) were having a party. I was tired and needed rest, but we went inside anyway. Andy stayed outside smoking and Matt started handing out the food. The grande meal was a good idea, purchased to feed the masses, and here were the masses. I grabbed a Mountain Dew from the fridge and enjoyed my tacos. Matt, Joe and some girls named Christina and Jessica began taking tequila shots. I stayed for a while, watching MTV and every other channel (Joe was trying to watch them all at once), but soon it was too much for me. I need the quiet that only comes from respectable married people who have to work for a living. I drove down the road to my friend Justin's place, used my key to enter and found a dead quiet apartment. It was just what I needed to get this story down on paper and to relax after what had been a long evening. I fired up the laptop and hooked into the high speed internet connection. It was four a.m., and about time to update Thunt.net, I thought to myself.

THE END


Author's Note: If your name was mentioned in this article, I'm certain that I misspelled it. For this I apologize.

data.headline: The begining of the End
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
I, like the rest of Americans today was shocked and saddened to see the utter destruction of the World Trade Center in New York and the vicious attack on The Pentagon in Washington D.C.. I was awoken at 07:30 PST, just in time to see building two fall live. I began monitoring the story from the TNCC (Thunt.Net Command Center). Pictures of the command center are available in the photo section.


TNCC

It is now 20:00 PST and I have watched approximately 10 straight hours of news coverage on the four monitors here in the TNCC. I have spoken to Secretary of Defense Bhaskar Sinha via secure telephone from Davis, CA where we are glad to say he has reached a secured, yet undisclosed location. Secretary of State Justin White arrived at the TNCC, around 16:30 PST, we debriefed Secretary White and came to a consensus on a course of action. We went to Papa Murphy's to secure a pizza, and Raley's to secure some soda and ice cream. While we walked from the TNCC to the helicopters waiting to take us to our destination we encountered NSA Director Michael Keenan and he accompanied us on our journey. We were also in contact throughout the day with FBI Director John Humphreys, CIA Director Mathew Brown, Secretary of Education Andrew H. Duong and Secretary of the Interior Joshua Pryor. Our entire staff are all at secured locations and are in no danger.



NSA Director Keenan was saddened to report that prior warning of the attack was available on the auction site Ebay (thunt.net exclusive), but experts failed to understand the information until it was too late.

Director Keenan also collected photos on the internet today, available for your perusal in the photo section.

Below are the contents of Thunt's notebook kept as the day's events unfolded:



Rather quoted Lincoln: "One cannot imagine the foot prints of an enemy in the Applications or drinking from the Ohio."

Best description, heard in the background of one of the amateur videotapes: "Fuck, the whole tower just collapsed, oh my God."

News 10: Breaking news! Arden Fair Mall closed.

Bases on Threat Condition Delta.

Channel 10 is breaking in for local weather.

Constant mention of how the tragedy reminded people of the movie Pearl Harbor. I completely agree, both were horrifying.

ABC: Man in the background with a pen in his mouth.

Jennings with glasses, horrible frames.

Rather looks beat-up.

Baseball, Space Needle, Disneyland closed.

Blair on TV, London on alert: "Will not rest until this evil is driven from this world."

Pope: "unspeakable horror".

Rather, Lincoln again: "Let not he who is houseless tear down the house of another, let him build his own house and be safe from violence."

Rather, Jefferson: "The price of freedom is eternal vigilance."

Emmy's canceled.

Of course during this disaster MTV was actually playing videos. I paused my coverage to watch U2's "Stuck in a moment and you can't get out". It was surprisingly fitting. They almost broke in with Dan Rather's voice, but cut back to the video. Soon after, MTV ended, and switched to CBS news.

HSN off the air. Only channels on are Cartoons, Kid Shows and the News.

E! & Sci-fi are still on. Not even a horrendous terrorist bombing can stop celebrity gossip or reruns of Star Trek.

Rather, Paine: "Times that try men's souls."

Hamas: "Humanity taking revenge against US oppression."

Coverage Wars:
CBS: Early lead.
CNN: Took lead.
ABC: coming on strong. Amateur video, dead wife of Solicitor General.
CNN: Back to front with Amateur video of Doctor.
ABC: Two major exclusives. Amateur video of rubble and video of under belly of plane into building.
CNN: Exclusive, bombing in Afghanistan. All other networks pick it up, except Fox News, who doesn't give a shit about its viewers.


Jennings is so tired. Ink marks on his hands. Pen in hand. No jacket. Clicking off things on his fingers.

No commercials. All day.

6:30 pm  All three networks restarted shows with logos and recaps. Cnn continues unheeded.

Great respect for HSN and VH1. HSN switched to a canceled programming right away, later picked up CNN coverage. VH1 dropped programming right away and picked up CBS news.

NBC's coverage is embarrassing. Not even covering the live pentagon briefing. Although NBC does lead in hot chicks. Cambell Brown is hot and the new blonde with hair Katie Couric doesn't look half bad.

NBC and CBS have the same headline: Attack on America.

End Thunt's notebook.


The bombing today was horrible but it is only the beginning. The twentieth century witnessed a new kind of war, a nuclear war. The twenty- first century is poised to witness a far worse kind of war, a war of terror. Today's attack was horrifying but imagine if the terrorists had used chemical or biological weapons. If that type of attack had occurred we might be saying goodbye to all of New York, not just a handful of buildings downtown. America has just faced the worst tragedy in all of her history, but I fear that this is not the end. I'm Thomas Hunt, reporting live from the TNCC.


Thunt
