data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
more info on the WTC at: http://thunt.net/wtc/

I really don't like this new trend in movie re-releases. I like the idea of a re-release. I like it when they restore the print, fix up the sound and release the movie on the big screen so that those of us who were too young or too not born can go see the movie on the big screen. But I don't like these new re-releases where they're changing the movie and adding and subtracting scenes, putting goofy digital characters behind all the main plot and totally altering the ending. It sucks for many reasons. Soon all re-releases will have to be like that. Movies that have nothing wrong with them will be re-cut so that audiences can "see it again for the first time" and shit. Even the Monty Python Re-Release, which I think would be a great re-release only with a new print and new sound, felt the need to add new scenes. Of course they only added 24 seconds, and were clearly poking fun at the silly re-release idea, they still did kowtow by adding in new footage. Soon they'll be re-cutting all re-releases. Here are some ideas I've had:

Casablanca  Now with 45 minutes of added footage. Did you always wonder what happened to Rick's Piano Player Sam? Now you can find out, because we've used breakthrough computer technology to digitally (not analogally) recreate Sam. Follow Sam on his adventures in Casablanca without Rick and Elsa for 45 minutes after Rick and Claude Reins walk off into the fog. What will happen? What excitement! Will Rick return for his good buddy? See the movie the way it was meant to be seen, before the racist censors said that Sam was just a minor character and that his story wasn't important enough to come to a complete conclusion. We've corrected that wrong, also we've enhanced the movie by creating a bold new print: IN COLOR and we've substituted that boring soundtrack with what the creators intended: music by Queen and some replacement singer singing Queen's songs, because Freddy is Dead (long live Freddy, he really was the best rock singer of the 80s). In Theaters: October 31st, 2001.

Citizen Kane  Do you find Orson Welles 1941 classic boring and depressing? We do too. We've taken the film and restored it to all of it's glory. Kane no longer looses his bid for governor, his wife and son don't die in a car accident and we've cut out all that boring shit about Rosebud. Who really cares about that anyway. We've also added a new character: Fonzie. Watch as Governor Kane and Fonzie take on the city bosses in New York. Watch as happily married Kane envy's the Fonze's ability to pick up chicks. (please note in 1941, the word chick still meant prostitute, Fonze would actually be paying these women). We've freshened up the print (it looked too dark and depressing) and we've added a new soundtrack by the Dixie Chicks, because we like their Dixie Tits. Coming soon to a theater near you!

Pearl Harbor  The best movie of the otts is back again, we command you to see it again. We've added three more hours detailing the lives of the parents of members of the love triangle. Guess what! They were involved in a love triangle too, what incredible irony! We've also cut out that battle scene because what's really important in a movie about Pearl Harbor is what happened between Ben Affleck and Kate Breckentits. This is what American History is all about. For example, the Vietnam War is only a metaphor for Ben's fight with alcoholism and the collapse of Silicon Valley is really just symbolic of Kate's rise to stardom. You can learn everything from Hollywood and the Star System. And now you can see Pearl Harbor again, for the first time. You will be supplied with an adult diaper so that you won't have to leave the theater and interrupt your pearl harbor experience. Armed guards will help you with the diaper and ensure that you stay seated the entire time so that you too can enjoy Michael Bay's epic: Pearl Harbor (voted best movie of all time by every major film critic, including Thunt).

Wow, what amazing and exciting films we have to look forward to. I just can't wait for these hot new releases, because I'm an ignorant shithead and don't realize that I could just walk two blocks to the video store and rent them for three bucks. I'd rather pay eight bucks to see them in the theater, because of 3 more scenes that fuck up the pace of the film and that cool scene where they inserted my favorite character into the backdrop so that there is only ten minutes when Affleck isn't on screen and that's when that big boat is sinking. I wish they'd cut that part out too. Who really gives a shit about boats sinking. I want to see people dancing and fucking around parachutes. That's what I'm all about.

data.headline: Équipage De Thunt
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
more info on the WTC at: http://thunt.net/wtc/
more info on Équipage De Thunt at: http://thunt.net/wanted/

Attention Thunt.net readers. I have received a top secret report from a source who calls himself LampshadeMan. Here is the report in it's entirety:

For the last six months I have been infiltrating a terrorist group known as Équipage De Thunt or "Thunt Crew". They do not know that I am a spy (or a ninja). If they found out they would expose the fact that I am a hermaphrodite.

A few weeks ago, I was at a party [location deleted]. It was there that I learned about another party, a party far more sinister and downright evil. I was invited to this party. The "Thunt Crew" was not. I knew that this would be bad, worse than anything I have ever seen before, horrifying and terrifying even.

When I arrived at the party I knew that something bad would happen. I just didn't know how far they would take it. Later I found their plans, showing that they took it as far as they could.

Excerpt from Équipage De Thunt Plans: I. The Padre will go in first, disguised as a Swedish Nun. Once inside he will begin talking to [name deleted] and [explicative deleted] [name deleted]. Carry out part one of the mission.

I wasn't able to recognize him in his disguise. But I was able to recognize what he had done. I noticed that the punch tasted a little off, judging by the smell it had clearly been pissed in. Also there was a weird smell coming from the microwave. Further examination led me to believe that somehow he had shit in the microwave. The psychokinetic abilities of this man boggles the mind. I also found the host of the party Codename NICE ASS? crying in the corner. Apparently someone dressed as Robin of Locksley had told her that she was an ugly bitch and that no one at the party could tell the difference between her ass and her face. They attempted to discover the difference by seeing which end shit came out of, but it turned out that shit came out of her mouth as well as her ass. The loveable rouge then disappeared to Sherwood forest, but I knew that he was not gone yet.

I heard a loud crash coming from the roof and I knew that the party was not over yet. I later learned that the crash was part of Thunt Crew plan II.

Excerpt from Équipage De Thunt Plans: II. Odd Job will be dropped by helicopter piloted by Swoop onto the roof of the house. He will then hack into their communications and force them to listen to Britney's new album again and again and again.

Soon the air was filled with screaming and I noticed a crowd of people coming towards me. They were rushing by me to get out the door. They jumped out windows and bashed themselves on the head with beer bottles in an attempt to block out all consciousness. I then heard the sound. The sound of a poppy teenage blonde screaming at the top of her lungs. You guessed it, it was Britney Spears. Her lyrics were horrible and her music was stolen from N-Sync. I was horrified at the sound and fled the building.

I was just in time to see The Padre and Odd Job holding on to a rope ladder attached to a Helicopter flown by Swoop. The helicopter was virtually silent and I believe it was one of those CIA Black Helicopters, but I'll never know for sure. They flew off into the night leaving the party destroyed and no clues to where they were headed or who financed their operation. I knew it had to be Thunt. He's the only one who could mastermind this kind of operation. I had to find him and stop him so I went to GUYS (with guns) and told them what I knew about Équipage De Thunt. They believed that I had helped with their operations and told me they were looking for me. That is why I had to go underground, to further my attempts to join Équipage De Thunt, and to destroy their evil leader. I will post more as soon as I can. Au revoir, LampshadeMan.


I attempted to contact the members of Équipage De Thunt. They claimed that they were playing Scrabble from 10:00 pm until 2:00 am. I didn't believe them, but they did have photographic evidence: (click here). Although I'm not sure if it's real. After what I saw, I'm not sure if anything is real anymore.

data.headline: Bin Laden's Hideous Plot
data.author: Thomas Hunt
data.story:
Inspired by John Humphreys
in the style of stratfor
1700 GMT, 011017

Summary

Due to the restrictions placed upon known terrorist Osama Bin Laden's bank accounts, his financial situation has become unsound. These restrictions, as well as the discovery and failure of his post-Sept. 11th stock market swindle has left him with no choice but to seek an alternative form of funding. Thus he has turned to the lucrative field of sports gambling.

Analysis

The New York Yankees, the so-called America's team was obviously not a good bet before the Sept. 11th tragedy, but afterwards, the political symbolism of America's Team cannot be overlooked.

Despite their natural skill and now "holy" quest, the Yankees began their post season by trailing in the ALDS series two games to zero and were in grave danger of being eliminated by the non-politically charged Oakland Athletics. It is now that we see Bin Laden's organization take action.

Using his cash resources, Laden was able to purchase one or several players on the Oakland team, and in a fashion befitting the classic 1919 White Sox team, the A's were persuaded to throw the series. The A's were an easy target for bribery as most of their players are rookies and thus incredibly underpaid. The Yankees, on the other hand would be incredibly difficult to bribe as their players are all incredibly overpaid. Due to Osama's work, the Yankees miraculously came from behind to defeat the A's in three straight games. America was overjoyed, but unfortunately Bin Laden had doubled and perhaps tripled his bets when the Yankees were trailing and profited greatly from the victory of America's Team.

No doubt Bin Laden and the Yankees will continue their disastrous and dangerous winning streak all the way to the World Series. It is at the World Series that the dual nature of Bin Laden's plan will become known. With his now restored cash flow, Laden will no doubt attempt to wash away the evidence of America's Team. If America and the Major League Baseball organization does not take steps to stop this tragedy, by overturning the ALDS series, both America and the Yankees will no doubt reap the whirlwind.

data.title: Boxing Helena
data.plot:
A crazed surgen dreams about "boxing helena", ie: 
Cutting off her arms and legs and keeping her locked 
up in his house.  Pretty sick.
data.opinion:
Creepy, but what else would you expect from David 
Lynch's daughter.  A fucked up story indeed, but I 
feel it would have played better as a comedy.  The 
first half seems like a porno while you keep waiting 
for the second half to turn into slapstick comedy.
data.creator: Jennifer Lynch

data.title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
data.plot:
Harry is back at Hogwarts for Year 2.  He and his 
friends fight another monster.  They win.
data.opinion:
Light.  Good story, good characters.  Not the greatest 
work of literature, but entertaining.
data.creator: J.K. Rowling

data.title: Cape Fear
data.plot:
Same plot as Cape Fear (1962).  But slightly 
different.  Man who is chased by evil convict is 
guilty.  He fixed the convict's case so he would 
loose, and was cheating on his wife on multiple 
occasions.  The small twists made to the story make it 
all the much better.
data.opinion:
Thrilling.  Scorssese takes on Hitchock, and makes a 
film worth of his name.  Hermman's score is back, 
beautifully enhanced by a DTS track (on the DVD 
version I watched).  DeNiro is stunning and creates a 
far more frightning Max Cady than the original.  Also 
there is some amazing excitement building camera work 
in the Raquetball scene, the scene where he beats up 
the girl and the closing the shades scene.  The wires 
tied to the bear is also incredibly suspenceful.  A 
remake that is superior to the original.
data.creator: Martin Scorssese

data.title: Winslow Boy, The
data.plot:
English.  A young boy is kicked out of school for a 
crime he didn't commit.  His family comes together and 
fights for his honor.
data.opinion:
Excellent.  Rebecca Pigeon is fantastic.  The movie is 
a little slow and the plot drags a bit but it is still 
packed with fantastic Mamet dialouge.
data.creator: David Mamet

data.title: Thin Blue Line, The
data.plot:
Documentary.  A man is falsely accused of murdering a 
police officer.
data.opinion:
Good, powerful, but too many re-enactments of the 
crime.  Seems too much like a drama than a documentary 
as well, but still worthwile because of its incredible 
power.
data.creator: Errol Morris

data.title: Spanish Prisoner, The
data.plot:
A man developing a process is caught in a complicated 
confidence scam.  People try to steal the process.  
Lots of twists and turns.
data.opinion:
Mamet-tastic.  Well written, well acted.  Major kudos 
go to Rebecca Pidgen (mrs. mamet), and Steven Martin 
(who is actually serious in this film).  Dialouge is 
fantastic.
data.creator: David Mamet

data.title: Dead Man
data.plot:
William Blake (Johnny Depp) travels to the wild west 
and looses his job.  He meets a girl and then gets in 
a gun fight with her boyfriend, kills him, the girl 
killed and he's on the run.  Travels with wierd indian 
and becomes killer.  Eventually dies.
data.opinion:
Slow.  Jarmush is too slow.  Too many fades to black.  
actors are good but movie drags like you won't believe.
data.creator: Jim Jarmush

data.title: Generation of Swine
data.plot:
Collection of articles from the 80s.  Mainly focusing 
on the 1988 presidential election, and the Iran Contra 
affair.
data.opinion:
Typical Hunter.  Gambling on candidates.  Madness, 
satelite tv, the end of Gary Heart, Ted Kenedy.  The 
attempted end of Reagan/Bush, but they were saved by 
an excellent performance by Oliver North.
data.creator: Hunter S. Thompson

data.title: Spy Game
data.plot:
Old CIA agent trained young CIA agent.  Young CIA 
agent ignores his teaching and falls for a girl and 
then puts his life on the line for her but he and she 
are saved by old CIA agent.
data.opinion:
Marred by a terrible flashback structure.  Plot 
in "present time" moving forward is interesting, but 
they keep flashing back to pointless and stupid 
flashbacks.  Also unbelievable because characters do 
not age.  In the age of super makeup and digital 
effects we should be able to do better.
data.creator: Tony Scott

data.title: Urban Legends 2: Final Cut
data.plot:
Film students making a film about urban legends get 
killed by their film proffessor who uses urban legends 
to kill them.
data.opinion:
Inferior to the original.  Too many long girl getting 
chased by a monster scenes.  Also had the stupid kid 
dies then we introduce his twin, who is played by the 
same actor, like a soap opera.  Also has a stupid film 
within a film, ending within an ending thing.

data.title: Luzhen Defence, The
data.plot:
A brilliant withdrawn chess player falls in love and 
has to choose between chess and life.  Sad ending:  he 
committs suicide.
data.opinion:
Excellent.  Gorris (Question of Silence, Antionia's 
Line) does a great job by bringing the game of chess 
into the direction of the film by shooting from above, 
nice reveals, etc.  John Turtoro is excellent in the 
reclusive lead.
data.creator: Marleen Gorris

data.title: Henry Fool
data.plot:
A garbage man writes an epic poem or is it 
pornography.  He is inspired to write by a wandering 
ex-child molester.
data.opinion:
Fantastic.  Nice pacing, good lighting, fantastic 
acting.  The character of fool is very interesting.  
Similar to Polonious.  Is he a fool or is he a 
genius.
data.creator: Hal Hartly

data.title: Cape Fear
data.plot:
A man released from jail seeks revenge.  Gregory Peck 
tries to protect his family from this madman (Mitchum).
data.opinion:
Cool.  Been paradied too much so it's tough to judge 
it, but it is still really cool.  Nice lighting, great 
music by Bernard Herman (psycho, taxi driver).

data.title: Bridget Jones: the edge of reason
data.plot:
Sequal to Bridget Jones' Diary.  Jones, now with a 
boyfriend, manages to screw that up, get locked up in 
Thailand, but gets free and gets back together with 
boyfriend.
data.opinion:
Light. Fun like original.  Wild story twists with 
Thailand, naked mexican houseboy, etc.  Character 
still likable though.  Decent for a sequal.
data.creator: Helen Fielding

data.title: Dave Berry's Complete Guide to Guys
data.plot:
Non fiction (Humor).  Berry writes about guys through 
the ages, from medievil times until modern times (ex: 
using a trebuchet to launch a dead horse, or using a 
trebuchet to launch a buick).
data.opinion:
Funny and brief.  Two good qualities.  I read it in a 
day thinking that I had read it before.  I probably 
had, but it was still damn funny.  Hilights: Launching 
Trebuchets, Corvair enthusiasts blowing up vacume 
cleaners, and much much more.  PS: Don't forget to 
fart.
data.creator: Dave berry

data.title: Zen and the art of screenwriting
data.plot:
Non fiction.  Interviews with screen writers and 
advice from former UCLA proffessor William Froug.
data.opinion:
Interesting.  Some famous screenwriters, some not so 
famous, facinating stories and insights none the 
less.  Suffers slightly from it's horrible title.
data.creator: William Froug

data.title: Smith of Wooton Major & Farmer Giles of Ham
data.plot:
Two novella length stories.  Wooton Major is about 
fairies and cooking.  While Farmer Giles is about 
dragons and giants.
data.opinion:
Interesting fantasies.  Legendary.  Similar to 
Beowulf, Sir Gwain, classic english literature style.  
Light, joyful, shiney.
data.creator: J.R.R. Tolkien

data.title: Glengarry Glen Ross
data.plot:
The troubles of salesmen are explored in this awesome 
play.
data.opinion:
(not a book, a play)  Great play.  Well written, very 
fast, very mamet.  Shorter and simplier than the movie 
version.  Four acts, no "coffee is for closers" scene 
and simpler in structure.
data.creator: David Mamet

data.title: Adventures in a TV Nation
data.plot:
Non fiction.  The behind the scenes story of Michael 
Moore's television show TV Nation.  Details several of 
their shows.
data.opinion:
Funny, but sad because the show was not renewed.  
Typical Moore humor, going places and getting kicked 
out.  Highlights include "sending a KGB spy to spy on 
Unsolved Mysteries", "mike trying to find the missile 
that pointed at Flint, MN", "Haulin' communism" 
and "Junk Mail", where they sent letters requesting 
money for Serial Killer Jeffery Dahmer, and recieved 
money.
data.creator: Michael Moore

data.title: Downsize This!
data.plot:
Non-fiction/humor.  Collection of chapters ("essays") 
on various political issues.
data.opinion:
Good.  Moore is funny and intelligent, although having 
watched all of his movies and most of his TV shows, 
the book does seem a bit repetative, but this is 
simply because I've already seen him talk about most 
of the issues he raises in this book.
data.creator: Michael Moore

data.title: Generation X
data.plot:
Three disafected youths escape their lives to the 
desert to tell stories and try to figure life out.
data.opinion:
Good.  Good stories, good definations in the margins, 
semi-disposable sweedish furnature, mid-twenties 
breakdown, jampenes minimalism.  A fun read, though 
provoking, well written.
data.creator: Douglas Coupland

data.title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
data.plot:
A young boy discovers that he is the son of a great 
wizard (and witch) and joins a wizard school. Similar 
to Star Wars, Hitchhiker's Guide, and of course The 
Lord of the Rings.
data.opinion:
Decent.  I read it in a day, so it was interesting and 
cute.  Predictable, like a bond film, when they 
describe something in the begining it shows up a few 
pages later.  Basic writing, but fun to read.
data.creator: J.K. Rowling

data.title: Microserfs
data.plot:
Nerds at microsoft join a startup and regain their 
lives.  Sad ending, unexpected and unneccessary.
data.opinion:
Stunning.  Like my life.  Similarities abound, from 
worship of simpsons, to Costco addiction (where one 
buys 10,000 straws, planning never to need to buy 
straws ever again).  A laugh on every page, characters 
that leap out at you because you know people just like 
them in real life.
data.creator: Douglas Coupland

data.title: Lord of the Rings, The
data.plot:
Mystical.  A hobbit must travel far to destroy an evil 
ring.  Many twists and turns.
data.opinion:
Slow but good. Fellowship drags due to too much travel, approx 300 pages of wandering. Second half picks up well by adding more characters and telling their stories in alternatating chapters, a tool Tolkien uses well to make the other books more interesting.

The wonder and the beauty of the book is the world created by Tolkien. He mentions minor things like legends (which you know are not even written down) but you buy that they exist in the world he's created. It's size is incredible and the travel in the story helps to highlight and give the reader a good tour of that world.
data.creator: J.R.R. Tolkien

data.title: Regulators, The
data.plot:
An evil spirit enchants a kid, then uses the child\\'s 
heroes (cartoon characters and cowboys) to wreck havoc 
in a suburban setting.
data.opinion:
Not that great.  Filled with cop outs, points where 
King describes something as like a pop culture object 
instead of actually describing it.  The snow was 
falling like the snow in the movie It\\'s a wonderful 
life.  Pretty lame.  Story is interesting from a 
surbanan invasion point of view but poor from another 
child enchanted with demon point of view.
data.creator: Richard Bachman

data.title: Music for Chameleons
data.plot:
Multiple plots.  Collection of short stories, self 
interviews and jouranlistic fiction.
data.opinion:
A good book.  Highlights: Mr. Jones, a story about a 
mysterious man, never quite explained.  Other 
highlighs include a self interview with Capote in the 
end of the book and a description of a day spent with 
Marylin Monroe.
data.creator: Truman Capote

data.title: Spike, Mike, Slackers and Dikes
data.plot:
Non-Fiction.  An overview of American Independant film 
from the 80s to the mid ninties.
data.opinion:
Good book.  Historical, informatative and funny. Good 
info about Spike Lee, Michael Moore, Kevin Smith and 
more.  Insight into how an independant film goes from 
a film to show your family into a film that the world 
goes ape shit over.
data.creator: John Pierson

data.title: The Odd Couple
data.plot:
Two people who shouldn\\'t live together do.  They 
fight, they fight and it\\'s hilarious.
data.opinion:
Great performances coupled with great writing.  
Performances by Lennon and Mathau.  Writing by Neil 
Simon.  Funny, but doesn\\'t loose that \\"play\\" feeling.

data.title: Finding Forester
data.plot:
Good Will Hunting on racial lines.  Sean Connery 
pretends to be J.D. Saldinger and teaches a young 
black man to write.  Uplifting.
data.opinion:
I love J.D. Saldinger who this film was nominally 
based on.  I liked it for that reason.  Otherwise it\\'s 
pretty much like Good Will Hunting, even had the same 
director.
data.creator: Gus Van Sant

data.title: Paths of Glory
data.plot:
A general is blamed for not forcing his men to 
committe suicide on the battlefield.  Courtroom drama, 
three men are held accountable and tried of 
cowardess.  They die.
data.opinion:
Well directed.  Plot is a little less than desirable.  
Good ending.  Great performance by Kurt Douglass.  A 
Kubrick film before he gained total control, but still 
a Kubrick film.
data.creator: Stanley Kubrick

data.title: Hope and Glory
data.plot:
A look at the homelife during the Blitz of WWII.  
Highlight: The gang of little boys bribes a girl to 
show them her privates, hilarious.
data.opinion:
Interesting look at the children of WWII.  Nice to see 
the war from a different angle.  Also nice to see an 
acurate portryal of what children would do in the 
middle of a bombed out city.

data.title: Muriel\\'s Wedding
data.plot: Wierd british woman wants to get married.
data.opinion:
Wierd british comedy.  Lots and lots of Abba Music.  
Funny in parts, british in other parts.  Quirky.

data.title: Simon Birch
data.plot:
A freakishly small boy helps foster belief in God for 
a young boy.
data.opinion:
Sad, heartwarming and well played.  Based on the book 
A prayer for Owen Meany by John Irving.  The movie has 
some good twists and turns, some bad acting by Ashley 
Judd and some great acting from the little actor 
playing the title role.

data.title: Pretty in Pink
data.plot:
Cinderella.  Teenage dating.  Redhead wants rich boy, 
but she should date Ducky, the charming nerd character.
data.opinion:
Ehh.  Not that great, but a typical 80s teen comedy.  
More romantic than funny, not my favorite John Hughes 
film.
data.creator: John Hughes

data.title: Magnificant Seven, The
data.plot: Seven cowboys protect a small village.
data.opinion:
Bad remake of Kurosawa\\'s classic The Seven Samurai.  
Steve McQueen rules as always, but the original is far 
superior.  The film also drags greatly.

data.title: Purple Rose of Cairo, The
data.plot:
A character from a movie jumps off the screen and 
falls in love with a real woman.
data.opinion: Whimsical.  Delightful.  Funny and heartwarming.
data.creator: Woody Allen

data.title: Hurricane, The
data.plot:
Here is the story of the hurricane.
A man the authorities came to blame.
For something he never done.
Put in a prison cell, but one time he could have been, 
the champion of the world.
data.opinion:
Fantastic retelling of a major error of American 
justice.  A great performance by Denzel Washington.

data.title: McCabe & Mrs. Miller
data.plot: A wild west entrepuner falls in love with a Madam.
data.opinion:
Slow.  The film is good, the plot is interesting and 
the performances are nice but the film moves 
incredibly slowly.  A nice ending though.

data.title: Evolution
data.plot: A gang of idiots fight aliens.
data.opinion:
A horrible rip off of Ghostbusters.  Cut to quickly, 
full of stupid fart jokes and other catchlines.  It 
makes me not want to make movies.

data.title: Nashville
data.plot:
Several characters explore the music scene of 
Nashville, TN.
data.opinion:
Muddled.  Too many characters and not enough plot.  
Altman seems a bit out of control and the movie drags 
and never seems to go anywhere.
data.creator: Robert Altman

data.title: Unbreakable
data.plot:
Issue #0 for a comic book series.  Genesis.  A man who 
is incredibly strong discovers his powers and his 
enemy.
data.opinion:
Slow.  The pacing has one speed, slow as fucking 
hell.  The trailer ruined the whole first half for me 
and the second half links up to the ending which is 
perfect, right up until they bring up this text scroll 
designed for stupid people.  The text gives a 
UNNECCESSARY contuation of the story (like it was 
based on a true story).  The text at the end pretty 
much destroys any enjoyment I had of the picture. But 
I\\'ll still rate it good, because that\\'s what it was up 
till that point.
data.creator: M. Night Shamlahan

data.title: Coyote Ugly
data.plot:
A hot girl gets a job in a bar.  She records a song 
and sings it at the bar to become a major songwriter.  
Horatio Alger exploitive.
data.opinion:
Lousy.  But marketable.  Tons of hot chicks, random 
slow motion shots and lots of music.

data.title: The shop around the corner
data.plot:
A young man (Stewart) falls in love with his co-
worker.  Ups and downs, he gets fired, he gets re-
hired.  Heartwarming.
data.opinion:
Good film.  Classic Stewart.  You\\'ve got Mail was 
based on this picture.  Plot is simplier than Mail, 
but film is much more heartwarming.

data.title: Truman
data.plot: Details the life of President Harry S. Truman
data.opinion:
Great \\"movie history\\".  Shows Truman as a hard worker, 
a bad businessman, and a compassionate President.  
Sinese does a great job in the title role.  Makes you 
feel kindof bad to think that the Presidental 
Retirement program was instituted to keep Truman off 
the dole after his business failed again.

data.title: Crimes and Misdemenors
data.plot:
A man (Landau) has his mistress murdered and feels 
bad.  Another man (Allen) looses the girl he loves to 
a pompous ass (Alda).  Highlight:  Allen makes a film 
within a film where he compairs Alda\\'s character to 
Mussolini and Mr. Ed.
data.opinion:
Funny.  Ups and downs, neurotic.  One of Allen\\'s 
better funny ones.  Laundau gives a fantastic 
performance.
data.creator: Woody Allen

data.title: Shadows and Fog
data.plot:
A man is chased by death througout the city of New 
York. (based on the play: Death)
data.opinion:
Dark, but beautiful.  Slow moving yet occasionally 
funny.  Dramatic yet mystical.  A good film, but not 
one of Allen\\'s better works.
data.creator: Woody Allen

data.title: Dear God
data.plot:
Mailmen start answering mail sent to God and doing 
good deeds.
data.opinion:
Cheesy, boring and unfunny.  Not a bad plot.  Horrible 
courtroom ending though.

data.title: Little Shop of Horrors
data.plot: An evil man eating plant goes wild in this MUSICAL.
data.opinion:
I hate musicals.  They just keep singing and singing 
and singing.  Although the Sadistic Dentist song 
starring Steve Martin makes the film almost worth 
watching.

data.title: Requim for a Dream
data.plot:
Several characters discover drugs, and loose too much 
to be described.  Highlight:  The split screen used 
perfectly to show the seperation of two characters 
when they\\'re incredibly close.
data.opinion:
Stunning.  Frightning.  Horrifying.  The best anti-
drug film ever.  I saw this film once and I\\'m not sure 
I ever want to see it again.  A genius at work.

data.title: I shot Andy Warhol
data.plot:
A look inside the life of the woman who wrote the 
S.C.U.M. Manifesto and shot Andy Warhol (hence the 
catchy title).
data.opinion:
Good picture.  Warhol is played well, but not better 
than Cripsin Glover\\'s performance in The Doors.  The 
woman who plays Valerie does a great job and is truly 
terrorifying.

data.title: I know what you did last summer
data.plot:
Stupid teenagers run over a stranger with their car 
and are \\"haunted\\" by him, one year later.
data.opinion:
Slasher horror pic.  Love-Hewitt is pretty but can\\'t 
act to save her life (which seems to be what most of 
the picture is about).  Some nice gore, a good chase 
or two, but not really quality.

data.title: Marty
data.plot:
A guy who can\\'t seem to get a date, but is such a nice 
guy.  He takes care of his family and he always makes 
sacrifices.  But in the end, he gets a date, and she 
likes him and all is well.  Uplifting.
data.opinion:
Ernest Bourgnigine excels in the title role.  The film 
centers around him and with him in the lead it can do 
no wrong.

data.title: The day of the jackel
data.plot:
The President of France is to be assassinated by The 
Jackel.  Dramatic game of cat and mouse.  Unfortuately 
the cop succeeds and the Jackel fails, but it\\'s 
exciting.
data.opinion: Dramatic cat and mouse chase.  Exciting.  Worth seeing.

data.title: The Hustler
data.plot:
A pool shark gets on a bad streak, falls in love, gets 
on a hot streak and his girl kills herself.
data.opinion:
Great picture.  Newman and Scott explode in their 
performances.  Drags in parts, but overall worth 
watching.

data.title: Outbreak
data.plot:
A bad monkey spreads a bad virus.  The world\\'s only 
hope is a rouge scientist named Rainman.
data.opinion:
Good movie.  All too real with the recent (2001) 
antrhax scares.  I still can\\'t get over Hoffman being 
anything but Rainman and I have a hard time believing 
him as a world saving doctor.  The secret government 
conspiracy plot line (starring Sutherland and Freeman) 
is a bit hard to swallow though.  And the ending is a 
bit mellodramatic.

data.title: Bait
data.plot: A black ex-con is used as bait for a \\"genius\\" thief.
data.opinion:
The only good thing about this movie is Jaimee Fox, 
he\\'s kinda funny and I\\'m glad he got the part in Any 
Given Sunday (a far better movie btw).  The film 
drags, fails to decide between drama and comedy and 
also fails to supply a decent ammount of action.

data.title: Sugar and Spice
data.plot: Idiotic cheerleaders decide to rob a bank.
data.opinion:
Tries to be poignant, tries to be funny, tries to be a 
good movie but is a total pile of shit.  Even hot 
chicks in cheerleader costumes fail to raise the mark 
on this pile of shit.  The narrative style is full of 
annoying voiceover, too.

data.title: Saving Silverman
data.plot:
An idiot is getting married to a woman who is bad for 
him.  His friends try to save him by kidnapping his 
fiance.
data.opinion:
Funny but not hilarious.  It does have Jack Black 
though, who is very funny.  Far too much Neil Diamond 
though.

data.title: Girl Interupted
data.plot:
A girl pretends to be a boy.  Later killed by 
rednecks.  Based on a true story.
data.opinion:
Good film.  The kind that you should see but you\\'ll 
never really find enjoyable.  Hillary Swank is awesome 
as the woman/man, if you\\'re into that sort of thing.

data.title: Save the last dance
data.plot:
A white girl in the ghetto falls in love with a black 
guy.  She also learns how to hip hop dance and uses 
her new found ghetto-sense to win a ballet scholarship.
data.opinion: Lousy, boring and predictable.  Stiles is hot though.

data.title: The Family Man
data.plot:
Have you seen It\\'s a Wonderful Life?  It\\'s pretty much 
the same thing.
data.opinion:
Ehhh.  Not bad, but not good either.  Cage is not 
really likable (when compaired to Jimmy Stewart).  
Leone is hot.

data.title: Proof of Life
data.plot:
A man (Dennis Quad) is kidnapped.  A proffessional 
negoatior is sent to negoiate for his release.  He 
falls in love with the man\\'s wife (Meg Ryan).
data.opinion:
Interesting look at negoiation.  Russell Crowe is 
great.  I\\'ll be he was also great fucking Meg Ryan, 
his married co-star of the picture. hehehe.

data.title: The Sun also Rises
data.plot:
A crew of misfits visit Pampaloma for some 
bullfighting action.
data.opinion: Beautiful.
data.creator: Ernest Hemingway

data.title: Junky
data.plot: A man develops a habit.  A habit for heroin.
data.opinion:
Brilliant.  The truth about what it\\'s like to be a 
junky from a junky.  Burroughs pours his heart out on 
the pages and pages glow, as they always do when it\\'s 
the truth.
data.creator: William S. Burroughs

data.title: Queer
data.plot:
While taking some time off from heroin, Bill Lee 
(burroughs) discovers his sex drive and has a serious 
of not so great affairs with men in Mexico.
data.opinion:
This book is true.  I would prefer if Burroughs had 
managed to use his real name as he did in Junky, but I 
admire him greatly for having the courage what is so 
obviously his deeply personal longings in a book for 
all to see.
data.creator: William S. Burroughs

data.title: Timequake
data.plot:
A mix between fiction and non fiction.  A timequake 
occurs, making the world repeat, but then the 
timequake ends and the world awakes.  Kilgore Trout is 
needed to make them wake up.
data.opinion:
Vonnegut\\'s self proclaimed last novel.  A bit 
depressing as he mixes in personal details and 
delivers a well written goodbye.  So it goes.
data.creator: Kurt Vonnegut

data.title: Another Roadside Attraction
data.plot:
The mummfied body of Christ is found and set up in a 
roadside circus.  So much more than just another 
roadside attraction.
data.opinion:
More madness from Robbins.  Great characters, great 
plot twists, worth reading.
data.creator: Tom Robbins

data.title: Riding the Rap
data.plot: A cop tracks down a killer or something.
data.opinion:
More brain candy from Elmore Leonard.  I read it on an 
airplane, as I always read Elmore Leonard books on 
airplanes.  Good characters, lots of crime, starts up 
your appetite but doesn\\'t bring it back down.
data.creator: Elmore Leonard

data.title: Faust
data.plot:
A man sells his soul to the devil.  Obviously a 
mistake.
data.opinion:
It\\'s important to read the classics.  I had a far 
simplier idea of the Faust story before I read this 
and I exited knowing that the real story is very 
complex and very interesting.
data.creator: Goethe

data.title: Carrie
data.plot:
A young girl is made fun of, until she develops 
supernatural powers and kicks everyones fucking ass.
data.opinion:
An explosion of power for a young writer (King\\'s first 
published work).  Nice narrative structure of reveals 
similar to Capote\\'s In Cold Blood.  The mix of mock 
newspaper and magazine articles also gives the book 
a \\"non fiction\\" feeling.  Worth reading.
data.creator: Stephen King

data.title: Even Cowgirls get the Blues
data.plot:
The story of a female hitchhiker.  A woman with 
extraordinary thumbs.  The tale of a cowgirl and her 
ranch.  Attempting to describe the plot of a Tom 
Robbins book is like trying to explain the 
constitution to a crack addict while balancing an 
elephant on top of your left index finger.
data.opinion:
Wild and crazy.  Plot turns, twists, and a cameo by 
the author.  All the madness you\\'ve come to expect 
from Robbins and a few references to Jack Kerouac, not 
bad, not bad at all.
data.creator: Tom Robbins

data.title: On Writing
data.plot:
Non Fiction.  King discusses how he writes, why he 
writes and touches on the time he got run over by a 
car.
data.opinion:
Good read, very informatative, but King\\'s ability to 
constantly turn out novels is a bit depressing to us 
normal people.
data.creator: Stephen King

data.title: Survivor : A Novel
data.plot:
A refugee from a religious cult starts a suicide 
promotion hotline.  Twists and turns, then a SUPRISE 
ENDING.
data.opinion: Good book, fun to read, but typical Palahniuk.
data.creator: Chuck Palahniuk

data.title: Invisible Monsters
data.plot:
A fashion model goes a bit whacked.  Interesting plot 
twists and a TYPICAL PALAHNIUK suprise ending.
data.opinion:
A good book, nice language, but Palaniuk fails on the 
ending.
data.creator: Chuck Palahniuk

data.title: Fight Club
data.plot:
A man who can\\'t sleep becomes addicted to self help 
groups, then starts one of his own: Fight Club.  You 
know the plot, you watched the movie.
data.opinion:
Great book.  Awesome counter-yuppie culture shock type 
language.  Attacks the core values of our Starbucks 
dominated society.  Quick style, agressive language, 
good read.
data.creator: Chuck Palahniuk

data.title: Bright Lights, Big City
data.plot:
An un-named character (you) journeys through 
depression in New York City.  Through sex and 
joblessness, love and despair.  Similar to The Catcher 
in the Rye.  Side note:  Great supporting character 
named Tad Allagash (the Allagasher), notoriously late, 
cares for no one, constantly the life of the party, 
worth reading for him alone.
data.opinion:
Great book, similar but inferior to The Catcher in the 
Rye.  I liked it a lot though.  I think I\\'ll re-read 
it sometime just to catch up on the adventures of a 
side character named Tad Allagash (the Allagasher).
data.creator: Jay McInerney

data.title: Easy Riders, Raging Bulls: How the sex-drugs-and-rock\\'n\\'roll generation saved hollywood
data.plot:
Non Fiction.  An excellent account of Hollywood\\'s 
rising directors of the 1970s and how they failed.
data.opinion:
Good book. Fun facts about your favorite directors.  A 
crazy book about a crazy time when Hollywood made 
great movies and ground breaking directors did drugs 
and made movies.
data.creator: Peter Biskind

data.title: Secrets and Lies: Digital Security in a Networked World
data.plot:
Non Fiction.  Schneider details the ins and outs of 
computer security.  Highlights: The Chapter on Users 
is hilarious, and the opening in which Schneider 
admits that total security is impossible due to the 
stupidity of users.
data.opinion:
Great book.  Nice overview of security.  Not too 
technical, but still informatative.
data.creator: Bruce Schneider

data.title: Basketball Diaries, The
data.plot:
A story about the streets of New York.  Mainly 
features a young man\\'s journey through basketball, 
drugs and sex.
data.opinion:
Excellent book.  Beautiful but simple language.  
Similar to The Catcher in the Rye.
data.creator: Jim Carroll

data.title: Driven
data.plot:
A young driver needs help from an old driver to be all 
he can be.
data.opinion:
Pure shit. Made for people with ADD. Storylines are 
dropped and picked up in naration. The movie runs and 
runs, occasionally stopping for a shitty speech by 
Burt Renolds. Ripped off from Days of Thunder, Top Gun 
and many others, this Stallone written and starring 
pic, sucks dick.
data.creator: Renny Harlin

data.title: Party Girl
data.plot:
A young Party Girl grows up and gets a job without 
loosing her party roots.
data.opinion:
Parker Posey is stunning. Beautiful and a good actor. 
This indy flick isn't as good as The Party with Peter 
Sellers but it's better than Valley Girl with Nick 
Cage. The lines are great, the plot is sketchy and the 
story drags, but Posey is awesome and makes the film 
fun and worth seeing.
data.creator: Daisy von Scherler Mayer

data.title: Dial 'M' for Murder
data.plot:
A man wants his wife dead. He blackmailes another man 
to do it, but everything goes wrong.
data.opinion:
Starring the beautiful Grace Kelly, this is not one of 
Hitchcock's best, but it's pretty great. Suspenceful 
and interesting, I rooted for the bad guy, but 
regreted his vendetta against the stunning Grace 
Kelly. But she looks better in Rear Window.
data.creator: Alfred Hitchcock

data.title: Bounce
data.plot:
A guy dies on a plane crash with someone else's 
ticket. Someone else feels bad about it and goes and 
fucks, falls in love with that dead guy's wife.
data.opinion:
Crappy. Affleck, boring. Paltrow, ugly brown hair. Un- 
interesting. Best part: The begining when Affleck, a 
player, skips his plane to have a lay-over with that 
hot chick from Con Air. It goes downhill from there.
data.creator: Don Roos

data.title: Little Nicky
data.plot:
The devil's son returns to earth to stop the other 
devil's sons from causing the devil to die. And he 
falls in love.
data.opinion:
Pure shit. This movie is not funny and all of the 
characters voices and actions piss me off. Jokes fail 
throughout. A guy with boobs on his head. His mother 
is an angel (the beautiful Reece Witherspoon), HA! 
Filled with stars, Keitel, Tarantino, Carvey, etc, but 
filled with shit. This movie was worse than Driven, 
but better than How Stella got her Groove back.
data.creator: Steven Brill

data.title: Shrek
data.plot: An ogre rescues a princess and they fall in love.
data.opinion:
Funny at parts. Fart jokes at others. Animation is 
breath- taking but seems to distract from story. 
Voices are good. Jokes at the expense of Fairy tale 
characters are better. Possible negative stereotype by 
Eddie Murphy playing the donkey, speaking in black 
tone, or perhaps just Eddie Murphy speak.
data.creator: Andrew Adamson

data.title: Story of Us, The
data.plot:
A married couple on the rocks. Will they split or not. 
Do they have a history? Are they an us? Who gives a 
fuck?
data.opinion:
Lousy movie by Reiner. Tries to be Woody Allen and 
fails. Willis is ok, Pfeiffer is crappy, she looks 
like a poltergiest with tits. You never really care 
about the characters and I don't believe the ending. 
When people are sick of eachother they split. And 
that's that.
data.creator: Rob Reiner

data.title: AntiTrust
data.plot:
A Bill Gates character is triumphing in the software 
market by stealing code from kids in garages and 
occasionally killing them. But it all goes wrong when 
a young silly programmer double crosses him.
data.opinion:
Pure shit. Bad plot. Bad technical stuff. Laughable. 
Attempts to mimic hitchcock at points and fails. 
Attempts to be suspenceful and fails. Attempts to be 
funny and fails. Do not see, even computer geeks who 
like to laugh at geek films won't like this one.
data.creator: Peter Howitt (II)

data.title: House of Yes, The
data.plot:
Parker Posey plays an insane woman who has a serious 
Jackie-O obsession. Her brother returns home with a 
finace and leaves in pine box.
data.opinion:
Funny at times, but mostly disturbing. I like Posey 
and as usual she's great. It's well written, but it 
was a play and feels very much like a play, rapid fire 
dialogue. Worth watching, but not worth running out 
for.
data.creator: Mark S. Waters

data.title: Speechless
data.plot:
Based on James Carville and Mary Madson, two speech 
writers, one republican, one democrat, fight, fuck and 
fall in love.
data.opinion:
I like this movie. Starring Michael Keaton (Batman, 
cool) and Geena Davis (hot) it's fun to watch over and 
over again. I like how the speeches they write attack 
each other and how they fight over the spin.
data.creator: Ron Underwood

data.title: Absence of Malice
data.plot:
Paul Newman plays a son of a mobster who is being 
invesigated in the disaperance of another mobster. The 
kicker is he's only being investigated because the 
feds are trying to get him to help them find the 
killer. They're using Sally Field, a newspaper 
reporter to publish the info
data.opinion:
Mildly interesting. Newman sets up the feds pretty good and has sex with Gigdet (Sally Field), also he shows anger and slaps her around a bit following the suicide of his friend that she caused.

I watched it because I borrowed a bunch of videos from my Mother.
data.creator: Syndey Pollack

data.title: Legend of Bagger Vance, The
data.plot:
Matt Damon, a golfer returns from WWI with a broken 
spirit. Will Smith (Bagger Vance), a black caddy, 
Jesus/God type figure appears and helps Damon regain 
his game. Because after all, golf is life?
data.opinion:
Typical Redford. Epic, lots of nature shots. Theron's accent is sketchy, and she doesn't get naked. Damon is adequate, but John complained constantly of his lousy golf swing. It's strange to see a period piece during prohibition where they drink so much. Bad history, and typical.

Film goes on forever. Drags on, I should say. Lousy attempt to recapure the glory of The Natural.
data.creator: Robert Redford

data.title: Ghost in the Shell
data.plot:
Humans, cyborgs, the future. Plot deals with AI & 
Consciousness. Do the cyborgs have souls? Follows the 
trail of a few cyborg cops from Section 9. Film is 
Japanese Animation.
data.opinion:
A good film. Lots of good chase scenes, fighting and 
typically with Japanese animation: large breasted 
female nudity, which is good. The film is an obvious 
precursor and inspiration for the matrix.
data.creator: Mamoru Oshii

data.title: Object of My Affection, The
data.plot:
Chasing Amy, reversed. Aniston falls in love with a 
gay guy. Mildly interesting plot twist, she's 
pregnant, they're going to raise the baby together...
data.opinion:
Bleck. Imagine a guy who doesn't want to sleep with 
Jennifer Aniston. Acting, bleck. Story, bleck. Goes on 
forever, about 4 endings tacked on the thing. Alan 
Alda great however, as usual, but nothing spectacular.
data.creator: Nicholas Hytner
